entries.text: Verification of Identity and Employment Information Name (First, Middle, Last) Date of Birth: Existing Clients (Not for Broker’s Use) ⦁ Client is known to ATB Mortgage Specialist and is an existing ATB Client (Verification of Identification and Employment Information not required) Existing Business Partner # New Clients or Clients Not Known to Mortgage Specialist (Broker’s Use) Verification of Identity One piece of government issued photo ID is required - refer to Appendix A for a list of acceptable ID. Team members can request a second piece of ID if necessary. Note: A SIN is required for any interest-bearing account types. Piece 1 Piece 2 (Optional) ID Type ID Type ID Number ID Number Place of Issue Place of Issue Expiry Date Expiry Date Does the name on Piece 1 match the name on Piece 2? ⦁ N/A ⦁ Yes ⦁ No, Explain: Supporting documentation examined: Employment Information Employment Status ⦁ Employed ⦁ Self-Employed ⦁ Freelance ⦁ Student ⦁ Retired ⦁ Unemployed Employer Name: Position: 1 Employed From : Employed Until: Occupation ⦁ Clerical Worker ⦁ Farm Worker ⦁ Homemaker ⦁ Manager/Supervisor ⦁ Not Disclosed ⦁ Other ⦁ Professional ⦁ Retired ⦁ Sale of Service ⦁ Self-Employed (Other) ⦁ Self-Employed (Farm/Ranch) ⦁ Senior Executive ⦁ Skilled Blue Collar/Trade ⦁ Skilled Technical White Collar ⦁ Student ⦁ Unemployed ⦁ Unskilled Worker Industry Sector ⦁ Banking/Finance ⦁ Construction ⦁ Education ⦁ Farming/Natural Resources ⦁ Government ⦁ Health ⦁ Hi-Tech ⦁ Leisure/Entertainment ⦁ Manufacturing ⦁ Other ⦁ Retail Sales ⦁ Services ⦁ Transport Subsector: Employer’s Physical Address Address: City/Town: Province: Postal Code: Work Phone #: I acknowledge that the above applicant is known to me either as a previously known existing client to ATB or because I have personally met with the applicant and examined original and valid copies of 2 pieces of acceptable ID as well as captured employment details. ATB Team Member Signature Name Date Entered into BP by: ATB Team Member Signature Name Date Appendix A – Acceptable Identification Personal identification documents (ID) must be original and valid. One piece of acceptable government issued photo ID will be required to verify an individual’s identity and are subject to the following limitations: ⦁ ID must come from list A which must be government issued photo ID documents from federal, provincial or territorial authorities; no exceptions permitted to this requirement. ⦁ ID must have a photograph of the individual; Exception for seniors unable to provide 2 picture ID due to physical, health or mobility limitations may provide a second piece of ID from List A or B. List A ⦁ Driver's license issued in Canada as permitted for identification purposes under provincial law ⦁ Valid Canadian passport (all passport types except Emergency and temporary are accepted). ⦁ Firearms Possession Acquisition License (PAL). ⦁ Canadian National Department of Defense Identification Card. ⦁ Nexus Card issued by the Government of Canada (PASS ID # on back of Nexus Card). ⦁ Secure Certificate of Indian Status Card issued by the Government of Canada. Refer below for Additional Information. ⦁ Certificate of Canadian Citizenship or Certificate of Naturalization which includes a photograph of individual, but not a commemorative issue letter-sized paper citizenship certificate. ⦁ Note: team members must not accept Certificates of Canadian Citizenship or Certificates of Naturalization issued after January 2012. ⦁ Refer to the Additional Information section below for more information. ⦁ Permanent Resident Card. ⦁ Citizenship and Immigration Canada Form IMM 1442 – Study Permit, Work Permit, Visitor Record or Temporary Resident Permit bearing the individual’s photograph. ⦁ Document or card bearing the individual's photograph and signature, issued by any of the following authorities or their successors: ⦁ Insurance Corporation of British Columbia ⦁ Alberta Registries ⦁ Saskatchewan Government Insurance ⦁ Department of Service Nova Scotia and Municipal Relations ⦁ Department of Transportation and Public Works of the Province of Prince Edward Island ⦁ Service New Brunswick ⦁ Department of Government Services and Lands of the Province of Newfoundland and Labrador ⦁ Department of Transportation of the Northwest Territories ⦁ Department of Community Government and Transportation of the Territory of Nunavut ⦁ Foreign passport is acceptable if it is equivalent to an acceptable type of Canadian ID, such as foreign passport or driver’s license. List B (Optional) ⦁ Canadian Birth Certificate (only acceptable if issued by Provincial or Federal Government Department, certificates issued by a church are not acceptable). 3 ⦁ Social Insurance Number (SIN) card issued by the Government of Canada*. ⦁ Expired temporary SIN card is not acceptable as identification. ⦁ Government of Canada is issuing SIN cards in paper format (Confirmation of SIN letter). ⦁ SIN Identification (Confirmation of SIN letter) is not acceptable as identification. ⦁ Plastic SIN cards are still acceptable, if they are not expired. ⦁ Provincial or Territorial health insurance card as permitted for identification purposes under provincial law. Valid in Quebec only if volunteered by the client. Not valid for Nova Scotia, Ontario, Manitoba and Prince Edward Island. ⦁ Old Age Security Card issued by the Government of Canada and embossed with the individual's name and SIN number. ⦁ Employee identification card bearing the individual’s name and photograph, employer’s name and issued by an employer well known in the community. ⦁ Client Card or bank card issued by a member of Payments Canada, bearing the individual’s name and signature. ⦁ Credit card issued by a member of Payments Canada, bearing the individual’s name and signature. ⦁ Canadian Institute for the Blind (CNIB) Client Card bearing the individual’s photograph and signature. ⦁ Canadian university or college student identification card bearing name, photograph, student reference number and barcode. Include the expiry date if applicable. *Note: A SIN is required for any interest-bearing account types. 4

If the funds are still in the donor's possession, provide a bank statement of the donor's account or written confirmation completed by the donor's financial institution that the funds are available for the gift. If the funds are at the solicitor/notary, provide confirmation from the solicitor/notary that the funds have been received. entries.name: 603-08-101E---Homeowners-Kit---Nov-2023.pdf
entries.summary: Conventional Broker Mortgages The Homeowner’s Kit is a smart and flexible financing solution for all your clients’ borrowing needs, through all their life stages. The Homeowner’s Kit brings together two credit products — a mortgage and a re-advanceable Home Equity Line of Credit (HELOC) — into one, easy-to-use lending solution.1 1. Why a line of credit? With the B2B Bank Home Equity Line of Credit, you have the added convenience of: • Interest-only or fixed dollar amount pre-authorized payments • View your account balance, perform inter-institution fund transfers (IIFT) and pay bills via B2B Bank Online Banking on onlinebanking.b2bbank.com. AREAS OF APPLICATION CREDIT GUIDELINES3 VALUE-ADDED
entries.text: Conventional Broker Mortgages The Homeowner’s Kit is a smart and flexible financing solution for all your clients’ borrowing needs, through all their life stages. The Homeowner’s Kit brings together two credit products — a mortgage and a re-advanceable Home Equity Line of Credit (HELOC) — into one, easy-to-use lending solution.1 1. Why a line of credit? With the B2B Bank Home Equity Line of Credit, you have the added convenience of: • Interest-only or fixed dollar amount pre-authorized payments • View your account balance, perform inter-institution fund transfers (IIFT) and pay bills via B2B Bank Online Banking on onlinebanking.b2bbank.com. AREAS OF APPLICATION CREDIT GUIDELINES3 VALUE-ADDED FEATURES, OPTIONS AND ALTERNATIVES • Purchase • Refinance Exclusions • Rental • Vacation/Secondary home • Purchase Plus Improvements • Max. LTV: Max. LTV: 80%4 (maximum 65% for HELOC portion) • > 660 GDS/TDS: 39%/44% • HELOC: Borrower must qualify using greater of mortgage line of credit reference rate + 2% OR prescribed applicable rate for mortgage qualification, with payment calculated using a 25 year amortization • Can be registered for the full appraised value of the home, not just the credit limit originally issued • Up to three different mortgage terms • Up to three different lines of credit • No fee re-advancement Homeowner’s Kit For broker information only OVERVIEW 2 of 2 603-08-101E (09/18/2023) CONVENTIONAL BROKER MORTGAGES 1. Registered as a single collateral charge. 2. This pre-payment privilege is non-cumulative and is available after the first anniversary of the mortgage. 3. The GDS/TDS ratios are calculated assuming 100% utilization of the requested credit limit for the revolving portion in addition to the total fixed mortgage portion. This product cannot be used in lieu of construction or bridge financing. Terms and conditions apply. All mortgages are funded by, registered in the name of, administered and serviced by B2B Bank. ®B2B BANK and BANKING THAT WORKS FOR BROKERS are registered trademarks of B2B Bank. ®HOMEOWNER’S KIT is a registered trademark of Laurentian Bank of Canada. B2B Bank is a subsidiary of Laurentian Bank of Canada. 4. As of October 31, 2023, new Canadian guidelines will limit the amount clients can use and reuse to a maximum of 65% of their property value without credit approval. Local support. National coverage. At B2B Bank, you’ll find a knowledgeable National Business Development team, experienced credit managers, competitive compensation and an impressive range of mortgage solutions. To us, that’s what banking that works for brokers is all about. For more information please contact your Business Development Manager or visit b2bbank.com/brokermortgages. Overview Eligible properties • 1 - 4 units • Owner occupied, primary residence Payment frequencies • Mortgage: Monthly, weekly, bi-weekly, accelerated weekly, accelerated bi-weekly • HELOC: Monthly (interest-only payment required) Pre-payment privileges Mortgage portion only • Increase payments by up to 15% once per calendar year2 • Pre-pay up to 15% of original principal once per calendar year ­Maximum amortization • Mortgage: Up to 30 years • HELOC: Revolving credit line Lending areas and loan amounts Contact your Business Development Manager for more information.

Notes • All properties must be legal rental units. • Student rentals, time share properties, including condo hotels, and short term rentals are not eligible. • Subject property is either owner occupied with a legal suite or a full rental property. • 50% of gross rental income from the subject rental property to be added to eligible income (excluding property tax and utilities). • Non-subject rental properties: Rental income x 80% Less: 100% P&I Less: 100% property tax Less: 50% condo fees Surplus or deficit Any remaining surplus is added to income. Any deficit is added to the liabilities.

entries.text: Our Net Worth Program helps to ensure your clients have access to mortgage solutions tailored to their unique situations. 1. Program overview For clients who have moderate income and meet the minimum requirement of proven liquid assets and satisfactory credit scores. Features Property type • Owner occupied and rental properties 1 - 4 units • Second homes 1 -2 units, vacation homes 1 unit • The property must comply with the neighbourhood and be well maintained (to be assessed on a case by case basis) • No deferred maintenance Lending areas and loan amounts Contact your Business Development Manager for more information. Max. amortization1 30 years For rental properties: 25 years Terms • 1 – 5, 7 and 10-year closed • 3 & 5-year VRM Min. credit score1 > 650 Max. GDS/TDS and LTV1 Owner occupied: TDS < 60% max. LTV 80% TDS > 60% max. LTV 65% Second homes/vacation homes: TDS < 60% max. LTV 65% Rental properties: TDS < 60% max. LTV 75% Other conditions2,3 • Standard income validation applies2,3 • Borrower must have a minimum of $150,000 in liquid assets • Full property appraisal required • Purchase or refinance are eligible • Only Canadian residents, claiming taxes in Canada, are eligible • No gifted down payment • No power of attorney • No previous bankruptcy, consumer proposal, no payments arrears or other major derogatory credit • No income tax arrears Net Worth Program For broker information only OVERVIEW 2 of 2 640-08-359E (11/21/2024) NET WORTH PROGRAM 1 Debt service flexibilities, LTVs offered and amortization options are based on the assessment of the strength of the application and are subject to change at any time without prior notification. Each application is assessed on a case by case basis. Satisfying the minimum credit score requirement alone does not automatically entitle the borrower to maximum LTV and amortization options or debt service flexibilities. Additional information and documentation may be requested at B2B Bank’s sole discretion for the approval of the loan. 2. The investment statement must be dated, in the borrower’s name and should identify the investment company, account number and balances. • Stocks Publicly traded on the TSX, S&P 500, Dow Jones Industrial Average, and Nasdaq Composite • Canadian savings bond • GICs • Chequing and savings accounts (business accounts are not admissible) • RESPs (capital only) • TFSAs • Retirement accounts (discounted by 30% for withholding tax) • Equity in a property being sold (firm P&S agreement is required) • Mutual funds • ESOP (assuming the assets are publicly traded) • RRIF • Locked-in accounts are not admissible • Segregated funds Note: Investments in American currency acceptable if held within Canada The exchange rate posted by the Bank of Canada will be used for qualification purposes.

entries.summary: Our Equity Program helps to ensure your clients have access to mortgage solutions tailored to their unique situations. Program overview This solution is ideal for borrowers who are salaried or self-employed and seeking to purchase or refinance a marketable property, where there is a large equity or down payment position. The borrower must have stable to moderate confirmable income1, and may have elevated debt service ratios. Features Property type • 1 – 4 units, owner occupied principal residence • The collateral must comply with the neighbourhood and be well maintained (to be assessed on a case by case basis) • No deferred maintenance • Property to be in an urban area with municipal service

entries.text: Form 160899 (03/22) Down Payment Gift Letter To: BMO Bank of Montreal This letter is to confirm the following: Name of Donor: ______________________ Relationship to Recipient: _________________ Name of Donor: ______________________ Relationship to Recipient: _________________ I am / We are providing a financial gift (the “Gift”) to: Name (borrower and recipient): _____________________________________________________ Name (borrower and recipient): _____________________________________________________ in the amount of $ _____________ CAD in the form of a monetary funds or equity in the purchase property to be used as part of or all of the down payment for the purchase of the following property: ______________________________________________________________________________ (Insert Property Address) Where my / our financial gift is $500,000 CAD or greater, the source(s) of the financial gift is from: (select all that apply) Employment Income Investment Income (Savings) Self-Employment Income Family Income Retirement/Pension Income Social Assistance Support Payments Insurance Claims/Payments Sale of Asset - Home/Business Trust / Inheritance Windfall-Casino/Contest/Lottery winnings Equity in purchase property The Gift is non-repayable. No part of the Gift is being provided by a third party having any interest (direct or indirect) in the sale of the Property. I acknowledge that this information will be used for mortgage approval purposes by BMO Financial Group and may be shared with CMHC (Canada Mortgage Housing Corporation), Sagen or Canada Guaranty for NHA mortgage loan insurance purposes. If you require any further information, please contact me at ___________________________________________. (Insert area code and phone number of Donor) ___________________________ __________________________ __________________ (Signature of Donor who gave the Gift) (Print name) (Date) ___________________________ __________________________ __________________ (Signature of Donor who gave the Gift) (Print name) (Date) _______________________________________________________________________________ Address of Donor(s) who gave the Gift ********************************************************************************************************************************************** I acknowledge receipt of the Gift and confirm that the statements above are accurate. ___________________________ __________________________ __________________ (Signature of borrower and Recipient) (Print name) (Date) 20/03/2024 1:21 pm Form 160899 (03/22) Certificate of Gifted Funds: (To be completed by Donor’s Canadian Financial Institution, if required) We certify that the above-named donor(s) _____________________, ______________________ (Name of Donor) (Name of Donor) has/have sufficient funds in their deposit accounts with us to cover the financial gift to the Recipient(s) as described above in the amount of $__________________ CAD. Dated this _____ day of __________, 20__. ___________________________________ ________________________________ (Name of Canadian Financial Institution) (Address with Branch Stamp) ___________________________________ ________________________________ (Name of Bank Representative) (Title of Bank Representative) ________________________________ (Signature of Bank Representative) _________________ (Telephone Number) -------------------------------------------------------------------------- END ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ 20/03/2024 1:21 pm

| Broker Handbook 1.0 KEY CONTACTS | 3 1.0 Key Contacts 1.1 Business Development Manager (BDM) Team.................................................4 1.2 Underwriting Team.............................................................................................4 1.3 Multi-Unit Residential (MUR) Mortgage............................................................6 1.4 BMO Branches.....................................................................................................6 1.5 Welcome Advisor (WA).......................................................................................6 1.6 Channel Protocol.................................................................................................6 | Broker Handbook 1.0 KEY CONTACTS | 4 1.0 Key Contacts 1.1 Business Development Manager (BDM) Team BMO has a strong team of dedicated professionals who are available to help brokers navigate transactions and grow the business. For questions about the following: • Signing up with BMO • Market presentations • Product training • Deal structuring guidance • Compensation • Deal escalation • Current campaigns and promotions Reach out to your local BDM, their contact information can be found on the BrokerEdge Portal. 1.2 Underwriting Team All files are reviewed by our Underwriting Centres. Deals are primarily assigned based on location; however, they may be reassigned at BMO’s discretion based on their capacity to maintain acceptable response times. Our Underwriting Centres and hours of operation are as follows: Centre Locations Phone Numbers Hours of Operation TBC Monday to Friday: 8:30 a.m.–5 p.m. ET Toronto Centre & Atlantic TBC Monday to Friday: 8:30 a.m.–5 p.m. ET Vancouver Centre & Praires TBC Monday to Friday: 8:30 a.m.–5 p.m. ET Montreal Centre | Broker Handbook 1.0 KEY CONTACTS | 5 New Deal Funds Released Deal Status Lawyer Instructions Escalation BMO Package Appraisal Documents • Underwriter OR • Business Development Manager • Underwriter OR • Business Development Manager • Refer to the BMO BrokerEdge Portal • Dedicated team member will be indicated • To be sent directly from the appraisal management company to the underwriter via email • Upload to the BMO BrokerEdge Portal • Document status can be viewed on the Portal • BMO commitment and signing package will be posted to the BMO BrokerEdge Portal • Review the BMO BrokerEdge Portal • Status will be reflected on the BMO BrokerEdge Portal • Contact your Business Development Manager if further clarification required It is recommended that all files be broker complete at least 10 days prior to funding to ensure optimal borrower experience. The BMO BrokerEdge Portal will provide the status of in-flight files along with the employee assigned to the file. To ensure efficient response times to questions, use the key contacts below through each step of the deal. 1 8 2 7 3 6 4 5 The BMO Broker Portal Key Contacts | Broker Handbook 1.0 KEY CONTACTS | 6 1.3 Multi-Unit Residential (MUR) Mortgage For questions relating to financing of multi-unit residential properties of more than four units, please connect with your BDM, who will introduce you to our Commercial Lending Team. 1.4 BMO Branches At time of closing, borrowers will be assigned a local full servicing branch for post-advance servicing needs such as change of payment frequency, change of funding account, etc. Borrowers may request a preferred full servicing branch. Brokers would be required to indicate the full servicing branch in their submission notes. If a full servicing branch is not indicated, a full servicing branch that is near the borrower’s home or work will be assigned. The assigned full servicing branch will be noted on the mortgage closing documentation package. Borrowers are not to be directed to a BMO branch until a full servicing branch has been assigned. 1.5 Welcome Advisor (WA) The Welcome Advisors (WA) are a virtual team dedicated to welcoming new borrowers from this line of business to BMO, ensuring a positive onboarding experience. The WA will proactively reach out to borrowers at predetermined intervals after approval, ensuring that borrowers are aware of BMO’s full suite of products and services. After the initial conversation, the WA will provide a warm introduction to the borrower’s local branch, which will be responsible for the ongoing borrower relationship. Brokers are encouraged to alert their borrowers that they will receive a call from BMO to welcome them. 1.6 Channel Protocol BMO borrowers can submit applications through many different channels (Online, BMO Mortgage Specialist, BMO Branch, North American Call Centre, or Broker). There may be times when a borrower has submitted their application through multiple channels for the same subject property; in this instance, BMO has a “first in” policy. If an existing application with the same subject property is identified in the system, then all other applications will not be permitted to proceed. | Broker Handbook 2.0 CONDUCT REQUIREMENTS | 7 2.0 Conduct Requirements 2.1 Required Conduct................................................................................................8 2.2 Product Appropriateness....................................................................................9 2.3 Prohibited Conduct...........................................................................................10 2.4 False or Misleading Information......................................................................10 2.5 Speaking Up About Misconduct.......................................................................10 2.6 Borrower Complaints........................................................................................11 2.7 Use Of BMO Logo..............................................................................................11 2.8 Privacy...............................................................................................................12 2.9 Broker Fees.......................................................................................................12 2.10 Broker/Agent Self-Submission.........................................................................12 2.11 Interprovincial Mortgage Brokering.................................................................12 | Broker Handbook 2.0 CONDUCT REQUIREMENTS | 8 2.0 Conduct Requirements 2.1 Required Conduct Doing What’s Right BMO’s conduct requirements outline the principles BMO expects brokers to support – BMO standards for integrity, fair dealing, product appropriateness and sustainability. By working with partners who share this commitment to these standards, BMO seeks to: • maintain a mutually beneficial, long-term relationship with our partners; and • uphold the reputation of both BMO and the partners we engage with These principles are incorporated in contractual arrangements. Compliance With Laws & Regulations BMO expects Broker to comply with the federal and provincial laws, regulations, guidelines and directives applicable in the jurisdictions in which they operate (e.g. consumer protection legislation, proceeds of crime legislation, etc.). This includes requirements such as not making any express or implicit agreements that violate the letter and spirit of those laws and regulations and communicating with consumers in a way that is clear, simple, and not misleading. Conflicts of Interest Brokers must be aware of potential conflicts and use good judgment to avoid them. Brokers must promptly disclose to their BMO Business Development Manager (BDM) all matters that could pose a potential, perceived or actual conflict of interest. Examples such as (but not limited to): • Broker/agent is related to the appraiser • Brokerage/agent/broker is also a lender • Favouring a lender for monetary reasons Anti-Corruption & Anti-Money Laundering Broker must never engage in any conduct that would put BMO at risk of violating any anti-corruption and anti-money laundering laws. Any potentially corrupt practices should be reported on a confidential, and if preferred, anonymous basis, to BMO through the Speak Up! reporting service available 24/7 in multiple languages or promptly contact the BDM and/or the BMO Anti-Corruption Office at aco@bmo.com. Important examples include: • Bribery: providing any item of value (such as a gift, favour, or cash sum) that could be viewed as an attempt to influence an individual’s actions or decision • Facilitating payments: providing money often a small amount, and usually cash for government services such as processing applications, permits, or expediting utility services • Gifts and entertainment: While exchanging customary business courtesies may be appropriate in certain situations, giving or receiving a gift or offer of entertainment is not appropriate if it is extravagant, too frequent, creates a sense of obligation, is done with the intent to influence a business decision or may be perceived as being done with the intent to influence a business decision | Broker Handbook 2.0 CONDUCT REQUIREMENTS | 9 Responsible Use of Information, Systems & Other Assets Brokers must protect and treat BMO’s confidential information as agreed to with BMO. This includes intellectual property, borrower information and any data BMO generates. Brokers are responsible for protecting BMO property as well as assets that belong to the borrowers. Brokers may use assets (e.g., borrower information data, systems, equipment, materials or premises) only for their authorized purpose. Let’s Connect Additional information about BMO, including how to contact us, can be found online at bmo.com. If you have any questions about conduct requirements, please contact your BDM. 2.2 Product Appropriateness There are many elements to determining the appropriateness of a product for a borrower, and brokers play a key part in determining this. All brokers must discuss a borrower’s financial needs and options ahead of all sales transactions to offer an appropriate product. It is the broker’s duty to know your borrower and collect this information. In addition to the Know Your Client (KYC) requirements, brokers are expected to comply with the Know Your Product (KYP) requirements and record the recommendation of the product to the borrower. Know Your Product (KYP) Brokers/agents are expected to: • Maintain up to date knowledge of BMO products and services, including their relevant features, charges, risks and benefits to the borrower • Regularly review product-specific information • Provide borrowers with all relevant and required disclosure or other information that could assist them in understanding the product • Explain BMO products and services, including their relevant features, charges, risks and benefits to borrowers in a manner that is clear, simple and not misleading • Maintain up to date on the BMO’s obligations related to the offer or sale of appropriate products or services Assessing Appropriateness & Offering Products/Services • Brokers/agents must consider KYC and KYP before offering any products and must also demonstrate consideration of the borrower’s financial needs • Brokers/agents must ensure that borrowers clearly understand the features, charges, risks and benefits of the home financing product Informing the Borrower & Documenting Appropriateness • The borrower cannot be forced to choose one product over another, and the final decision is always the borrower’s • If the borrower requests a specific product or service, the broker/agent must still conduct an appropriateness assessment • If the borrower selects a product that does not align with the guidance of the broker/agent, and is determined not appropriate for the borrower, or if the broker cannot conduct the assessment, the broker is required to inform the borrower accordingly Product appropriateness will be reviewed and acknowledged on the Preliminary Mortgage Loan Approval and Confirmation of Product Appropriateness. Borrowers will be required to acknowledge that the conversation has taken place. Product appropriateness will need to be re-acknowledged if there are any product changes, including product type, rate type and term length. | Broker Handbook 2.0 CONDUCT REQUIREMENTS | 10 2.4 False or Misleading Information BMO’s long tradition of commitment to high ethical standards is grounded in it's core values of integrity, empathy, diversity, and responsibility. It is important to BMO that borrowers are fully informed when transacting with brokers and choosing from BMO’s suite of products and services. Brokers must focus on doing what’s right for the borrower. Doing so means brokers shall not communicate or otherwise provide false or misleading information, knowingly or unknowingly, to BMO, the borrower, the public or the Commissioner. To help achieve this, Broker's must: • be familiar with the product or service being discussed with the Borrower • confirm details and ensure communication is clear and factual • avoid assuming or guessing the details of a product, service, or transaction • investigate thoroughly before providing information to a Borrower • ensure there is no omission of information that would be material to a Borrower making a financial decision 2.5 Speaking Up About Misconduct If anyone suspects someone working at BMO or providing a service on behalf of BMO is engaging, is planning to engage or has already engaged in misconduct such as improper sales practices, corruption or bribery, promptly report your concern through BMO’s Speak Up! Reporting Service. The Speak Up! Reporting Service is operated by an independent company to facilitate secure, confidential and anonymous reporting 24 hours a day, seven days a week, in multiple languages. For more information, please refer to the BMO Whistleblower Hotline. 2.3 Prohibited Conduct Broker plays a key role in representing BMO’s values when dealing with Borrower and the public by ensuring that they follow all current policies and procedures. It is incumbent on broker to understand BMO's expectations around prohibited conduct and how it is defined. When interacting with borrower and the public, broker is prohibited from: (a) Imposing undue pressure or coercing a person to obtain a product and service from a particular bank or any of its affiliates as a condition for obtaining another product or service from the bank Important: Undue pressure means any pressure, imposed in the form of a practice or communication or otherwise, that could be reasonably considered to be excessive or persistent in the circumstances. (b) Taking advantage of a borrower. Examples of Prohibited Conduct, Coercion, pressure, or taking advantage of a Broker can take many forms. | Broker Handbook 2.0 CONDUCT REQUIREMENTS | 11 2.6 Borrower Complaints “Complaint”, is broadly defined in the Financial Consumer Protection Framework (FCPF) under the Bank Act legislation as meaning dissatisfaction, whether justified or not, expressed to an institution with respect to: a. a product or service, in Canada that is offered, sold or provided by the institution; or b. the manner in which a product or service in Canada is offered, sold or provided by the institution. A. Complaints received about BMO products/services Brokers have a responsibility to immediately (not later than 2 business days following receipt of a complaint) report to the Business Development Manager any and all complaints (verbal or written) made by a borrower pertaining to BMO (i.e. BMO products, processes, or services), regardless of the borrower’s approval status (“BMO Complaint”). If an Underwriter becomes aware of a BMO Complaint, whether through a broker, third party supplier, subcontractor or otherwise, such Underwriter has a responsibility to immediately (not later than 2 business days following receipt of such complaint) report the BMO Complaint to the Business Development Manager. The Underwriter should also advise the broker that the broker must report the BMO Complaint to the Business Development Manager. In additi

entries.text: Form LF612 (07/11) Page 1 of 12 Hypothecation/Pledge of Securities and Accounts Dated as of: __________________ To Bank of Montreal (the "Bank"): This Agreement, except for Sections 2 and 9, shall be governed by the laws of the Province of _______________________________ (the "Province") and the federal laws of Canada applicable therein. Sections 2 and 9 of this Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the Province of Quebec and the federal laws of Canada applicable therein. Terms used in this Agreement that are defined in the Personal Property Security Act (the "PPSA") and, if applicable, the Securities Transfer Act (the "STA") in effect from time to time in the Province and not otherwise defined herein have the meanings ascribed to them in the STA or the PPSA, as applicable. To secure payment and performace of the Obligations (as defined below), for good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged by the undersigned, the undersigned agrees with the Bank as follows: 1) The undersigned hereby grants to the Bank a security interest in, and hereby deposits with and assigns, pledges and hypothecates to the Bank, all present and after- acquired personal property of the undersigned consisting of the following (collectively, the "Pledged Collateral"): (a) the securities, investment property, units, trust units, partnership, membership and other equity interests, participations, membership or other equivalent rights, guaranteed investment certificates, notes and other property (collectively, "Securities"), as applicable, listed in Part 1 of Schedule "A" attached hereto of the issuers listed opposite such Securities in Schedule "A" (collectively, the "Issuers" and individually, a "Issuer"), together with, from and after the date of the acquisition or issue thereof, all additional Securities referred to in Section 11(f) below of any Issuer and any other Securities in the capital of (or otherwise issued by) any Issuer issued to, or acquired by, the undersigned after the date of this Agreement, in each case whether now or in the future issued and outstanding, and the certificates, if any, representing such Securities and all proceeds, income, replacements, renewals, distributions, substitutions, accretions, dividends, cash, earnings, entitlements, rights and benefits from time to time received or receivable or otherwise distributed in respect of or in exchange for any or all of such Securities, and all rights, privileges, entitlements, proceeds and claims of any and every nature whatsoever of the undersigned in, to, or in respect of, or evidenced by, any of the foregoing (the whole, including any additional Securities identified in any supplement to Schedule ³A´ from time to time signed by the undersigned hereafter, collectively hereinafter called the "Securities Collateral"); and (b) all cash, credit balances, investment property (including but not limited to shares, stocks, warrants, bonds, debentures, debenture stock, mutual funds, treasury bills, instruments and other securities of whatsoever nature or kind, whether evidenced by a security certificate or an uncertificated security, and financial assets, security entitlements, securities accounts, future contracts and futures accounts and as that term is defined in the PPSA and the STA, as the same may be amended from time to time), intangibles, units, trust units, 20/03/2024 1:22 pm 1 - Branch 2 - Customer Form LF612 (07/11) Page 2 of 12 partnership, membership and other equity interests, participations, membership or other equivalent rights, guaranteed investment certificates, notes and other property and all proceeds, income, replacements, renewals, distributions, substitutions, accretions, dividends, cash, earnings, entitlements, rights and benefits relating to any of the foregoing or arising therefrom which are now or hereafter from time to time held in or credited to the account(s) (each separately, and collectively, if more than one, the "Account") described in Part 2 of Schedule A hereto, together with all such personal property which is otherwise specifically described in Part 2 of Schedule A hereto, in each case together with all rights, privileges, entitlements, proceeds and claims of any and every nature whatsoever of the undersigned in, to, or in respect of, or evidenced by, any of the foregoing; (the whole including any additional Accounts identified in any supplement to Schedule ³A´ from time to time signed by the undersigned hereafter, collectively hereinafter called the "Account Collateral"); as general and continuing collateral security for any indebtedness which is now or may at any time be due by the undersigned to the Bank, and as general and continuing collateral security for the fulfillment of all obligations, present or future, direct or indirect, absolute or contingent, matured or not of the undersigned to the Bank (together, the "Obligations"). 2) The hypothec by the undersigned created in Section 1 above shall be for an amount equal to twice the maximum principal amount of the authorized credit forming part of the Obligations on the date hereof with an additional amount equal to twenty percent (20%) of such amount, the whole with interest from the date hereof at the rate of twenty-five percent (25%) per annum, such amounts and such rate of interest are indicated for security purposes only, to comply with requirements of the laws of the Province of Québec. 3) The undersigned represents and warrants to and in favour of the Bank as follows: (a) the information provided in Schedule A is accurate and complete; (b) it is sole and absolute legal and beneficial owner of the Pledged Collateral; (c) the Pledged Collateral has not been pledged, hypothecated or otherwise made subject to a security interest in favour of any person other than the Bank (other than a security interest in favour of a securities intermediary in respect of the Account, provided that such security interest has been duly subordinated to the Bank¶s security interest hereunder or otherwise permitted pursuant to a control agreement entered into between the Bank, the undersigned and a securities intermediary for the purpose of perfecting the security interests granted to the Bank hereunder); (d) the undersigned has not entered into and is not aware of the existence of any control agreement to which any of the Pledged Collateral is subject (other than any control agreement entered into between the Bank, the undersigned and a securities intermediary for the purpose of perfecting the security interests granted to the Bank hereunder); 20/03/2024 1:22 pm 1 - Branch 2 - Customer Form LF612 (07/11) Page 3 of 12 (e) the Pledged Collateral does not include any certificated securities which the undersigned has not already delivered to the Bank or is not delivering to the Bank concurrent with the undersigned¶s execution of this Agreement; (f) all of the Securities have been duly and validly issued, are fully paid and non- assessable; (g) neither the undersigned nor any Issuer is a party to any agreement or instrument (including without limitation any shareholders' or other similar agreement) which would in any way limit, restrict or affect the rights, entitlements and benefits of the Bank or its nominee under this Agreement including, without limitation, the ability of the Bank or its nominee to exercise, enforce and realize on the rights, remedies and powers granted to the Bank or its nominee under this Agreement and/or any control agreement with the Bank; (h) no person, firm or corporation has any right to acquire or cause to be issued to them any of the Pledged Collateral and the undersigned shall not, without the prior written consent of the Bank, transfer, sell or otherwise dispose of, or enter into any agreement to transfer, sell or otherwise dispose of, or grant any option respecting, any of the Pledged Collateral; (i) the Account is not a margin account, and the undersigned has not entered into any agreement or arrangement (a "Discretionary Arrangement") pursuant to which any securities intermediary is authorized to buy or sell any Account Collateral on behalf of the undersigned in such securities intermediary¶s discretion; (j) The undersigned has the full power, authority and legal right and capacity to incur and perform its obligations under this Agreement, and if a corporation, partnership or entity other than an individual, has been duly formed and validly exists under the laws of the jurisdiction of its formation, and has taken all necessary corporate or other action required to authorize the making and performance of this Agreement; (k) this Agreement (i) has been duly executed and delivered by the undersigned, and (ii) constitutes a valid and binding obligation of the undersigned enforceable against the undersigned in accordance with its terms, subject to bankruptcy, insolvency, moratorium and other laws of general application affecting creditors' rights generally and subject to the discretion of a court to grant equitable remedies and to apply general principles of equity; (l) the making and the performance by the undersigned of this Agreement do not violate the provisions of any law or regulation applicable to the undersigned or any agreement to which the undersigned is a party or by which it is bound or require any consent from any third party or any government or governmental agency or authority; and (m) if the undersigned is an individual, the complete name and principle place of residence of the undersigned are set forth beneath the undersigned¶s signature below. 20/03/2024 1:22 pm 1 - Branch 2 - Customer Form LF612 (07/11) Page 4 of 12 All of the foregoing representations and warranties shall survive the execution and delivery of this Agreement and shall be deemed to be continuously made under this Agreement until such time as this Agreement is terminated. 4) The undersigned agrees with the Bank that (i) each Account identified in Schedule A is a "securities account" within the meaning of the STA and PPSA; and (ii) all property credited to an Account, all other investment property and financial assets relating to the Account and all of the other personal property described in Section 1(b) above, including substitutions or replacements thereof, and any proceeds, dividends or payments of money relating thereto and all credit balances and cash balances, and all other rights of the undersigned against the intermediary maintaining the Account arising out of the Account, are included in the definition of "Pledged Collateral" for the purposes of this Agreement and will be treated as "financial assets" for the purposes of the STA and PPSA. 5) The undersigned shall be in default under this Agreement upon the occurrence of any one of the following events (each a "Default"): a) payment in respect of the Obligations has been demanded from the undersigned, or the Obligations have otherwise become immediately due and payable, and payment has not been made by the undersigned, or the undersigned is otherwise in default in respect of the Obligations; b) the undersigned is in default in the due observance or performance of any covenant, undertaking or agreement heretofore or hereafter given to the Bank, whether contained herein or not and including any covenant or undertaking set out in any schedule to this Agreement; c) an execution or any other process of any court becomes enforceable against the undersigned or a distress or analogous process is levied upon the property of the undersigned or any part thereof; d) the undersigned becomes insolvent or commits an act of bankruptcy, or makes an assignment in bankruptcy or a bulk sale of its assets or a bankruptcy petition is filed or presented against the undersigned and is not opposed by the undersigned, acting in good faith or if opposed is not set aside within five business days; e) the undersigned ceases to carry on business; or f) any representation, warranty or statement which is made by the undersigned to the Bank, whether contained herein or not and including any representation, warranty or statement set out in any schedule to this Agreement, is untrue or incorrect when made in any material respect. 6) Following the occurrence of a Default, the Bank may forthwith realize on the Pledged Collateral by any method not prohibited by law, and, subject to law, may have transferred into its own possession or sell (or give instructions to the applicable securities intermediary to sell) the Pledged Collateral, or any of them, on any recognized exchange dealing in such Pledged Collateral or by public or private sale, and enforce payment and realize upon security of any kind (including with respect to any investment property) comprised amongst the Pledged Collateral as fully and effectually as if the Bank were the absolute owner thereof. Without limiting the 20/03/2024 1:22 pm 1 - Branch 2 - Customer Form LF612 (07/11) Page 5 of 12 foregoing, following the occurrence of a Default, the Bank may realize on the Pledged Collateral by sale for deferred payment or redemption before maturity with or without penalty, and the Bank shall not be responsible for any loss or damage to the Pledged Collateral whether caused by the negligence or fault of the Bank, its servants or agents, or a sheriff or receiver, and the Bank shall not be obliged to preserve rights against other persons or keep the Pledged Collateral identifiable or protect the Pledged Collateral or prepare the Pledged Collateral for disposition, and shall only be liable to account for funds (net of costs of collection, realization and sale, including solicitor and his/her own client legal costs), actually received by the Bank. 7) The records of the Bank as to whether or not a Default has occurred or of any demand having been made shall, in the absence of manifest error, be final and conclusive without further or other proof. 8) The Bank shall not be bound under any circumstances to realize upon any Pledged Collateral or allow any Pledged Collateral to be sold, and shall not be responsible for any loss occasioned by any sale of any Pledged Collateral or by the retention of or refusal to sell the same. 9) In addition to the rights and remedies specifically provided for in this Agreement, the Bank shall, following the occurrence of a Default, have the rights and remedies of a hypothecary creditor under the Civil Code of Quebec and, if applicable, An Act respecting the transfer of securities and the establishment of securities entitlements (Quebec). No remedy for the enforcement of the rights of the Bank under this Agreement shall be exclusive of, or dependent on, any other such remedy but any one or more of such remedies may from time to time be exercised in whole or in part, independently or in combination. The Bank shall be entitled to exercise its rights hereunder without having to give a prior notice thereof to the undersigned. 10) The undersigned hereby irrevocably constitutes and appoints the Bank, or any Manager thereof, as the true and lawful attorney of the undersigned and each of them (if more than one), with full power of substitution, to transfer, endorse, negotiate and sign on behalf of the undersigned and each of them (if more than one) all or any of the Pledged Collateral and to fill in all blanks in any transfers of stocks, bonds or debentures or any power of attorney or other documents delivered to it, to provide instructions or entitlement orders to any securities intermediary which maintains any Account in which any Pledged Collateral is maintained, and to delegate its powers and for any delegate to sub-delegate the same, and to exercise any of the powers hereby given in the name and on the behalf of the heirs or devisees, executors or administrators of the undersigned or (if more than one) of such of them as may be dead, and none of the powers hereby given shall be revoked by the death of the undersigned or any of them (if more than one). 11) The undersigned covenants and agrees in favour of the Bank as follows: a) the undersigned will defend its title to the Pledged Collateral and the security interests granted hereby against the claims and demands of any other person and will maintain and preserve such security interests as perfected security interests; b) except as may be permitted under this Agreement, the undersigned shall not sell, assign, transfer, pledge, encumber, grant a security interest in, or control 20/03/2024 1:22 pm 1 - Branch 2 - Customer Form LF612 (07/11) Page 6 of 12 over (by control agreement or otherwise) the Pledged Collateral or any Account, or otherwise dispose of any right, title or interest it now has or may hereafter acquire in or to the Pledged Collateral, nor will it agree to do any of the foregoing; c) the undersigned will, at its expense, execute, endorse, acknowledge and deliver to the Bank, or as the Bank may direct, all such proxies, stock powers, instruments, control agreements, entitlement orders and other documents and take all such action (including, without limitation, obtaining governmental, corporate, securities intermediary, securities broker or other consents) and do or cause to be done all such other things as the Bank may, from time to time, deem necessary or advisable or reasonably request in order to give full effect to this Agreement to perfect, preserve and protect the rights intended to be granted to the Bank hereunder; d) without limiting the foregoing, upon demand of the Bank, the undersigned will: (a) cause all of the Securities Collateral to be duly transferred to the Bank or its nominee and cause all certificates, if any, representing Securities Collateral to be re-registered in the Bank's name or in the name of its nominee, as the case may be; This verification is required to be provided at the time of mortgage application. Option 1 Donor(s) to request their financial institution to provide the confirmation below: When you have completed and signed this agreement, please submit this form to the BWB underwriting department with your void cheque or provide your bank account information above and obtain a teller stamp from your financial institution. Keep a copy for your records. If you have any questions or wish to obtain further information regarding this agreement, please contact Bridgewater Bank’s Underwriting Department Option 2 Donor(s) to provide a current copy of financial institution or investment statement(s) confirming they have sufficient funds available to provide the above-noted financial gift to the recipient(s). The statement(s) must be recent and dated within 90 days of your signature on this letter. This letter, along with any supporting documents will be retained as part of the mortgage file and in accordance with Bridgewater Bank’s Privacy Policy available online at https://www.bridgewaterbank.ca/privacy/. I/we hereby authorize Community Trust Company and any of its representatives to disclose any information concerning me/us in your possession, including information of a personal nature, and discuss any other facts or details concerning my/our account(s) with the individual named below. This form does not authorize the named individual to request maintenance or changes to the status or terms and conditions of the account. Community Trust Company is committed to protecting the personal information of our clients. Personal information obtained in the course of doing business with you is not collected, used or disclosed except in compliance with applicable provincial and federal legislation, including Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act (PIPEDA). This Authorization shall continue in force until revoked in writing me/us. entries.text: Succeeding together Our residential lending toolkit is here to help you and your clients Last updated: March 2024 Dear Mortgage Professional, Welcome to Community Trust, a flexible alternative to larger, more traditional financial institutions. We were established in 1975, and acquired in 2019 by Questrade Financial Group (QFG), one of Canada's leading FinTech companies. We have been working with our valued partners for many years to help Canadians achieve the security of owning their home, and remain 100% committed to serving the mortgage broker community. We are regulated by the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions (OSFI). Why choose us? > We are a leader in the alternative lending space We are not your typical financial institution. Instead of finding reasons to say "No", we are always looking for alternative approaches to assist Canadians in achieving their home ownership goals through flexible and innovative solutions. > We are a leader in technology With platforms such as Community Trust Café, InstaShare and InstaValue, as well as much more to come, we constantly strive to find new ways to make working with us even faster and easier. > We are a leader in service Our award-winning 360-Service approach is one that provides an elite level of service, giving you direct access to a dedicated team of experienced mortgage advisors. > We genuinely care About the partners we work with, about their clients, and about helping Canadians become more financially successful and secure. That underlying motivation is what drives everything we do, and is why we are so passionate about building relationships based on trust, collaboration, integrity and responsiveness. Our goal has always been to help you and your clients succeed, and the contents of this toolkit illustrate how we’re doing more than ever to make that happen. Should you choose to partner with us on your next deal, you’ll see just how serious we are about that commitment. We look forward to getting started! Sincerely, Sebastien Kuperhause Director, Business Development Ready to connect, or need more information? T: +1 (888) 649-1169 E: partners@communitytrust.com The information contained here is for broker use in Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia and Atlantic provinces only, and subject to change at our sole discretion. Flexible down payment options, including shared equity through partners such as Lotly and Ourboro (owner occupied, first position conventional mortgages only) Conventional mortgages Conventional mortgages (first or second position) Purchases and refinances Owner occupied up to 80% LTV Investment property up to 80% LTV Standard ratios of 50% GDS and 50% TDS Minimum credit of 500 FICO Maximum amortization of 35 years *Additional documentation may be required. Each mortgage application is subject to approval based on its own merits in accordance with Community Trust lending criteria. Eligible property types: > Single family homes > Row & townhouses > Condo/strata > Well & septic > Other non-traditional property types also considered - check with your BDM! Types of property Non-eligible property types: > Modular > Student rentals > Leased land > Commercial, industrial or agricultural use > Multi-unit homes (max. 4 units) > Rental pools > Hotel-condos > Airbnb-style and rooming house Who qualifies? Self-employed* Articles of incorporation/Business license, HST/GST license (if applicable), and either of the following: > Six months bank statements with supporting invoices, and Business Disclosure Statement > Two years T1 Generals and Notice of Assessments Other acceptable sources of income* > Child tax credit/UCB > Foster care income > Maternity/paternity income > Seasonal employment > Tip income > Disability income > Investment income Salaried* > Current letter of employment > Current paystub > Current paystub with InstaShare confirming 12 months direct deposit income > Alimony/child support > Pension income > Contract income Ready to connect, or need more information? T: +1 (888) 649-1169 E: partners@communitytrust.com The information contained here is for broker use in Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia and Atlantic provinces only, and subject to change at our sole discretion. Some borrowers may require even more resourcefulness than those who fit the alternative profile. For these especially unique clients, Community Trust offers non-conforming mortgages. This program pairs perfectly with clients who have: > Difficulty providing standard income documentation > Lower FICO scores than are traditionally considered > Higher qualifying ratios Program highlights Program details Owner occupied and / or investment properties Up to 65% LTV on purchases and refinances No minimum FICO score 65 LTV Up to 65% GDS Higher ratios can be considered TDS Higher ratios can be considered Maximum amortization 35 years Don’t see what you’re looking for? Give us a call to create a customized offering. No two situations are alike, so we'll do everything we can to help you succeed. > Non-conforming mortgages Ready to connect, or need more information? T: +1 (888) 649-1169 E: partners@communitytrust.com The information contained here is for broker use in Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia and Atlantic provinces only, and subject to change at our sole discretion. Community Trust offers second mortgages on owner-occupied and investment properties in both first and second position. Program highlights Program details Minimum loan $100K, or $25K if first mortgage is with us Owner occupied Up to 80% LTV Investment properties Up to 80% LTV GDS Up to 50% TDS Up to 50% Maximum amortization 35 years Already have a Community Trust mortgage and looking to top up? Get in touch with us to ask about our existing client specials. Second mortgages Don’t see what you’re looking for? Give us a call to create a customized offering. No two situations are alike, so we'll do everything we can to help you succeed. > Up to 80% LTV on owner-occupied and rentals Will go behind most 'A' lenders, including credit unions and monolines 80 A Ready to connect, or need more information? T: +1 (888) 649-1169 E: partners@communitytrust.com The information contained here is for broker use in Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia and Atlantic provinces only, and subject to change at our sole discretion. The Community Trust investment property program is the perfect fit for smaller investors looking to purchase or refinance an investment property. Program highlights 50 BPS rate premium Up to 80% LTV on purchases and refinances All applicant types are eligible 80 Investment property program Program details LTV Up to 80% Standard GDS Up to 50% Standard TDS Up to 50% Maximum amortization 35 years Completion rate for appraisals 80% 50 Don’t see what you’re looking for? Give us a call to create a customized offering. No two situations are alike, so we'll do everything we can to help you succeed. > Subject properties: > 90% of gross rental income added to income > 100% of PITH added to liabilities Non-subject properties: > 90% of gross rental income, less rental expenses > Surplus may be added to income, deficits added to liabilities Rental Income Ready to connect, or need more information? T: +1 (888) 649-1169 E: partners@communitytrust.com The information contained here is for broker use in Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia and Atlantic provinces only, and subject to change at our sole discretion. Community Trust offers additional documentation options for those who dare to do things differently. This program pairs perfectly with clients who: > Have bruised credit or previous bankruptcy > Have established a reasonable ability to make future mortgage payments > Are self-employed, or commissioned with stated income, and are unable to provide traditional documentation Documentation requirements > Proof of business ownership (business license or certificate of incorporation) and a minimum of two years experience in the field > Self-employed documentation (choose one) Option 1: Two years of T1 Generals and corresponding NOAs Option 2: Business Disclosure Statement, six months of bank statements and invoices or receipts supporting a minimum of three deposits or expenses Business for Self (BFS) program Don't see an option that supports your client? Contact us to discuss their needs. > Ready to connect, or need more information? T: +1 (888) 649-1169 E: partners@communitytrust.com The information contained here is for broker use in Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia and Atlantic provinces only, and subject to change at our sole discretion. With Community Trust’s Home Equity Line of Credit, you can offer your clients the opportunity to repurpose their existing home to aid with future expenses. This program is ideal for clients who: > Already have a mortgage with Community Trust, or own property outright > Need access to secured credit for an upcoming or future large expense > Want to consolidate higher interest rate debt on other loans Program highlights Program details No maximum loan size Up to 80%* combined LTV on purchases and refinances Owner occupied residences only 80 LTV Up to 80%* GDS Up to 50% TDS Up to 50% (not standard) Payments Interest only Commission 50 BPS paid on HELOC limit Home Equity Line of Credit * HELOC portion may not exceed 65% of the value of the property Don’t see what you’re looking for? Give us a call to create a customized offering. No two situations are alike, so we'll do everything we can to help you succeed. > Ready to connect, or need more information? T: +1 (888) 649-1169 E: partners@communitytrust.com The information contained here is for broker use in Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia and Atlantic provinces only, and subject to change at our sole discretion. Our award-winning 360-Service approach has redefined the way we help you by providing an elite level of total service for every partner. Enjoy direct access to our dedicated team of experienced mortgage advisors – on standby from 8:00 a.m. to 8:00 p.m. ET every business day – to get the expertise, answers and decisions you need faster than ever before. Along with enjoying shorter turnaround times, you’ll have a single location for: > Information and updates on current applications > Answers regarding an in-flight application > Information related to existing mortgages, renewals, refinances, taxes, insurance, payouts and discharges, and more > A review that assures a new deal fits within our policies > Reviewing conditions with us, finding solutions that work for your client > Updating applications, providing more details to our underwriting team > Reviewing a declined deal to determine alternative options Working with us is easy 1 (888) 649-1169 partners@communitytrust.com Community Trust Café Ready to connect, or need more information? T: +1 (888) 649-1169 E: partners@communitytrust.com The information contained here is for broker use in Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia and Atlantic provinces only, and subject to change at our sole discretion. Our online broker hub -- available for desktop and mobile -- gives you direct access to more information about your deals in a simpler format, shows a clear history of all activity, and makes communication between you and our team seamless. It offers all you need to be more productive and successful, so you can spend more time building client relationships. Instant and secure document management Save time with less hassle and paperwork, simple uploading and easy, secure document sharing. Streamlined message centre Directly connect to us, with notifications of our responses and a saved record of all communications. Information-rich resource centre Whether it's relevant information from us or industry-related news, you'll find it here. Conditions Manager feature Upload, keep track, and review documents easily and more efficiently. Up to date deal tracker All active deal information is constantly refreshed, with notifications sent for every update. Current rates, offers and promotions Review our best rates, newest promotions and incentives all in one place. Handy tools and calculators The tools you need to work faster and more efficiently are all right here at the Café To learn more about Community Trust Café call us at 1 (888) 649-1169 or email partners@communitytrust.com. Community Trust Café / All you need in one place > Ready to connect, or need more information? T: +1 (888) 649-1169 E: partners@communitytrust.com The information contained here is for broker use in Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia and Atlantic provinces only, and subject to change at our sole discretion. Community Trust InstaShare makes sharing your clients' required banking information lightning fast and remarkably easy. Provide your clients with their Community Trust loan number, which can be obtained from their mortgage commitment letter. Ask your clients to visit communitytrust.com/InstaShare for more information. Sit back and enjoy the hassle-free process. How to get started Benefits of InstaShare A convenient platform for your clients to share their bank statements directly with us Quick and direct transfer of required verification data in just a few seconds 1 2 3 To learn more about InstaShare call us at 1 (888) 649-1169 or email partners@communitytrust.com. > InstaShare / Quick, easy sharing of banking information Ready to connect, or need more information? T: +1 (888) 649-1169 E: partners@communitytrust.com The information contained here is for broker use in Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia and Atlantic provinces only, and subject to change at our sole discretion. Community Trust InstaValue is our automated valuation service that removes the inconvenience and time of a home appraiser's visit from the entire process. Or in case a full appraisal is still required, we'll let you know this at the Commitment stage. > Eligible on both purchase and refinance transactions > Owner occupied properties > Eligible for LTVs up to 80% > Minimum FICO Score of 610+ > Maximum property value of $1.5 million** Program details Benefits of InstaValue Quick, easy home valuation done entirely by us No home visitation required No upfront cost to your client* *A $399 fee is deducted at closing. If your client’s transaction doesn’t meet the InstaValue qualifications or the property value is not supported, there will be no fee charged to your client for the InstaValue service, however a full appraisal will be required at the expense of the borrower. **Geographical and other terms and conditions apply. To learn more about InstaValue call us at 1 (888) 649-1169 or email partners@communitytrust.com. > InstaValue / Home evaluation at your fingertips

entries.text: Down Payment Gift Letter Form 2350 Matheson Boulevard East | Mississauga, ON | L4W 5G9 | (416) 763-2291 | T: 1 (800) 268-1576 | communitytrust.com If a ﬁnancial gift is being used as part or all of the down payment, applicants and the gift donors must conﬁrm that the gift meets certain regulatory and legislative requirements. Applicant Information This letter conﬁrms that a ﬁnancial gift has been made to the applicants(s) to assist in the purchase of a property according to the details provided below. Total Amount of Financial Gift: $ Confirmation By signing this document, I/we, as the donor(s), conﬁrm the following: • These funds are being provided as a gift and will never have to be repaid. • I/we am/are a relative of the applicant and that no part of the financial gift is being provided by any third party having any direct or indirect interest in the purchase and/or sale of the subject property referred to in this letter. Verification In addition to conﬁrming the gifted funds, the donor(s) may be asked to provide evidence of suﬃcient funds to cover the amount of the gifted funds. Acceptable documentation must show the amount of the gifted funds and includes a bank statement, investment statement, web statement, or passbook. Community Trust Company is committed to protecting the personal information of our clients. Personal information obtained in the course of doing business with you is not collected, used or disclosed except in compliance with applicable provincial and federal legislation, including Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act (PIPEDA). Primary method Obtain 1 of the following: • Recent paystub • 2 months of recent direct deposit history • Current letter of employment If a letter of employment and another additional documentation from a primary method is obtained, verification using a secondary method is not required. Secondary method Obtain 1 of the following: • Previous year-end paystub • Most recent T4 • Most recent NOA For Variable/Fluctuating Income follow Insurer guideline Use a primary and secondary verification method. Primary method Obtain 1 of the following: • Recent paystub • 2 months of recent direct deposit history • Current letter of employment If a letter of employment and another additional documentation from a primary method is obtained, verification using a secondary method is not required. Secondary method Obtain 1 of the following: • Previous year-end paystub • T4 • NOA • Verbal verification with employer Calculation No averaging is required. • Up to 100% of bi-weekly income x 26 • Up to 100% of monthly income x 12 • Up to 100% of annual income For Variable/Fluctuating Income follow Insurer guideline. Calculation No averaging is required. • Up to 100% of bi-weekly income x 26 • Up to 100% of monthly income x 12 • Up to 100% of annual income © DUCA Financial Services Credit Union Ltd. | Broker Services Retail Guidelines 9 Income Type: Casual, Secondary Job, Contract, Seasonal, Overtime, Bonuses, Tips, and Commissions Insurer Verification (High Ratio, and Prime Insurable) Traditional Verification (Prime Non-Insurable, HELOC, Non-Readvanceable HELOC, Near Prime, and Bruised) Use a primary and secondary verification method. Primary method Obtain 1 of the following: • Recent paystub • Current letter of employment Secondary method Obtain 2 of the following to verify income for the past 2 years: • Previous year-end paystub • T4 • NOA *For Variable/Fluctuating Income follow Insurer guideline. Use a primary and secondary verification method. Primary method Obtain 1 of the following: • Recent paystub • Current letter of employment Secondary method Obtain 2 of the following to verify income for the past 2 years: • Previous year-end paystub • T4 • NOA • Verbal verification with employer Calculation Averaging is required. • Up to 100% of 2-year average For seasonal workers no more than 30% of income should be EI. Calculation Averaging is required. • Up to 100% of the most recent year • Income from the most recent year can be used for cases where income has shown a steady increase (multiple years) • If income has been showing a steady decline (multiple years) a lower amount than the 2-year average should be used • Otherwise, use up to 100% of 2-year average. For seasonal workers no more than 30% of income should be EI. © DUCA Financial Services Credit Union Ltd. | Broker Services Retail Guidelines 10 Income Type: Foreign Sources Insurer Verification (High Ratio, and Prime Insurable) Traditional Verification (Prime Non-Insurable, HELOC, Non-Readvanceable HELOC, Near Prime, and Bruised) Use a primary and secondary verification method. Primary method Obtain 1 of the following: • Recent paystub • Current letter of employment Secondary method Obtain the following: • NOA for the past 2 years Use a primary and secondary verification method. Primary method Obtain 1 of the following: • Recent paystub • Current letter of employment Secondary method Obtain the following: • NOA for the past 2 years Calculation Averaging is required. • Up to 100% of 2-year average Calculation Averaging is required. • Up to 100% of 2-year average Income Type: Maternity Leave, and Paid Parental Leave Insurer Verification (High Ratio, and Prime Insurable) Traditional Verification (Prime Non-Insurable, HELOC, Non-Readvanceable HELOC, Near Prime, and Bruised) Calculation 100% of return-to-work income if: • Written confirmation has been obtained from the employer stating anticipated return to work date The applicant has the resources to cover monthly mortgage payments while on leave.
