Application processing....................................................................................................... 31 f. Decision..................................................................................................................................32 11. Useful links...........................................................................................33 Table of contents 3 Data privacy This guide is reserved exclusively for mortgage brokers accredited by Desjardins to operate in Ontario. The information contained in this guide is confidential and cannot be disclosed, published, communicated, exploited or used in any way whatsoever, except as part of the services provided by an accredited mortgage broker. In addition, this guide cannot be reproduced, remitted, distributed or transmitted to anyone whatsoever, including clients of the accredited broker. In addition, it’s your responsibility to ensure that you comply with the various obligations set out in the business partnership between Desjardins and your agency. This guide applies to both conventional and insured financing applications. In the latter case, the terms and conditions set out in the insurers’ insurance policies and programs must also be upheld. A Ontario broker cannot submit an application to purchase or refinance a property in Quebec unless one of the borrowers is currently a Ontario resident. Otherwise, the application must be submitted by a mortgage broker licensed in the province of Quebec. A separate version of this guide is also available for the exclusive use of Desjardins-accredited mortgage brokers operating in Quebec. Important All the information contained in this guide is provided solely for the purpose of helping you submit mortgage financing applications at Desjardins. This guide must not in any way be interpreted as a commitment on the part of Desjardins to approve any such applications. A number of factors may influence decisions on whether or not to accept mortgage applications. Certain conditions may apply, which may be modified from time to time. The information in this guide may also be modified without prior notice. It’s the broker’s responsibility to ensure that the insurers’ standards are met (CMHC and Sagen) and to stay up to date on these rules. Guide for Ontario mortgage brokers 1. 4 Back to Table of Contents About Desjardins  Canada’s leading financial cooperative, with more than 7 million members and clients.  First Canadian financial institution to sign the UN’s Principles for Responsible Banking.  The largest mortgage lender in Quebec.  An extensive multinetwork presence ranging from brick and mortar to virtual and mobile offerings and customer contact centres. ✓ For its fast, efficient processing of mortgage financing applications ✓ For its competitive rates ✓ For its exclusive member advantages ✓ For its special offers ✓ For its product flexibility ✓ Because we’ve been the leading lender in the Quebec mortgage broker market since 2018 At Desjardins, being a member is always an advantage. A Desjardins caisse is a financial services cooperative that belongs to its members. When they open an account to process their mortgage and deposit their membership share, your clients will become members of a financial services cooperative. Opening such an account is mandatory, as is membership in the cooperative. Feel free to consult the Useful link section at the end of the document. As a cooperative financial group, Desjardins Group looks at the bottom line every year and pays a portion of surplus earnings to members in the form of a dividend. These dividends are set for each member based on their use of their cooperative’s financial services and their contribution to the cooperative’s surplus earnings. 2. Why choose Desjardins? Desjardins’s distinct advantages: Member dividends a b 5 Back to Table of Contents Eligible transactions Requirements for submitting a mortgage pre-approval request: ● Application for purchases only. ● FICO score of 720+ for at least 1 borrower. It all takes up to 4 hours. The Fico rating is required, the number of logs is required, and the rate increase must be included in your application and mentioned in your notes. ● Completed consent form (Desjardins (Ontario) and broker consents) If 1 of these requirements is not met, the application will be automatically rejected. You will need to resubmit the application ensuring that the file is complete and compliant. Please note that no documentary proof of income or situation is required to submit a mortgage pre- approval request. The analysis is based on the borrower’s declaration only. In addition, in the interest of transparency and to meet the various regulatory requirements, Desjardins would like to remind you that the Act to modernize legislative provisions as regards the protection of personal information has come into effect. To meet one of the new requirements, Desjardins has added a note in its declaration and consent forms. Desjardins is counting on you to comply with this law, mainly by explaining to clients what personal information was used to make the decision, as well as the reasons and main factors that led to it. The pre-approval is valid for 6 months, regardless of how long the rate is guaranteed. Note: The rate guarantee rules in effect at the time of the application also apply when the loan is granted. If you have any questions regarding this change, please contact your BDM. For purchases: All conventional and insured mortgages, excluding self-builds. For mortgages that are already insured (purchase or refinancing): The premium portability program is eligible at the analysis centre, according to the insurer’s eligibility criteria. You are responsible for providing the previous application number, including the balance and remaining amortization. For refinancing: The loan-to-value ratio is up to 80% of the market value. 3. Mortgage pre-approval (purchase) Purchasing or refinancing Purchase or refinancing with renovations a b c Act 25 Desjardins gives you different ways to finance your clients’ renovation projects. Insurer (CMHC and Sagen) and Desjardins requirements have been standardized. We’ve also defined the document requirements, which will make our communication with members and clients more effective, since they can now know what documents they need in advance. Important: The Disbursement practice and the disbursement methods remain unchanged. The 10% threshold (cost of improvements to the value of the property) for insured loans has been replaced by a $30,000 threshold (total cost of the renovation project). ■ Rule ● Insured files: The requirements issued in this practice take precedence over the insurers’ requirements for improvement financing (CMHC) or the Purchase Plus Improvements Program (Sagen). ● Documentation: The documentation required to confirm the renovations to be financed and the work to be completed is defined based on the valuation method used to determine the fair market value (FMV). ● New Declaration of Renovations form to simplify traceability and follow-up for renovations to be financed and completed (available from the rate bulletin). ■ Practice summary: All requirements must be met, no exceptions possible. ● Buying a home with renovations: • Insured: The document requirements and program to enter will be determined based on a $30,000 renovation threshold. We’ll no longer be using percentages based on property values. • Conventional: Required documentation will depend on the valuation method used to confirm the fair market value (FMV). ● Refinancing with renovations: • The document requirement will only be necessary when a professional appraisal report showing the future value of the property is used to confirm the FMV. 6 Back to Table of Contents Eligible transactions (cont.) Desjardins gives you different ways to finance your clients’ renovation projects.  Progressive disbursements Your role is to explain the steps throughout the disbursement process. ● Proof will be requested from the member as disbursements are made and must substantiate the disbursement amount. For

entries.summary: CF-00100-096A (ON) Page 1 of 2 2023-09 NAME OF MORTGAGE AGENCY: (hereinafter referred to as the “Agency”) NAME OF MORTGAGE BROKER: (hereinafter referred to as the “Broker”) BORROWER 1 Name: BORROWER 3 Name: GUARANTOR Name: BORROWER 2 Name: BORROWER 4 Name: DECLARATIONS Each of the undersigned declares that all the information provided in the mortgage loan application completed through the Agency and the Broker (herei­ nafter referred to as the “Mortgage Application”) is truthful and complete in every respect, that they have no creditors other than those declared on this application and that they know that the Desjardins caisse to which this application will be submitted (hereinafter referred
entries.text: CF-00100-096A (ON) Page 1 of 2 2023-09 NAME OF MORTGAGE AGENCY: (hereinafter referred to as the “Agency”) NAME OF MORTGAGE BROKER: (hereinafter referred to as the “Broker”) BORROWER 1 Name: BORROWER 3 Name: GUARANTOR Name: BORROWER 2 Name: BORROWER 4 Name: DECLARATIONS Each of the undersigned declares that all the information provided in the mortgage loan application completed through the Agency and the Broker (herei­ nafter referred to as the “Mortgage Application”) is truthful and complete in every respect, that they have no creditors other than those declared on this application and that they know that the Desjardins caisse to which this application will be submitted (hereinafter referred to as the “Caisse”) and Fédération des caisses Desjardins du Québec (hereinafter referred to as the “Federation”) will use this information to respond to this Mortgage Application or accept the guarantee offer. In this regard, each of the undersigned declares that they have read the information reproduced on screen or on the support document used by the Agency and the Broker, including new data entered directly by the Broker, and have verified that it is accurate and complete. Each of the under­ signed also declares that they have not concealed any information that would affect the decision of the Caisse or Federation. The fact that financing or a portion of financing is granted or that Canada Mortgage and Housing Corporation (hereinafter referred to as “CMHC”) agrees to insure financing must not be construed or relied upon by the borrower(s) and the guarantor(s), if any, as a confirmation of the value or state of the property sought, regardless of whether assessments or inspections have been performed by CMHC or on its behalf, or as a confirmation of their ability to repay the financing. Each of the undersigned acknowledges that the Caisse to which the Mortgage Application is submitted will have the right not to act on it, notwithstanding any commitment it may make in this regard, if any of the undersigned fail to act on this application within thirty (30) days after it is accepted, if significant changes occur in the financial situation of any of the undersigned or in the property provided as collateral on the loan, if any of the undersigned fail to provide the titles or other documents required from each of them, if they fail to fulfill the conditions required by the Caisse or if the amounts to be paid by the Caisse were intended to serve purposes other than those described in this application. Unless otherwise agreed with the Caisse or Federation, each of the undersigned acknowledges that they must pay all assessment or inspection fees applicable to this application, unless they are acting as guarantor herein. The Caisse may debit the account(s) of each of the undersigned to pay for such fees. CONSENT REGARDING PERSONAL INFORMATION Each of the undersigned consents to the Agency and Broker communicating the information on the Mortgage Application and this form to the Federation or Caisse so that they can evaluate the mortgage loan application. Each of the undersigned consents to the Agency, Broker, Caisse or Federation collecting from anyone, including credit rating agencies, the information requi­ red to analyze the Mortgage Application, and each of the undersigned authorizes the Agency, Broker, Caisse and Federation to use and share said informa­ tion, as well as the information on the Mortgage Application, to evaluate such application and act on it. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, each of the undersigned consents to the Agency, Broker, Caisse and Federation collecting from any personal information agent, lender, employer, financial institution and, if applicable, any other person or body holding personal information on them, including revenue agencies (hereinafter referred to as “Third Parties”), the information required regarding their creditworthiness or financial situation for the purposes of responding to the Mortgage Application, as long as each of the undersigned has a business relationship with the Caisse or Federation. Such consent also applies to information updates for the purposes of enabling the Caisse or Federation to re-examine the commitments that each undersigned has to them, particularly in connection with any resulting renewal, amendment or extension of a commitment and any change in their business relationship. Each of the undersigned acknowledges that by providing this authorization, the Federation and the Caisse may consult credit reports concerning them with personal information agents and that such consultation may affect their credit rating. Each of the undersigned acknowledges that the Caisse or Federation may request that a mortgage insurer, at any time after financing is granted, insure the financing at the Caisse’s expense. Each of the undersigned consents, in such case, to the mortgage insurer collecting from the Caisse, Federation and any personal information agent the information required to respond to the insurance application, including information on their creditworthiness or financial situation, and using it for such purpose. Each of the undersigned consents to the Third Party to which the Caisse or Federation addressed itself in any of the circumstances described above communicating such information even if it is in a closed or inactive file. If information is communicated to CMHC, each of the undersigned consents to it using such information for any purposes pertaining to underwriting loan insurance. Each of the undersigned also acknowledges that all documents retained by CMHC will be subject to Canada’s Access to Information Act and Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act. Each of the undersigned authorizes the Agency, Broker, Federation and Caisse to communicate, to anyone with whom it has entered into a promise to pur­ chase or a building purchase or construction contract, and any real estate broker or agency involved in the transaction, if applicable, the Mortgage Application information required to demonstrate compliance with certain conditions provided for in such promise or contract, and to communicate the decision of the Caisse or Federation regarding such application to anyone who is entitled to receive compensation for referring such application to the Caisse or Federation. Each of the undersigned acknowledges that the Federation and Caisse provide a record on behalf of the undersigned to enable them to obtain the financing required. Each of the undersigned acknowledges that any personal information obtained in the context of this application and contained in their file, particu­ larly information that is reproduced in the Mortgage Application, will be retained, as appropriate, on the premises or in the computer systems of the Caisse, the Federation or their agents and will be consulted by their respective employees when justified in the performance of their duties. Each of the undersigned also acknowledges that the Federation, the Caisse or their agents may do business with certain service providers to process and transmit the Mortgage Application and consents to the information in their file and the documents described above being communicated to such service pro­ viders. Each of the undersigned acknowledges that in the event such service providers are located outside Canada, such information may be shared with authorities of the country involved in accordance with applicable law. Each of the undersigned declares they have been informed of their right to access the contents of their file and have any inaccurate information corrected. The preceding consents also apply to any future application that each of the undersigned may make to reborrow the amounts repaid or obtain an increase in credit granted. Collection, use and disclosure of each of the undersigned personal information To serve each of the undersigned effectively every day and fulfill our legal obligations, we need to collect, use and disclose information about them. Each of the undersigned can read Desjardins Group’s Privacy Policy at www.desjardins.com/privacy-policy for more details. Each of the undersigned acknowledges and accepts that this consent takes precedence over any other consent each of the undersigned has previously signed. This consent remains in effect for as long as each of the undersigned maintains a business relationship with Desjardins Group. By signing this form, each of the undersigned authorizes the Caisse and Federation to collect, use and disclose their personal information in accordance with privacy regulations and with Desjardins Group’s Privacy Policy that was presented to them before signing this consent. Automated processing of the personal information may be used to make a decision about the application. Declarations and consents Mortgage Application * For use in Ontario only CF-00100-096A (ON) Page 2 of 2 2023-09 CONSENTS REGARDING OTHER PRODUCT AND SERVICE OFFERINGS By checking off “I agree” below, each of the undersigned consents to the Agency, Broker, Federation or Caisse communicating to the Desjardins Group enti­ ties identified below the information required to enable them to offer products and services likely to be of interest to each of the undersigned, such as insu­ rance (Quebec residents only), savings or credit products. For the purposes of this consent, the Desjardins Group entities involved are Fédération des caisses Desjardins du Québec, Desjardins General Insurance Inc.( Quebec residents only), Desjardins Financial Security Life Assurance Company (Quebec residents only) and the Desjardins Caisse to which the Mortgage Application will be submitted. Each of the undersigned that agrees to such communication consents to such entities contacting them at any phone number or email address that is indicated herein or on the Mortgage Application or that is provided later. This consent may be withdrawn at any time, by each of the undersigned, by contacting their Caisse. entries.text: CF-01255-022A 2021-04 Attestation Regarding a Gift Donor(s): Name Address (Hereafter the “Donor”) Beneficiary/beneficiaries: Name Address (Hereafter the “Beneficiary”) Relationship of the Donor and the Beneficiary: For the purchase of the property located at: Monetary gift Amount............................................................................................................................................................................................................................... $ Date the gift was given................................................................................................................................................................................................... Gift of equity Amount Recognized value of the residence............................................................................................................................................................................. $ Sale price........................................................................................................................................................................................................................... $ Gift of equity..................................................................................................................................................................................................................... $ (Hereafter the “Gift”) The Donor and the Beneficiary certify that: • The Gift is irrevocable and unconditional. • With respect to the Beneficiary, the Donor waives any claim of any nature, in cash, services or other, as well as any claim to reimbursement for this Gift. • All documentation required to verify the provenance of the Gift must be provided upon request to Desjardins’s authorized representative. • The Gift is not a cash rebate nor an incentive to purchase. The Donor and the Beneficiary authorize the Fédération des caisses Desjardins du Québec and any caisse to which the Beneficiary has submitted a mortgage application for the purchase of the above-described building, as well as their employees and representatives, including Desjardins mortgage representatives (hereafter “Desjardins”), to obtain from any person any information necessary to verify the existence, the value and the provenance of the Gift, including the financial institution designated hereafter: For the purpose of the above-mentioned verifications, the person to whom such a request is submitted is authorized to transmit to Desjardins all information required to confirm the existence, the value and the provenance of the Gift. The Donor and the Beneficiary also authorize Desjardins to transmit all necessary information related to the Gift to the notary or lawyer mandated for the signature of all documents required to institute mortgage financing, as well as any person whose work requires them to have this information. x x x x Date (YYYY-MM-DD) Name and address of financial institution Date (YYYY-MM-DD) Signature (Donor) Signature (Beneficiary) Signature (Donor) Signature (Beneficiary)

entries.summary: It’s an easy transition moving from Excalibur to Prime! Our Graduation Program is for your Excalibur deals that may now qualify on the Prime Side at maturity. What is the Graduation Program? Any existing Excalibur mortgage currently up for renewal that qualifies as a Prime Mortgage. Prior to the maturity of your Excalibur deal, submit the deal through your delivery system with updated credit and updated income. No new funds allowed. Existing mortgage balance only. • FCT fees paid by First National • No AVM or Appraisal fees (Paid by First National) • Minimum 3 year term for Prime mortgage NO COST TO YOUR CLIENT! FULL BROKER COMPENSATION FOR A PRIME DEAL! Our goal at First National is to make
entries.text: It’s an easy transition moving from Excalibur to Prime! Our Graduation Program is for your Excalibur deals that may now qualify on the Prime Side at maturity. What is the Graduation Program? Any existing Excalibur mortgage currently up for renewal that qualifies as a Prime Mortgage. Prior to the maturity of your Excalibur deal, submit the deal through your delivery system with updated credit and updated income. No new funds allowed. Existing mortgage balance only. • FCT fees paid by First National • No AVM or Appraisal fees (Paid by First National) • Minimum 3 year term for Prime mortgage NO COST TO YOUR CLIENT! FULL BROKER COMPENSATION FOR A PRIME DEAL! Our goal at First National is to make it as easy as possible to provide the best solutions for your clients and for our broker partners. We are your one-stop shop for everything mortgages. Contact your Manager, Business Development !

entries.text: *Please see most recent rate sheet for current Qualifying Rate Excalibur Net Worth Program EXCALIBUR Looking for a lending solution that reflects your clients’ full financial story? DESCRIPTION Our Net Worth Program is designed with a comprehensive approach, enabling high-net-worth clients to qualify based on both their income and overall net assets. At First National, we partner with you to deliver tailored, flexible solutions that support your clients’ long-term financial goals. VANCOUVER 1140 West Pender Suite 600 Vancouver, BC V6E 4G1 800.567.8711 CALGARY 800- 5th Avenue SW Suite 600 Calgary, AB T2P 3T6 888.923.9194 TORONTO 16 York Street Suite 1900 Toronto, ON M5J 0E6 800.465.0039 MONTREAL 2000 Peel Street Suite 200 Montreal, QC H3A 2W5 888.499.1733 FSRA Ontario Mortgage Brokerage License No. 10514. For other provincial license numbers, see www.firstnational.ca/licenses NWPE-0625 Loan Purpose Purchase/Refinance Maximum LTV Tiered breakdown – Loan Amount to asset %: 1. 25% Assets • LTV Maximum - 80% • TDS Maximum - 65% • Asset Requirements: 25% of the mortgage amount, excluding down payment funds (minimum $75,000) 2. 50% Assets • LTV Maximum - 65% • TDS: Maximum - 80% • Asset Requirements - 50% of the mortgage amount, excluding down payment funds (minimum $75,000) • Premium added to rate Asset measurement is made after deducting down payment requirement. Max Loan Amount $1,500,000 Minimum Beacon and Credit History 600+ Derogatory credit history to be supported with a reasonable explanation Bankruptcy – minimum 6 months past the discharge date Only one major credit occurrence (bankruptcy, consumer proposal, orderly payments of debts, etc.) Term 1, 2 and 3 year fixed terms available Max. Amortization 30 years Rate Guarantee Purchase 120 days Refinance 90 days Down Payment Minimum 20% down payment from own resources Gifted funds are not accepted Payment Frequency Weekly, bi-weekly, semi-monthly or monthly Title Insurance Title Insurance is required Appraisal AVM or Appraisals ordered from the First National approved appraisers list Borrower Qualifications Qualification will be determined using the greater of the contract interest rate plus 2% or the Qualifying rate Pre-Payment Options 15% + 15% + Double up Early Payout Greater of 3 months interest or Interest Rate Differential (IRD) Income Available to both Business for Self borrowers and salaried borrowers. Standard salaried income guidelines apply for all full- time employment Business for Self requires proof of 2 years business for self, confirmed by one of the following: • Articles of Incorporation • Valid Business License • Last two years of HST/GST returns • Commission sales letter or employment contract confirming commission arrangement Proof of income requires: • Borrower must provide a signed affidavit declaring the income used to qualify. • In addition, income must be supported by: - 12-month bank statements; and - Sales order contracts, invoices or employment contract to confirm income generated Additional notes: • Income earned from Long Term Disability and Dividends will be considered • Individuals who derive income solely from pension are not eligible • Borrowers may only have 1 Net Worth Product mortgage Property • Primary owner-occupied and primary owner- occupied with rental suite residential property located in major urban centres with demonstrated ongoing resale demand • No rental properties • Maximum 5 properties including owner-occupied • Minimum property size: • 700 sq. ft – single family dwellings • 500 sq. Ft – condominiums • Approved Lending Area List (*Certain restrictions apply) Eligible Sources of Liquid Assets Asset Requirements: • Investment Statement - Point in time Canadian investment statement dated within 30 days of approval confirming assets to qualify • Day to Day Bank Statements - Minimum 3 months confirming assets to qualify • Assets must be in the account or investment for a minimum of 30 days • Equity in existing property(s) being sold: Statement of adjustments or trust ledger to be provided Asset Exclusions: • Equity in other properties • Equity in businesses • Cash Surrender Value of life insurance policy • Locked in Retirement Account (LIRA) • Gifts • All other forms of assets (i.e. automobiles, personal effects, art, etc.

entries.text: CLIENT CONSENT – BROKER AUTHORIZATION Fax: 1-866-325-2563 | broker@firstnational.ca 2025-05 2048v5 Date: ___________________________________________________ Client Name(s): ___________________________________________________ Mortgage Property Address: ___________________________________________________ FN Mortgage Number: ___________________________________________________ Contact Phone Number: ___________________________________________________ Email Address (Optional): ___________________________________________________ MORTGAGE BROKER INFORMATION Mortgage Broker / Agent (Name(s)): ___________________________________________________ ___________________________________________________ Brokerage Name:** ___________________________________________________ Contact Phone Number: ___________________________________________________ CLIENT AUTHORIZATION (All borrowers must sign this form) Client Name: (print name) Client Signature: Client Name: (print name) Client Signature: Client Name: (print name) Client Signature: Client Name: (print name) Client Signature: ∗ The changes that may be made by my/our broker/agent will be limited to: balance requests and mortgage information statements, property tax inquiries, due date and frequency changes, payment increases and decreases, changes to bank account information for mortgage and other payments by pre-authorized debit (delivery of void cheque is required). The broker/agent shall also be authorized to obtain quotes on applicable prepayment charges and may request renewal rates on my/our behalf. ** This consent also authorizes other brokers, agents and representatives of the same brokerage (listed above) to make the above changes and receive the information described in this Consent. This consent (“Consent”) authorizes and directs First National Financial LP (“First National”) to act upon written instructions from my/our mortgage broker or agent to make changes to my/our mortgage (and without independent verification with the undersigned). I/We acknowledge and agree that in connection with the making of such changes, First National may disclose confidential mortgage and personal information to my/our mortgage broker and/or agent. ALL FIELDS BELOW MUST BE COMPLETED. Once signed and submitted to First National, this Consent will be effective until the date that a separate notice is received by First National from me/us cancelling this Consent. I/We understand that this Consent may also be cancelled by First National for any reason at any time. SIGN SIGN SIGN SIGN

entries.text: REQUEST FOR STATEMENT & AUTHORIZATION Standardized FCT Request for Statement and Authorization Version 20200715 – non PDF Page 1 of 1 Date: ______________________ Address of Other Financial Institute (OFI) OFI Name: Address: City: Province: Postal Code: Tel. No: Fax. No: Existing Mortgage Number (MANDATORY) Mortgage #: Existing Mortgage Maturity Date (if known) Maturity Date: Borrower and Property Information Borrower Name (1) Borrower Name (2) Address: City: Province: Postal Code: Tel. No: Borrower Email (1): Borrower Email (2): Purpose (PLEASE COMPLETE THE FOLLOWING) Purpose: ☐Discharge ☐Assignment/Transfer ☐Information Only Sir/Madam, I/We hereby authorize you to provide a statement to FCT for the noted mortgage/account. If this mortgage is due for renewal on or around the effective date of this statement, consider this your instruction to not renew this mortgage, pending payout of it. Should the mortgage have to be renewed, consider this your instruction to renew the mortgage only for the shortest term available that is open to prepayment unless the mortgage contract/renewal agreement provides otherwise. The above mentioned statement should reflect the outstanding principal balance; accrued interest as of the above date; any tax account debit or credit; the per diem rate of interest on such principal balance accruing from the above date; whether the loan is in good standing; and if the mortgage contains a readvanceable provision and/or if additional principal advances can be made after the date of the statement. If there are multiple products secured by the mortgage security, provide a statement for each product. Prepare the statement(s) on the basis that any allowable prepayment privilege has been applied prior to the calculation of any prepayment charges, if prepayment privileges apply to full repayments under the mortgage contract. * For assignment/transfer statements, please provide the default insurer’s reference number associated with this mortgage, if applicable. Please note: If this mortgage secures a Line of Credit or other readvanceable product, I/we hereby acknowledge that: 1. Upon receipt of this request freeze the credit limit on the product so no further credit can be extended/utilized, pending receipt of payout funds, if applicable. 2. Any and all credit lines are to be closed upon receipt of payment and a request for discharge. I/We hereby further acknowledge that in order to facilitate the payout and discharge/transfer of the mortgage/account there may be additional per diem interest charged to me/us representing the required time to deliver funds to the lending institution. I/we authorize you to release any information requested by FCT in connection with the Purpose. I/we authorize FCT to make corrections to the Purpose, to any typos hereunder, and/or to complete the Purpose portion or any other incomplete portion in order to obtain the Statement. (1) Borrower’s Signature (2) Borrower’s Signature

entries.text: Payout statement authorization (FOR DISCHARGE PURPOSES) To : Date : Telephone : ( ) - Fax : ( ) - RE : Reference number : Address : City : Postal Code : We hereby authorize and instruct you as follows: a) Please provide to the recipient mentioned below with a MORTGAGE DISCHARGE STATEMENT as of _________________________ (Payout Date); and b) Do not renew the Mortgage, as the Mortgage will be paid out under new financing. However should you have to renew the mortgage, consider this your instructions to renew the Mortgage only for a six (6) month “open” term. These instructions are to have priority over any other renewal letter, document or other instrument you may have sent to me/us. c) The above-mentioned statement should reflect the outstanding principal balance as at payout date, accrued interest as at the payout date, any debit or credit tax account balance (for mortgages), the per diem rate of interest on such principal balance accruing from the payout date, administration fees (if applicable), the date and the amount of the last payment, the payment frequency and whether the loan is in good standing. d) If this mortgage secures a combination mortgage (securing a Conventional Mortgage, Collateral Mortgage, Credit Card, Overdraft, etc.), please provide all necessary statements. e) If this mortgage secures a Line of Credit we will require that no further withdrawals may be made on the account from the date your statement is provided and that the Line of Credit be closed upon receipt of payment. We will rely on the accuracy of your statement despite any general exception or qualification in it. Please fax the discharge statement as soon as possible to: FNF Canada 1 Westmount Square, Tower 1, Suite 446 Westmount, Quebec, H3Z 2P9 Telephone: 1-866-388-4319 Fax: 1-866-374-2422 ____________________________________ _________________________________ Borrower(s) Borrower(s)

entries.text: GIFT LETTER PO Box 1160, STN TD Toronto, ON M5K 1P2 Haventreebank.com 1.855.272.0050 03/05/2024 - GiftLetter FIRST NAME INITIALS LAST NAME SIGNATURE DATE Recipient #1 FIRST NAME INITIALS LAST NAME DATE OF BIRTH HOME PHONE MOBILE PHONE RELATIONSHIP ADDRESS SOURCE OF FUNDS 1 OCCUPATION 2 SIGNATURE DATE Donor #1 This letter confirms that the undersigned is making a non-repayable financial gift of $ To: (print names of recipients) For use toward the purchase of the property located at: (print address of property) We, the undersigned recipients and donors, hereby certify that: • These funds are a genuine gift from the donors and do not have to be repaid; • We are relatives and no part of the financial gift is being provided by any third party having any direct or indirect interest in the sale of the subject property. BANK NAME DATE GIFT DEPOSITED BANK ADDRESS BANK ACCOUNT NO(S) BANK TRANSIT BANK CODE FIRST NAME INITIALS LAST NAME SIGNATURE DATE Recipient #2 Financial Institution and account number where the gift will be deposited is as follows: 1 Please be as specific as possible when explaining the source of funds 2 The occupation must clearly reflect the nature of the work and the industry in which it is performed The donor(s) may be asked to provide evidence of sufficient funds to cover the amount of the gifted funds. Acceptable documentation must show the amount of the gifted funds and includes a bank statement, investment statement, etc. SIGNATURE REQUIRED ON PAGE 2 FIRST NAME INITIALS LAST NAME DATE OF BIRTH HOME PHONE MOBILE PHONE RELATIONSHIP ADDRESS SOURCE OF FUNDS 1 OCCUPATION 2 SIGNATURE DATE Donor #2 SIGN SIGN SIGN SIGN GIFT LETTER CONSENT We collect your personal information for the purpose of validating the source of the funds provided to the Recipient and to assure ourselves that the funds constitute a gift. We collect the personal information you provide on this form and validate it by checking it against publicly available records, such as information available via internet searches and Teranet inquiries. If we request your bank statements, we may reach out to your financial institution to confirm details. We ingest your information into our loan management system and store it on our own servers as well as in cloud storage provided by third-party service providers. Note that your personal information may thus be communicated outside of your province of residence or Canada. Other categories of third parties to whom we may disclose your personal information for purposes of meeting our legal and regulatory obligations are: • Auditors • Government agencies • Regulators • Name screening service providers While you are not obliged to provide your personal information or to consent to the use of your personal information, we will not be able to accept the funds as a contribution to the Recipient’s downpayment if you do not provide your personal information, or if you withhold or withdraw your consent to the use and disclosure described herein. Note that you have a right to access and rectify the personal information we hold on you, except where the law or regulations provide for an exception to these rights. I consent to the collection, use, and disclosure of my personal information as described. I hereby confirm and agree that the collection, use, release and disclosure of the Necessary Information by Haventree Bank or by a third party constitutes the collection, use, release, and disclosure of such information, including sensitive information, with my full knowledge and consent within the meaning of the Canadian Federal Privacy Laws, as amended, as well as the applicable provincial legislation, as amended. PO Box 1160, STN TD Toronto, ON M5K 1P2 Haventreebank.com 1.855.272.0050 03/05/2024 - GiftLetter Donor’s Signature Date SIGN

entries.text: Tell us about your business We want your experience with us to be easy and FAST. Sending us this information upfront (along with the digital bank statements) should allow you to receive a faster response and it should keep our follow-up questions to a minimum. BORROWER INFORMATION Borrower Name Business Name/Operating Business Address City Province Postal Code Business Phone Number Company Website Nature of Business No of Years Self-Employed Number of Employees (if any) GST/HST # (Required if declaring income in excess of $30,000 annually) Number of Major or Long-term Contract(s) (if applicable) Business Ownership Type (select one) Sole Proprietor Partnership Corporation # Business Ownership Structure (Please outline all owners and their percentage of ownership) Name % Name % Name % Name % Tell us about your finances Most Recent - 12 months Prior - 12 months Gross Revenue or Commission $ $ Expenses Cost of Materials / Goods $ $ Subcontractors (if applicable) $ $ Salaries and Wages (excluding yours and spouse) $ $ Insurance $ $ Lease Expense or Rent $ $ Telephone/Internet/Utilies $ $ Automotive Expenses $ $ Other (if applicable, with description) $ $ $ $ $ $ Total Expenses $ $ Net Income Used for Qualifying $ $ (Gross Revenue or Commissions minus Total Expenses) ENG_TellUsAboutYourBusiness(30Apr2026) PO Box 1160, STN TD, Toronto, ON M5K 1P2 haventreebank.com Toll Free 1 855 272•0050 Tell us about your business ENG_TellUsAboutYourBusiness(30Apr2026) PO Box 1160, STN TD, Toronto, ON M5K 1P2 haventreebank.com Toll Free 1 855 272•0050 I acknowledge that Haventree Bank is relying upon the information contained in the credit application and supporting documentation, including the information on both pages of this form and my attestation below. I certify to Haventree Bank the following: The status of my personal income taxes with Canada Revenue Agency or Revenu Québec is as follows: I do not have any outstanding personal income taxes in arrears I have outstanding personal income taxes in arrears in the amount of $ I further certify the following company located at has no overdue remittances for taxes collected (GST/HST, CPP/EI) to the Canada Revenue Agency or any other tax collection agencies. The corporate taxes (GST/HST) last remittance was on A brief discussion will be required with you once we have reviewed your completed form. Please provide your preferred phone number and date/time for us to connect. Borrower Acknowledgement I HEREBY DECLARE THAT ALL INFORMATION INCLUDED IN THIS FORM IS COMPLETE AND ACCURATE. Borrower Full Name (print) Signature Date Personal Taxes Corporate Taxes Phone Number Date Hours Co-Borrower Full Name (print) Signature Date I/We certify to Haventree Bank the following: • That I/We expect the business’ profitability to continue for the foreseeable future and to the best of my/our knowledge, we do not foresee any material decline in earnings/income. • That all information provided to Haventree Bank, directly of through my representative/agent, is a true and correct representation of my financial position. • That I/We can afford to make all loan payments and perform all obligations under the mortgage agreement based on the information provided.

entries.text: PRE-AUTHORIZED DEBIT (PAD) PLAN AGREEMENT FINANCIAL INSTITUTION BRANCH ADDRESS 5 DIGIT TRANSIT # INSTITUTION # ACCOUNT # PO Box 22248, Bankers Hall Calgary, AB T2P 4J6 Haventreebank.com 1.855.272.0051 18/04/2024 - PreAuthorizedDebit_ENG to debit my/our account MORTGAGE NUMBER PAYOR NAME(S) PROPERTY ADDRESS TELEPHONE Your Authorization as Payor to Haventree Bank (“Haventree”) to Debit an Account. INSTRUCTIONS 1 Please complete all sections in order to instruct your financial institution to take payments directly from your account. 2 Please sign this Authorization, and attached Terms and Conditions. For joint accounts, all account holders must sign. 3 Return the completed and signed form (including the signed Terms and Conditions) to Haventree Bank with a blank cheque marked “VOID” or a Bank Account Confirmation form. Convenience cheques and cheques drawn on credit accounts (i.e. line of credit or major credit card) are not permitted. 4 Please ensure your account is open, has chequing privileges and the bank numbers on the void cheque are clearly displayed. PAYOR INFORMATION (PLEASE TYPE OR PRINT CLEARLY) Signature of Payor Date Signature of Payor Date PRE-AUTHORIZED DEBIT DETAILS I/We hereby authorize Please attach a VOID cheque or a Bank Acccount Confirmation Form PRE-AUTHORIZED DEBIT (PAD) PLAN AGREEMENT TERMS & CONDITIONS 18/04/2024 - PreAuthorizedDebit_ENG 1. I/We authorize Haventree and any successor or assignee of Haventree to draw funds from my/our account (the “Account”) at the Financial Institution set out in this Authorization (the “Financial Institution”) for the purpose of making regular mortgage payments as outlined in the Mortgage Commitment. I/We authorize the Financial Institution to honor and pay such Personal PADs (“the PAD”). I/We agree that any direction I/We may provide to draw a PAD and any PAD drawn in accordance with this Authorization, shall be binding on me/us as if signed by me/us, and, in the case of paper debits, as if they were cheques signed by me/us. This Authorization will remain in effect until all my/our obligations under the Mortgage have been satisfied and include payments for any renewals or amendments to the Mortgage. 2. I/We authorize Haventree to deduct regularly scheduled payments (which may be a fixed, or a variable amount), from the Account at the payment frequency selected on my/our loan. I/We agree that Haventree can deduct one-time payments such as late interest, service fees and other charges from time to time, from my/our account (or add such one-time charges to my/our next regularly scheduled payment, in accordance with the terms of my/our Mortgage. If the amount that I am/we are required to pay under my/our mortgage loan agreement with Haventree changes, this Authorization will continue to apply. 3. This Authorization is provided for the benefit of Haventree and the Financial Institution and is provided in consideration of the Financial Institution agreeing to process debits against my/our Account in accordance with this Authorization and the rules of the Canadian Payments Association as amended from time to time. 4. I/We may cancel this Authorization at any time by delivering a written notice of revocation to Haventree at least 10 days prior to the next payment date. The Authorization may also be cancelled or suspended without notice if the Financial Institution refuses PADs for any reason or if I/we are in default under the Mortgage or other agreement with Haventree. 5. I/We agree that the Financial Institution is not required to verify that any PAD has been drawn in accordance with this Authorization, including the amount, frequency and fulfillment of any purpose of any PAD. 6. I acknowledge that I/we have consented to the disclosure of the personal information that may be contained in this Authorization to such financial institutions or others where necessary to carry out the transactions contemplated by this Authorization in the Commitment Letter where I/we have been informed of Haventree Bank’s privacy practices and my rights in this regard. 7. The frequency, payment dates and amount of a PAD are set and subject to changes in accordance with Section 8, and additional fees as provided hereafter. Haventree may draw additional PADs (for example, in connection with a prepayment on my/our mortgage) where so authorized by me/us. Where a PAD has been dishonored by the Financial Institution for any reason, Haventree may re-present the dishonored PAD or draw a PAD in place of the dishonored PAD on the understanding that a return fee will be added to the amount owing on my/our Mortgage. 8. I/We understand that with respect to: a) Fixed Amount PADs - If the payment amount is not set out in the Authorization, subject to the Waiver of Notice set out below, I/We shall receive written notice from Haventree of the amount to be debited and the due date(s) of debiting, at least ten (10) calendar days before the due date of the first PAD, and that such notice shall be received every time there is a change in the amount or payment date(s); b) Sporadic PADs - The ten (10) day notice period does not apply as they will be issued in response to my/our direct action (such as, but not limited to, a telephone instruction) for amounts authorized; and c ) Variable Amount PADs - That I/We shall receive regular written notice from Haventree of the amount to be debited and the due date of debiting. WAIVER OF NOTICE; Notwithstanding the aforementioned, it may not always be feasible for Haventree Bank to give 10 calendar days’ notice given the time between a change in my/our payment amount due to payment frequency, interest rate renewal or other change and my/our first or next payment date. I/We hereby agree to waive the notice periods required for regular fixed, variable and sporadic or one time PADs. 9. I/We certify that all information provided with respect to the Account is accurate. I/We agree to inform Haventree, in writing, of any change in the Account Information provided in this Authorization at least ten (10) business days prior to the next due date of a PAD. 10. I/We have certain recourse rights if any PAD does not comply with the terms of this Authorization. For example, I/We have the right to receive reimbursement for any PAD that is not authorized or is not consistent with this PAD Agreement. I/We understand that to obtain more information on my/our recourse rights, I/ We may contact the Financial Institution or visit www.payments.ca. 11. I/We understand and agree to the terms and conditions of this PAD Authorization and guarantee that all persons whose signatures are required to sign on the Account have signed below. 12. I/We acknowledge receipt of a copy of this Authorization. PO Box 22248, Bankers Hall Calgary, AB T2P 4J6 Haventreebank.com 1.855.272.0051 Name Signature Date Name Signature Date

entries.text: PAGE 1 of 2 HTC_PREAUDEBPLAGEN_WF38858_062016 Pre-authorized Debit Plan Agreement Your Authorization as Payor to Home Trust Company (the “Payee”) to Debit an Account Instructions 1. Please complete all sections in order to instruct your financial institution to make payments directly from your account. 2. Please sign this Authorization and the Terms and Conditions attached to this document. For joint accounts, all account holders must sign if more than 1 signature is required on cheques issued against the account. 3. Return the completed form (including the signed Terms and Conditions) to the Payee with a blank cheque marked “VOID”. A convenience cheque is not permitted (cheques drawn on a credit account, i.e., line of credit or major credit card.) 4. Please ensure your account is open, has chequing privileges and the account numbers are clearly displayed. Payor Information (Please type or print clearly) Mortgage Number (If available)______________________________________ Payor Name_ ___________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________ Address________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________ Telephone______________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________ Signature of Payor _________________________________________________ Signature of Payor___________________________________________________ Date (MM/dd/yy) _ _________________________________________________ Date (MM/dd/yy) _ __________________________________________________ Pre-authorized Debit Details I hereby authorize_______________________________________________________________________________________________________________________ Financial Institution Branch Address to debit my account _____________________________________________________________________________________________________________________ 5 digit Transit # Institution # Account # for payments to Home Trust Company as detailed below: A. Mortgage – Fixed Loan For the frequency, payment date and amount set out below: ____________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________ Payment Frequency Payment Date (MM/dd/yy) Payment Amount B. Equityline Visa For the periodic payment due on the date indicated on my monthly Home Trust Equityline Visa statement: Type of PAD Agreement: c Personal/Consumer c Business YOU MUST ATTACH A VOID CHEQUE HERE Suite 2300, 145 King Street West Toronto, Ontario m5h 1j8 Telephone 416-360-4663 Toll Free 1-855-270-3630 Facsimile 416-360-0745 Website www.hometrust.ca PAGE 2 of 2 HTC_PREAUDEBPLAGEN_WF38858_062016 Pre-authorized Debit Plan Agreement Terms & Conditions 1. I authorize the Payee and any successor or assignee of the Payee to draw debits for the purpose of making regular payments with respect to one or more loans (a “PAD”) from my account (the “Account”) at the financial institution set out in this Authorization (the “Financial Institution”) and I authorize the Financial Institution to honour and pay such debits. I agree that any direction I may provide to draw a PAD and any PAD drawn in accordance with this Authorization, shall be binding on me as if signed by me, and, in the case of paper debits, as if they were cheques signed by me. 2. This Authorization is provided for the benefit of the Payee and my financial institution and is provided in consideration of my financial institution agreeing to process debits against my Account in accordance with this Agreement and the rules of the Canadian Payments Association as may be changed from time to time. 3. I may cancel this Authorization at any time by delivering a written notice of revocation to the Payee at least 10 days prior to the next payment date. This Authorization applies only to the method of payment and does not terminate or otherwise have any bearing on the mortgage, cardholder agreement or any contract between the Payee and me. 4. The Payee may cancel this Authorization at any time by sending a 30 day notice to you. The Authorization can also be cancelled or suspended without notice if the Financial Institution refuses PADs for any reason or if you are in default under the mortgage or other agreement with the Payee. 5. I agree that my Financial Institution is not required to verify that any PAD has been drawn in accordance with this Authorization, including the amount, frequency and fulfillment of any purpose of any PAD. 6. I agree that delivery of this Authorization to the Payee constitutes delivery by me to my Financial Institution. I agree that the Payee may deliver this Authorization to the Payee’s financial institution and agree to the disclosure of any personal information that may be contained in this Authorization to such financial institution or to others where necessary to carry out the transactions contemplated in this Authorization. 7. The frequency, payment dates and amount of a PAD are set and may be changed in accordance with section 8 below. The Payee may draw additional sporadic PADs (for example, in connection with a prepayment on my mortgage) where so authorized by me. Where a PAD has been dishonoured by the Financial Institution for any reason, the Payee may represent the dishonoured PAD or draw a PAD in place of the dishonoured PAD for the payment. 8. I understand that with respect to: a. fixed amount PADs, if the payment amount is not set out in the Authorization, that I shall receive written notice from the Payee of the amount to be debited and the due date(s) of debiting, at least ten (10) calendar days before the due date of the first PAD, and that such notice shall be received every time there is a change in the amount or payment date(s); b. variable amount PADs, that I shall receive regular written notice from the Payee of the amount to be debited and the due date of debiting; c. sporadic PADs, that the ten (10) day notice period does not apply as they will be issued in response to my direct action (such as, but not limited to, a telephone instruction) for amounts authorized; and d. Waiver of Notice: However, I recognize that it may not always be feasible for the Payee to give 10 calendar days notice given the time between a change in my payment amount because of a payment frequency, interest rate renewal or other change and my first or next payment date and I agree to waive any such written notice. 9. I certify that all information provided with respect to the Account is accurate. I agree to inform the Payee, in writing, of any change in the Account Information provided in this Authorization at least ten (10) business days prior to the next due date of a PAD. In the event of any such change, this Authorization shall continue in respect of any new account to be used for PADs. 10. I have certain recourse rights if any PAD does not comply with the terms of this Authorization. For example, I have the right to receive reimbursement for any PAD that is not authorized or is not consistent with this PAD Agreement. To obtain more information on my recourse rights, I may contact my Financial Institution or visit www.cdnpay.ca. 11. I understand and agree to the terms and conditions of this PAD Agreement and guarantee that all persons whose signatures are required to sign on the Account have signed below. 12. Applicable to the Province of Quebec only: It is the express wish of the parties that this Authorization and any related documents be drawn up and executed in English. Les parties convenient que la présente autorisation et tous les documents s’y ratachant solent rédigés et signés en anglais. ____________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________ Name of Account Holder Signature Date (MM/DD/YY) ____________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________ Name of Account Holder Signature Date (MM/DD/YY) Suite 2300, 145 King Street West Toronto, Ontario m5h 1j8 Telephone 416-360-4663 Toll Free 1-855-270-3630 Facsimile 416-360-0745 Website www.hometrust.ca

entries.text: Eclipse Mortgages is a division of MCAP Financial Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10600 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11790 Business For Self Product Sheet Criteria Requirements Location Urban Centres (major, medium or minor - see Eclipse Acceptable Lending Locations) Occupancy Owner-occupied or rental Loan Amount $100,000 - $1,500,000 Beacon 550 or greater Team 1, 2 or 3 year closed terms Rate Hold 60 days for a refinance 120 days for a purchase GDS/TDS 55/55 Amortization Up to 35 years Fee 1% fee Business for Self Income Requirements Require the following: • Proof of business ownership • Minimum 12 months bank statements (personal or business) • HST Number (ON) if income is greater than $30,000 • Signed declaration of income Plus one of the following: • Invoices consistent with the business • Deposits with cheque summary • Contracts signed and executed • Accountants letter verified directly Broker submission note to include • Description of the business outlining: products or services offered, tenure, business structure, number of employees, website, business address and phone number, etc. Client Interview • Eclipse to conduct a borrower interview prior to funding Loan to Value Up to 80% LTV Secondary financing permitted with a maximum combined LTV of 80% Page 1 of 2 March 2025 Eclipse Mortgages is a division of MCAP Financial Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10600 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11790 Page 2 of 2 March 2025 Business For Self Product Sheet Extra Privileges Payment Options: • Monthly, semi-monthly, bi-weekly, weekly (accelerated) Privilege Payments: • Lump sum prepayments of up to 20% of the original principal amount each year (minimum $100) • Increase of fixed payment by up to 20% Early Payout Charge: • The greater of 3 months interest, or Interest Rate Differential (IRD). IRD is calculated at our current posted rates

entries.text: REQUEST FOR STATEMENT & AUTHORIZATION Standardized FCT Request for Statement and Authorization Version 20200624v1 – non PDF Page 1 of 1 Date: ______________________ Other Financial Institution (OFI) details NOTE: a separate form to be completed for each OFI Sir/Madam, I/We hereby authorize you to provide a statement to FCT for the noted mortgage/account. If this mortgage is due for renewal on or around the effective date of this statement, consider this your instruction to not renew this mortgage, pending payout of it. Should the mortgage have to be renewed, consider this your instruction to renew the mortgage only for the shortest term available that is open to prepayment unless the mortgage contract/renewal agreement provides otherwise. The above-mentioned statement should reflect the outstanding principal balance; accrued interest as of the effective date and any tax account balance as well as the per diem rate of interest. If there are multiple products secured by the mortgage, include all balances in the statement or provide a statement for each product. Prepare the statement(s) on the basis that any allowable prepayment privilege has been applied prior to the calculation of any prepayment charges if prepayment privileges apply to full repayments under the mortgage contract. * For assignment/transfer statements, please provide the default insurer’s reference number associated with this mortgage, if applicable. Please note: If this mortgage secures a Line of Credit or other re- advanceable product, I/we hereby acknowledge that: 1. Upon receipt of this request you will freeze the credit limit on the product so no further credit can be extended/utilized, pending receipt of payout funds, if applicable. 2. Any and all credit lines will be closed upon receipt of this request and of payment in full of the mortgage. I/We hereby further acknowledge that in order to facilitate the payout and discharge/transfer of the mortgage there may be additional per diem interest charged to me/us representing the required time to deliver funds to the lending institution. I/we authorize you to release any information requested by FCT in connection with the Purpose. I/we authorize FCT to make corrections to the Purpose, to any typos hereunder, and/or to complete the Purpose portion or any other incomplete portion in order to obtain the Statement. (1) Borrower’s Signature (2) Borrower’s Signature OFI Name: Tel. #: Fax #(Mandatory): Transit #: Existing Mortgage Number (Mandatory) NOTE: if the mortgage secures multiple accounts include all account numbers Mortgage #: Existing Mortgage Maturity Date (if known) Maturity Date: Borrower and Subject Property Information Borrower 1 Name: Email: Tel. # Borrower 2 Name: Email: Tel #: Property Address Street: City: Province: Postal Code: Purpose Purpose: ☐ Discharge ☐ Assignment/Transfer ☐ Information Only

MCAP Service Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10515 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11692 service@mcap.com www.mcap.com English: 1-800-265-2624 French: 1-888-811-2529 Lock into a fixed rate now and convert to a longer closed term mortgage at any time without penalty. • Option to convert to a longer closed term at any time without penalty • With a fixed interest rate mortgage payments are guaranteed not to change for the entire length of the term • Uninsurable, insured and insurable financing available • Perfect if selling in the near future or undecided Purpose Purchase, refinance or debt consolidation, Purchase Plus Improvements Property Type Owner-occupied (maximum 2 units) Term 6 month convertible Rate See rate sheet for details Loan Amount $50,000 to $500,000 Maximum LTV • 95% for purchases • 80% for refinances Down Payment Gifted or from own resources Amortization • Minimum 5 years • Maximum 25 years Payment Options Monthly, semi-monthly, bi-weekly, weekly (accelerated) 20% + 20% Prepayment Privilege • Lump sum up to 20% of the original principal amount (minimum $100) • 20% increase in payment, incremental fixed amount can be added to principal and interest payment Early Payout • Early Payout Premium: Greater of 3 months interest or Interest Rate Differential • Reinvestment Fee: $500 • Can be converted to a longer closed term (greater than 6 months) at any time without penalty Other • Available with Home Mortgage Protection (mortgage life insurance) • Portable and assumable upon approval Borrower Qualification • Insured / Insurable / Uninsurable - All applications must qualify using the greater of contract rate + 2% or the Minimum Qualifying Rate - The maximum GDS/TDS ratios allowed is 39%/44%. - Mortgage default insurance is available for homes with a purchase price of less than $1.0 million • Minimum Credit Score of 600 required - Refinance: minimum 650 for all applicants • No bankruptcies or judgements

MCAP Service Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10515 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11692 service@mcap.com www.mcap.com English: 1-800-265-2624 French: 1-888-811-2529 Designed to help BFS borrowers obtain mortgage financing who cannot provide traditional income verification but have a proven 2yr history of managing their credit and finances responsibly. Purpose Insured/Insurable Purchases only. Client paid insurance premium applicable for LTVs above 65%. • Purchase • Ports and Replacements • Purchase Plus Improvements (Not Eligible: Pre-approvals, Secondary Homes, Small Rental Program, Transfers, Refinances, AVM) MCAP Products • Standard 5yr Fixed • 5yr VIP MPower LTV • Maximum 90% • Borrower paid default insurance is required for LTVs over 65% Property Value • Maximum: - Insured: $1,499,999 - Insurable: $999,999 Loan Amount • Minimum: $50,000 • Maximum: - Insured : $1,349,999 - Insurable </= 65% LTV: $649,999 Down Payment • From own resources • Gifted down payment permitted. Minimum 5% down payment must be from own savings. Rate Insured / Insurable rates apply (no rate premium) Qualifying Rate • All applications must qualify using the greater of contract rate +2% or the Minimum Qualifying Rate Amortization • Minimum 5yrs • Maximum 25yrs • 30 years permitted for Insured only under the following conditions: - Property is owner occupied by an immediate family member AND either: ▪Minimum one borrower is a First Time Homebuyer OR ▪New construction property Insurance Premium LTV Up to 65% 65.01% - 75% 75.01% - 80% 80.01% - 85% 85.01% - 90% Premium N/A 2.60% 3.30% 3.75% 5.85% Top-Up Premium N/A 6.50% 7.00% 7.50% 9.00% MCAP Business-for-Self (BFS) Insured/Insurable Program Page 2 of 2 February 2025 Mortgage approval is subject to our policies and guidelines. Products subject to change without notice. MCAP Service Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10515 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11692 service@mcap.com www.mcap.com English: 1-800-265-2624 French: 1-888-811-2529 Designed to help BFS borrowers obtain mortgage financing who cannot provide traditional income verification but have a proven 2yr history of managing their credit and finances responsibly. Borrower Eligibility • > 80% LTV: Minimum 650 credit score for all applicants • Minimum 680 credit score for at least 1 applicant up to 80% LTV • Borrower(s) operating a business for a minimum of 2yrs. • Maximum GDS/TDS 39% /44% • Minimum 2 trade lines with at least 2yr history • No mortgage, instalment or revolving credit delinquencies in the past 12 months • No reported mortgage defaults in the past 7yrs • No previous bankruptcy • No judgements or unpaid collections in the last 12 months • No property or income tax arrears Qualifying Income Income stated on application will undergo a Reasonability Test based on industry, length of operation and size of business. • Borrower must provide proof of self-employment for a minimum of 2 yrs. • Most recent Notice of Assessment (NOA) • Disclosure of annual gross BFS revenue, type of business and ownership structure in submission notes • Commission income NOT eligible To confirm minimum full 2yrs Self Employment • Sole Proprietorships & Partnerships - one of the following is required - Business License - GST/HST Return Summary - Accountant prepared T1 Generals and Statement of Business Activities for the last 2yrs - Audited Financial Statements for the last 2yrs prepared & signed by a Chartered Accountant • Corporations – one of the following is required: - Articles of Incorporation - Business/Corporation Search - 2 years T2 Corporation Income Tax Returns - Business License - Business Credit Report - Audited Financial Statements for the last 2yrs prepared & signed by a Chartered Accountant Note: If there is income being used from sources other than self-employment (i.e. rental, investment, pension, commission) this income needs to be confirmed via full standard documentation requirements. Property Guidelines • Full appraisal required for Insurable applications (≤65% LTV) • Readily marketable residential dwellings, located in markets with demonstrated ongoing re-sale demand • SFD = minimum 750 sq. ft. • Condo = minimum 500 sq. ft. • Maximum 4 units where 1 unit must be owner occupied

Maximum Loan to Value (LTV) Up to 95% (refer to BDM for possible exception) Borrower Qualification • Minimum 650 credit score for all applicants • GDS/TDS 39% / 44% • Insurable / Uninsurable must qualify using the greater of contract rate + 2% or the minimum qualifying rate • Existing Insured Collateral Transfers qualify using contract rate MCAP Fusion qualifying: • HELOC limit -> of HELOC contract rate + 2% or Minimum Qualifying rate • Amortizing Segment -> of contract rate + 2% or Minimum Qualifying rate − Insured collateral transfer: qualify at contract rate Collateral Transfer (Partnership with First Canadian Title) December 2024 Mortgage approval is subject to our policies and guidelines. Products subject to change without notice. MCAP Service Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10515 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11692 service@mcap.com www.mcap.com English: 1-800-265-2624 French: 1-888-811-2529 Amortization • Minimum 5 years Insured: • Maximum: Original scheduled amortization minus time elapsed since the original mortgage registration Insurable: • Maximum 25 years - Remaining amortization = Original amortization less time elapsed since original mortgage registration OR - Single charge mortgages are eligible for amortization extension to 25 years - Two separate charges being combined to one charge are subject to a blended amortization - Blended amortization calculator located in “Tools” - underwriter will confirm final amortization once file is submitted Uninsurable: • Maximum 30 years Payment Options Monthly, semi-monthly, bi-weekly, weekly (accelerated) Prepayment Privilege • Applicable to all products: - 20% lump sum allowance based on the original principal amount (minimum $100) - 20% increase in payment, incremental fixed amount can be added to principal and interest payment Conversions • VIP MPower ARM: Can convert to fixed term with no penalty (new term must be equal to or longer than remaining term) • MCAP Value Flex ARM – Can convert to MCAP Value Flex Fixed 5 year term product with no penalty • MCAP Fusion ARM - Can convert to MCAP Fusion 5 year fixed term with no penalty Early Payout • Fixed Term Penalty: - Greater of 3 months interest or IRD. After 5th anniversary, 3 mos interest only • • ARM Penalty: - 3 months interest based on greater of MCAP Prime Rate or Contract Rate • MCAP Fusion HELOC segment - no penalty NOTE: Payout of MCAP Value Flex products prior to the end of the term is allowed only upon bona fide arm’s length sale. The above penalty calculations are applicable where payout is allowed Collateral Transfer (Partnership with First Canadian Title) December 2024 Mortgage approval is subject to our policies and guidelines. Products subject to change without notice. MCAP Service Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10515 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11692 service@mcap.com www.mcap.com English: 1-800-265-2624 French: 1-888-811-2529 Documentation Fully qualified income documentation required • AVM or Appraisal - Where appraisal required, MCAP will order once signed commitment is received - MCAP will cover the cost of the AVM/appraisal for FUNDED transfer files only • Payout Authorization Form, Current Renewal Agreement, Fire Insurance particulars, Insurer Certificate Number (if applicable), & proof of municipal & school taxes are paid and up to date Fees • Discharge fee paid by MCAP up to $300 • Appraisal fee paid by MCAP for FUNDED transfer files only • FCT Legal Fees: - Paid by MCAP - reduce commission by 10 bps - Paid by client - standard commission - Fees vary by province / premium for mortgages above $750,000 • MPoints can be used to cover FCT fee • FCT Legal fee can be capitalized into loan amount (with restrictions) • Pre-payment penalties and/or fees charged by the current lender may be capitalized to the mortgage loan under the following circumstances only: - Not to exceed a maximum of $3,000 in penalties or fees, AND - Mortgage amount with capitalized Portable/Assumable Yes, subject to MCAP approval Lending Area All provinces (see separate Collateral Transfer product sheet for the province of Quebec) Broker Requirements Insurer Certificate Number (for all existing insured applications: CMHC, Sagen or Canada Guaranty) • FCT Payout Authorization Form • Request “Collateral Charge Transfer” in notes section of application • For extended amortization requests, the following must be provided UPFRONT: - Broker to request in submission notes - Broker confirmation that current mortgage is registered as a collateral charge - Broker to provide a copy of mortgage statement, current within 90 days - must include current balance and amortization Rate Buydowns • Specific to product • Allowed using commissions or MPoints (Excluding MCAP Value Flex) Collateral Transfer (Partnership with First Canadian Title) December 2024 Mortgage approval is subject to our policies and guidelines. Products subject to change without notice. MCAP Service Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10515 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11692 service@mcap.com www.mcap.com English: 1-800-265-2624 French: 1-888-811-2529 Broker Compensation Standard broker compensation applies as it relates to Standard Fixed, VIP MPower, MCAP Fusion & MCAP Value Flex mortgages Covenant Changes – Removal/Addition of a covenant • Will ONLY be permitted under the following scenarios, where there is no exchange of monies: - Removal/Addition - of a parent, child, deceased individual where title held as joint tenants or legal spouse (defined by province) and NOT as a result of a marital separation/divorce • Will NOT be permitted for marital breakdowns, removal of siblings, arms- length transactions, deceased individuals where title registered as “tenants in common” etc… • If unable to meet requirements, confirmation that covenant change has been completed prior to transfer must be obtained OR application will have to be processed as a Refinance • Additional FCT Legal Fees may apply depending on scenario & province Other • Insurable/Uninsurable - Transfer in of 2 separate charges into a first mortgage charge - both charges to be from approved Canadian financial institutions. No Privates. A small additional FCT fee will apply to discharge 2 mortgages (not available for Insured loans) • Insured ONLY - If secondary financing is currently registered against the property behind the existing mortgage, a postponement of the second mortgage is required. - If transferring to Fusion, HELOC segment may be used to payout secondary financing if available • At funding, the MCAP mortgage will payout the existing mortgage component (this may include a payment of the penalty/fees up to a maximum of $3000 if applicable) • When file is 100% complete, FCT will be instructed to obtain discharge statement and setup client signing appointment
