entries.text: MCAP is Canada’s largest, independent real estate lending company for residential mortgages and commercial mortgages & construction loans. MCAP originates and underwrites mortgages through offices across the country and employs over 1,500 people. We believe Brokers play a key role in helping borrowers get the best mortgage — one tailored to their specific needs. Purpose Purchase, Port, Replacement, Collateral Transfer, Refinance (No Pre-approvals, No Assumptions) Blended rate options not available See rate sheet for details Number of Segments in the product 2 Segment Product — a HELOC with redraw capability combined with one standard fixed or adjustable segment Revolving Segment Home Equity Line of Credit (HELOC) Amortizing Segment • 3 Year Fixed • 5 Year Fixed • 3 Year VIP M-Power ARM • 5 Year VIP M-Power ARM Charge Type / Registration Both segments combined as a 1st charge, registered as a Collateral Mortgage (registered for 100% of the appraised value) Programs Standard, Secondary Home Property Type • Owner Occupied • Partially Owner Occupied • Maximum of 4 units Loan Amount • Minimum Amortizing Segment $50,000 • Minimum HELOC Amount $15,000 • Maximum Total Combined Loan Amount: − $1,000,000 − $2,000,000 for Greater Toronto and Vancouver areas Linked Limits • The maximum HELOC credit limit cannot exceed 65% LTV at any time • CLTV up to 65%; HELOC credit limit increase equivalent to principal reduction • CLTV 65.01-80%; HELOC credit limit increase will be less than principal reduction amount based on factor determined by regulatory rules Maximum Loan-to-Value (LTV) • Maximum HELOC LTV — 65% • Maximum Combined LTV (CLTV) — 80% (including all segments) Amortization • HELOC Segment — 25 years • Amortizing Segment: − Uninsurable - 5 to 30 years − Insurable - 5 to 25 years Term • HELOC Segment — 25 Years • Amortizing Segment — 3 or 5 Years Interest Compounding Frequency • HELOC Segment- monthly • Amortizing Segment- semi-annually Interest Only Period • HELOC Segment — 25 Years • Amortizing Segment — n/a Subject to change without notice. September 2024 MCAP Service Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10515 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11692 MCAP Fusion Mortgage MCAP Fusion Mortgage is ideal for savvy homebuyers, self-employed customers and those with an eye on the future. December 2024 MCAP Service Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10515 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11692 MCAP Fusion Mortgage Borrower Qualification • Minimum credit score is 680 for primary applicant and a minimum 650 for all other applicants • GDS/TDS 39%/44% • No bankruptcy or judgments • No major derogatory credit Qualifying Rate Approved HELOC Limit: − Greater of HELOC contract rate + 2% or Minimum Qualifying rate Amortizing Segment: − Greater of mortgage contract rate + 2% or Minimum Qualifying rate − Insured collateral transfer: qualify at contract rate Business-for-self Applicants • Add-Backs and Gross Up for Limited or Incorporated companies are NOT eligible for qualifying income • Add-Backs and Gross Up for Sole Proprietorship or Partnership ARE eligible for qualifying income Guarantor Permitted based on standard policy Down Payment • Gifted or from own resources Rate Buydown Amortizing Segment only • Borrower Paid Buydown • DND - Borrower Paid Buydown • Broker Paid Buydown – Commission • MPoints - 3 and 5yr tems are eligible Payment Options Will be drawn as 2 separate PAD payments from borrowers account: • HELOC Segment — monthly • Amortizing Segment — weekly, bi-weekly, semi-monthly, monthly Pre-Prepayment Options • HELOC Segment — Fully open • Amortizing Segment — 20/20 Property Taxes • Property taxes are collected from bank account assigned to HELOC segment of the loan in combination with the monthly interest only payments, if applicable • Client also has option to pay their own property taxes through the municipality Early Payout • HELOC Segment — Fully open; no penalty • Amortizing Segment (fixed) — up to maturity date, greater of 3 months interest or IRD • Amortizing Segment (adjustable) — 3 months interest (at prevailing interest rate or MCAP Prime, whichever is greater) • Reinvestment fee applies to amortizing segment • Full early payout penalties apply if increasing either amortization or HELOC segment of the loan Conversion • HELOC Segment — no conversion • Amortizing Segment - conversions from VIP M-Power product to fixed rate options without a penalty available. If remaining term (amortization) of the HELOC segment is less than 5 years a new registration will be required Access to Funds • Borrowers can access some or all of their credit by requesting funds through their lawyer at time of closing • Borrowers can also access funds throughout their term by going online, or by contacting MCAP’s Servicing Centre to transfer funds to their designated bank account • Broker to indicate in notes if client needs Full Advance, Partial Advance or $0 Advance from the HELOC at closing Other • Please contact your Business Development Manager regarding Mortgage Life Insurance and Disability Insurance • Straight Ports or Port & Decrease available on approval • Blended rate options NOT available • Renewals - Existing MCAP Fusion clients can only renew into the MCAP Fusion 3 year or 5 year products • NOTE: If an additional mortgage is registered behind an MCAP Fusion mortgage at any time, the associated HELOC account will be frozen and no further draws will be permitted Subject to change without notice. December 2024 MCAP Service Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10515 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11692 MCAP Fusion Mortgage Broker Compensation HELOC Segment: 25 bps paid up front on full approved limit of the HELOC portion + 35 bps paid on the balance of the HELOC portion 60 days after funding as long as there is at least a $10,000 balance. No volume bonus or performance bonus paid on the HELOC. Amortizing Segment: Standard upfront commission for 3 or 5 year Fixed or VIP M-Power based on the approved loan amount for the amortizing segment + Performance bonus (paid at 5 bps if funding ratio is greater than 75%) + M-Points (5yr terms only) and Volume bonus paid based on partnership status Subject to change without notice.

Amortization • 5 years minimum • 30 years maximum Borrower Qualification • Minimum credit score of 650 for all applicants • GDS/TDS 39%/44% - Based on loan amount for ALL Components (1st Charge + MCAP Safeguard) • No bankruptcy or judgments • No major derogatory credit • Guarantors must reside in the subject property Qualification Rate The MCAP Safeguard will be qualified at the greater of: • The contract rate + 2% or • The Minimum Qualifying Rate • Current payment on MCAP 1st mortgage along with qualifying payment on MCAP Safeguard will be used to calculate GDS/TDS Home Warranty Not available on MCAP Safeguard Mortgage Mortgage Life Insurance Not available Pre-payment Privileges • Lump sum up to 20% of the original principal amount (minimum $100) • 20% increase in payment, incremental fixed amount can be added to principal and interest payment Early Payout • MCAP Safeguard Fixed: up to 5th anniversary, greater of 3 months interest or IRD • MCAP Safeguard ARM: 3 months interest (at prevailing interest rate or MCAP Prime, whichever is greater) • No Reinvestment fee for MCAP Safeguard Property Taxes • Property Taxes are collected from the bank account assigned to the 1st mortgage charge • Client also has option to pay their own property taxes through the municipality Renewals / Conversions • Existing MCAP Safeguard can renew into MCAP Safeguard Fixed or MCAP Safeguard ARM • MCAP Safeguard ARM can convert into MCAP Safeguard Fixed Assumptions • Available - must assume both the 1st and MCAP Safeguard at the same time • Assumption fee waived Portability (Existing MCAP Safeguard) • Not available for MCAP Safeguard • Can Port & Increase existing MCAP 1st Mortgage using additional funds to pay out MCAP Safeguard

entries.text: Secondary Home Program December 2024 Mortgage approval is subject to our policies and guidelines. Products subject to change without notice. MCAP Service Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10515 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11692 service@mcap.com www.mcap.com English: 1-800-265-2624 French: 1-888-811-2529 With MCAP’s Secondary Home Program, financing up to 95% loan-to-value can be obtained for the purchase of a secondary residence that will be occupied at some point during the year in addition to a current primary residence. *Please see product fact sheet for details. **Downpayment for MCAP Fusion Mortgage cannot be gifted. ***Some exceptions available for transfer ins. See Transfer Program fact sheet for details. Eligible MCAP Products* • Insured, Insurable, Uninsurable • Fixed Rate: 3-5 Year Term • VIP M-Power ARM : 3 and 5 Year Term • MCAP Value Flex Fixed/ARM • MCAP Fusion • MCAP Safeguard Purpose Purchase, Refinance, Purchase Plus Improvements, Transfer Property Type • Eligible properties must be owner-occupied (or occupied by a relative on a rent-free basis) up to a maximum of 1 unit • Property must be suitable for and available for year round occupancy, properties located on an island must have a year-round bridge or ferry access, borrower’s ability to occupy the property must not be restricted or limited at any time. • Some examples of features suitable properties typically have include: - A permanent heat source - Acceptable year-round water source - A permanent primary source of electricity, please note that generators are considered a supplementary source of electricity - Insulated for year-round occupancy - Indoor kitchen and bathroom facilities - Accessible year-round by a vehicle that is licensed for use on municipal, provincial and territorial roadways. Note: Vacation properties are eligible as long as they meet all the property requirements above. Vacation properties such as time-share interests, life leases, properties in rental pools, properties constructed for seasonal use or with seasonal access, and mobile homes are not eligible. Loan Amount • Minimum : $50,000 • Maximum : - Insured: $1,374,999 Insurable: $799,999 - Uninsurable based on maximum LTV tiers for property location and Product / Program selected Maximum Loan-to-Value (LTV) • MCAP Fusion - Maximum HELOC LTV — 65% - Combined LTV (CLTV) - 80% (including all segments) • Fixed & ARM - Purchase / Transfer — 95% LTV - Refinance — 80% LTV Down Payment** Gifted or own resources Mortgage Insurance All insured Secondary Home Mortgages must be insured through CMHC, Sagen or Canada Guaranty and approvals received prior to commitment. GDS/TDS Ratios • GDS/TDS 39%/44% Credit Score • Fixed & ARM — 600 minimum (650 - property value >= $1 million) • MCAP Fusion — Minimum credit score is 680 for primary applicant and a minimum 650 for all other applicants. • Refinance — Minimum 650 credit score for all applicants Secondary Home Program December 2024 Mortgage approval is subject to our policies and guidelines. Products subject to change without notice. MCAP Service Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10515 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11692 service@mcap.com www.mcap.com English: 1-800-265-2624 French: 1-888-811-2529 With MCAP’s Secondary Home Program, financing up to 95% loan-to-value can be obtained for the purchase of a secondary residence that will be occupied at some point during the year in addition to a current primary residence. *Please see product fact sheet for details. **Downpayment for MCAP Fusion Mortgage cannot be gifted. ***Some exceptions available for transfer ins. See Transfer Program fact sheet for details. Documentation Requirements MCAP is responsible to ensure that the property meets the general requirements and is not seasonal. Pre-approvals Not eligible for pre-approvals Maximum Amortizations *** • 30 years on uninsurable • 25 years on insured, insurable • 30 years permitted for Insured only under the following conditions: - Property is owner occupied by an immediate family member AND either: ▪Minimum one borrower is a First Time Homebuyer OR ▪New construction property Borrower Qualification • Insured, Insurable, Uninsurable - Applications must qualify using the greater of contract rate + 2% or the Minimum Qualifying Rate - Existing Insured Transfers / Collateral Transfers qualify using contract rate - The maximum GDS/TDS ratios allowed is 39%/44%. - Mortgage default insurance is available for homes with a purchase price of less than $1.5 million • Minimum Credit Score of 600 (650 - Property value > = $1 Million) - Refinance: Minimum 650 credit score for all applicants Payment Options Monthly, semi-monthly, bi-weekly, weekly 20% + 20% Prepayment Privilege • Lump sum up to 20% of the original principal amount (minimum $100) • 20% increase in payment, incremental fixed amount can be added to principal and interest payment Early Payout Early Payout Premium: Fixed Rate: - 3–5 Year - greater of 3 months interest or Interest Rate Differential • ARM: - VIP M-Power, 5 Year Term - 3 months interest based on greater of contract rate or MCAP Prime • Reinvestment Fee: - $500 in Year 1, $400 in Year 2 and $300 in Year 3 of term (excludes Value Flex and Safeguard products) • MCAP Value Flex: - Full repayment before maturity can only occur if property is sold to an unrelated purchaser at fair market value or in the event the primary borrower is deceased Other • Available with Home Mortgage Protection (mortgage life insurance) • Portable and assumable upon approval

65% • Max combined LTV (CLTV) - 80% Amortization • Minimum 5 years Insured: • Maximum: Original scheduled amortization minus time elapsed since the original mortgage registration Insurable: • Maximum 25 years - The remaining amortization is not to exceed the original amortization less time elapsed since the original mortgage registration Uninsurable: • Maximum 30 years • Minimum 5 years Insured: • Maximum: Original scheduled amortization minus time elapsed since the original mortgage registration Insurable: • Maximum 25 years - Remaining amortization = Original amortization less time elapsed since original mortgage registration OR - Single collateral charge mortgages are eligible for amortization extension to 25 years - Two separate charges being combined to one charge are subject to a blended amortization - Blended amortization calculator located in “Tools” - underwriter will confirm final amortization once file is submitted Uninsurable: • Maximum 30 years • Minimum 5 years Insured: • Maximum: Original scheduled amortization minus time elapsed since the original mortgage registration Insurable: • Maximum 25 years - Remaining amortization = Original amortization - less time elapsed since original mortgage registration OR - Single charge mortgages are eligible for amortization extension to 25 years - Two separate charges being combined to one charge are subject to a blended amortization - Blended amortization calculator located in “Tools” - underwriter will confirm final amortization once file is submitted Uninsurable: • Maximum 30 years MCAP Transfer Matrix Standard Charge Transfer Collateral Charge Transfer MCAP Fusion Transfer Minimum/Maximum Loan Amounts Minimum: • 3-5 year fixed & VIP MPower ARM - $45K • 10 year fixed, Value Flex, Second Home & Small Rental Programs - $50K Maximum: • Insured - $1,374,999 • Insurable - $799,999 • Uninsurable - $2M* *Max loan amount is subject to property location. Minimum: • $50K Maximum: • Insurable - $799,999 • Uninsurable: - $2M* GTA/GVA - $1M Rest of Canada *Max loan amount is subject to property location. Qualifying Rate Insured: • Contract Rate Insurable / Uninsurable: • Greater of contract rate + 2% or the Minimum Qualifying Rate • Insurable Loans funded prior to Nov 30, 2016 - Contract Rate can be used for 5 year fixed terms. All OTHER terms - greater of contract rate + 2% or the Minimum Qualifying Rate IInsured: • Contract Rate Insurable/Uninsurable: • HELOC limit: > of HELOC contract rate + 2% or Minimum Qualifying rate • Amortizing Segment: > of contract rate + 2% or Minimum Qualifying rate Grandfathered Property Value Rules (Value > $1M) Current Market Value > $1M: • Existing Insured - Value based on Original Purchase Price. • Insurable(Conv) - Prior to Nov 30th, 2016 - Value used based on Current Value. (example: originally purchased for $1.1M in 2015 and current value $1.2M. Will qualify as Insurable based on $1.2M). • Insurable(Conv) - After Nov 29th, 2016 - Original Purchase Price or Current Value must be less than $1M. (example: originally purchased for $900K in 2017 and current value $1M+. Will qualify as insurable based on $900k. • Loans placed as a purchase or refinance with existing lender after November 29, 2016 must meet the current B20 Insurable guidelines to obtain insurable rates OR will be treated as a refinance and Uninsurable rates will apply. Second Charge Registered Behind First Charge • Assignment in favour of MCAP in 1st position • 2nd charge not required to be postponed or discharged • Require postponement from lender holding 2nd mortgage OR • If transferring to Fusion, HELOC segment may be used to payout secondary financing if available Insurable/Uninsurable: • May combine 1st and 2nd charge with existing lenders into a new single charge with MCAP • Signing appointment may take place prior to receiving payout statement from existing lender • Second signing appointment fee may apply, should client not sign initial documents on the 1st signing *Subject to change without prior notice* December 2024 MCAP Service Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10515 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11692 Notes: Review Standard & Collateral Transfer Program sheets on Professor for FULL Program details • Close date to be a minimum 30 days from submission date • Documents to be submitted 20 business days prior to closing date • File to be “Broker Complete” 10 business days to instruct and fund Property Tax balance at transferring institution: • Tax account balance will be transferred to an MCAP tax account ONLY IF our file is set up as “Lender to Pay” for property taxes. • If file is set up as “Client to Pay” for property taxes, tax account balance with current lender will be applied to the outstanding mortgage amount. MCAP Transfer Matrix Standard Charge Transfer Collateral Charge Transfer MCAP Fusion Transfer Covenant Changes Not Available • Adding or removing covenant of borrower to an existing Standard or Collateral Charge may be available subject to approval (child, parent, spouse (including common law spouse), siblings, grandparents, grandchildren or deceased individuals where title held as joint tenants) *Conditions apply, ask your BDM for details*. • Additional FCT fees may apply. Client to pay or include in the $3,000 capitalization allowance if available. FCT/FNF Signing Appointment • Signing appointment may take place prior to receiving payout statement from existing lender • Second signing appointment fee may apply, should client not sign initial documents on the 1st signing

New application must be submitted FAQ How will my client know what interest rate you are charging? • Statements will be sent annually, however, we will also send notices within 5 weeks of every change to MCAP Prime. Rate change notices will include the: A) New Rate B) New Payment Amount C) Effective Date What happens if my client wants to lock-in? Your client can convert up to a 5yr fixed rate mortgage at no cost if the new term plus the time elapsed on the existing mortgage is equal to or greater than the original term and the mortgage is not in default. At the time of conversion, your client will get MCAP’s best published rate. Your client can lock-in one day after completion and still be offered MCAP’s best published rate. Conversion into a 7 or 10yr fixed available on an exception basis only.

Borrowers can choose either a 5 year fixed mortgage or 5 year ARM Refinance • Only available to existing MCAP Value-Flex clients • Minimum 650 credit score for all applicants • Rate premium will apply • Minimum $10K increase • MCAP Value-Flex mortgage to a NEW MCAP Value-Flex mortgage, 10% penalty reduction Early Renewal / Conversions • Early renew from an MCAP 5 year Value-Flex Fixed to an MCAP Value-Flex Fixed or ARM • Convert from an MCAP Value-Flex ARM to an MCAP 5 year Value-Flex Fixed mortgage, no penalty Early Payout • Full repayment before maturity can only occur if property is sold to an unrelated purchaser at fair market value or in the event a borrower is deceased • Early Payout Premium: Fixed: - Up to 5th anniversary - Greater of 3 months interest or Interest Rate Differential ARM: - 3 months interest penalty is the greater of the contract rate or MCAP Prime • No reinvestment fee • Only standardized penalty applies on sale, no additional penalty Other • Mortgage life insurance available • Portable and assumable upon approval • Bridge financing available • Not available for pre-approvals going live. New application must be submitted Broker Compensation Standard broker compensation applies as it relates to 5 year fixed mortgage and ARM mortgages with the exception of the following: • If borrower pays mortgage default insurance (Insured) (including existing insured transfers) - Volume bonus earned • If borrower does not pay the mortgage default insurance (Insurable) - Volume bonus not earned

entries.text: Borrowed Down Payment December 2024 Mortgage approval is subject to our policies and guidelines. Products subject to change without notice. MCAP Service Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10515 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11692 service@mcap.com www.mcap.com English: 1-800-265-2624 French: 1-888-811-2529 The Borrowed Down Payment program is ideal for home buyers that have established an excellent credit history but have not yet accumulated the required funds for a down payment towards their dream home. The program enables you to purchase a home with part, or all of the down payment originating from borrowed sources. Purpose • Insured Purchases only • Purchase Plus Improvements • Ports and Replacements Insurer Info • CMHC, Sagen or Canada Guaranty • Standard Premium: 4.50% • Top Up Premium: 6.60% Product/Term • 3-5 Year Fixed • 3 and 5 Year VIP MPower ARM Purchase Price Maximum $999,999 Loan to Value 90.01-95% LTV Minimum/Maximum Loan Amount • Minimum: $50K • Maximum: $1,374,999 Occupancy Owner Occupied or Partially Owner Occupied – maximum 2 units Amortization • Minimum 5 years • Maximum 25 years • 30 years permitted for Insured only under the following conditions: - Property is owner occupied by an immediate family member AND either: ▪Minimum one borrower is a First Time Homebuyer OR ▪New construction property Qualifying Rate Greater of the contract rate + 2% or the Benchmark Rate GDS/TDS 39%/44% Credit Requirements • Minimum 650 credit score for all applicants • Minimum 2 trade lines with at least 2-year history • No previous bankruptcy or judgements • No income tax arrears Down Payment • Any portion of the down payment may be borrowed • Repayment of borrowed funds included in TDS as per standard debt servicing requirements (see Guidelines – Debt Servicing in Professor) • Eligible sources are to be arm’s length and may include but are not limited to: - Personal loans - Lines of credit - Credit cards - Gifts from non-immediate family members • Non-arm’s length sources are ineligible and may include but are not limited to: - Loans or incentives from the builder, realtor, mortgage broker, solicitor or vendor - Any loan to the borrower that impacts the property selling price

See schedule on Pg. 3 for more details • Dentists: $175,000 • Veterinarians: $86,000 The MCAP Medical Professionals Program allows borrowers who have recently completed or are in the process of completing their residency/fellowship in the last 12 to 24 months to obtain financing for the purchase of a new home. MCAP Financial Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10515 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11692 service@mcap.com www.mcap.com English: 1-800-265-2624 French: 1-888-811-2529 Program Details Purpose Purchases, Purchase Plus Improvements, Ports and Replacements. Currently not available in Quebec. Borrower Eligibility • Non-Permanent: Foreign workers with valid Canadian employment authorization (Work Permit) in compliance with the Prohibition on the Purchase of Residential Property by Non-Canadians Act • Permanent: Verification of landed immigrant (permanent resident) status is required - Immigrated or relocated to Canada within the last 60 months - 3 months minimum full time employment in Canada (borrowers transferred under a corporate relocation are exempt) - Minimum 2 years employment history required • Individuals with diplomatic immunity are not eligible Purchase Price Maximum : • Insured - $1,499,999 • Insurable - $999,999 Minimum/Maximum Loan Amounts Minimum: • Standard Fixed/ARM - $45K • Value Flex Fixed/ARM - $50K Maximum: • Insurable - $799,999 • Insured - $1,374,999 Loan to Value • High Ratio and Insurable only • Up to 95% LTV High Ratio (Maximum 90% LTV for 3-4 Units) • Up to 80% LTV Insurable Beacon Scores Minimum of 0 or 600+ (Scores of 1 to 599 are NOT eligible.) (Borrowers without a Canadian credit history are to comply with “Alternative Sources of Credit” requirements) GDS/TDS 39%/44% (all debts outside of Canada are to be included in the ratios) Down Payment • Minimum 5% down payment from own resources or gifted from an immediate relative (i.e.: parent, sibling, grandparent, legal guardian, legal dependent, spouse). • Remainder of down payment can be gifted from a corporate subsidy. • Government grants are eligible for Permanent Residents only. Rate Qualification High Ratio / Insurable: - All applications must qualify using the greater of contract rate + 2% or the Minimum Qualifying Rate The MCAP New To Canada Program makes it possible for individuals relocating to Canada to purchase a home sooner with as little as 5% down. Through this program, MCAP helps new Canadians purchase their first home, build equity, and become economically established in Canada. Financing is available to borrowers with permanent and non-permanent residence status where there is limited Canadian credit history and where foreign credit bureaus may not be available. New To Canada Program Page 2 of 3 March 2025 Subject to change without notice. MCAP Financial Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10515 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11692 service@mcap.com www.mcap.com English: 1-800-265-2624 French: 1-888-811-2529 Program Details Amortization • Minimum 5 years • Maximum 25 years • 30 years permitted for Insured only under the following conditions: - Property is owner occupied by an immediate family member AND either: ▪Minimum one borrower is a First Time Homebuyer OR ▪New construction property Product/Term • 1 – 5 year Fixed • VIP MPower Adjustable • Value Flex Fixed/Adjustable Alternative Sources of Credit Requirements One of the following is required: • Option 1: US or UK International to have a credit bureau demonstrating a strong credit profile: • Option 2: 2 alternative sources of credit demonstrating timely payments (no arrears) for the past 12 months (from a Canadian source). The two alternative sources required are: - Rental payment history confirmed via letter from landlord with supporting bank statements (Letter from landlord indicating the following is required: name of tenant, monthly rent, length of tenancy, payment history) AND - Hydro/utilities, telephone, cable, cell phone and auto insurance to be confirmed via letter from service provider or 12 months of billing statements. • Option 3: - LTV > 90%: 12 months verifiable bank statements from a recognized Canadian financial institution or a financial institution from the country of origin confirming satisfactory savings and repayment habits - LTV </=90%: 6 months bank statements from a recognized financial institution for borrower’s primary account • Option 4: A letter of reference from the borrower’s recognized financial institution Occupancy • 1-4 Units • Owner Occupied • Partially Owner Occupied Portable / Assumable Yes (subject to MCAP approval) Payment Frequency Weekly, Bi-weekly, Semi-monthly, Monthly Prepayment 20/20 Rate Buy Down Maximum 20 bps buy down Other Regional and property type restrictions may apply, contact your BDM for details New To Canada Program Page 3 of 3 March 2025 Subject to change without notice. Please request another piece of Primary identification. Secondary Identification • A valid driver’s licence issued in Canada; • A valid driver’s licence issued in Canada; • Current Canadian Passport; • Nexus card • A Federally issued Firearms Licence • Certificate of Canadian Citizenship (containing your photograph) or Certification of Naturalization (containing your photograph); • Federally issued Permanent Resident Card; • A Certificate of Indian Status issued by the Government of Canada; • A Provincial Health Insurance Card (with photo)1; A Provincial Government issued Photo ID Card; or document or card with your signature and photograph on it issued by: • The Insurance Corporation of British Columbia; • Alberta Registries; • Saskatchewan Government Insurance; • The Department of Service Nova Scotia and Municipal Relations; • The Department of Transportation and Public Works of the Province of Prince Edward Island; • Service New Brunswick; • The Department of Government Services and Lands of the Province of Newfoundland and Labrador; • The Department of Transportation of the Northwest Territories; or • The Department of Community Government and Transportation of the Territory of Nunavut. Acceptable forms of Identification • Each Borrower, Guarantor and Signatory, where applicable, must present to you for examination: • One piece of “Primary Identification” and one piece of “Secondary Identification” or • Two pieces of “Primary Identification • No other form of identification is acceptable. All identification must be original, valid, legible and in good condition. Each Borrower, Guarantor and Signing Officer, where applicable, is required to be physically present when you ascertain his or her identity.

entries.text: Alternative closings, designed by mortgage experts What’s Platinum Services Fees • National, excluding Quebec Tips for a successful submission • How to avoid delays • Did you know? The Platinum process FAQ Why FCT Contact Platinum Services Just like you, we’re committed to your clients’ real estate journey. Support your clients’ quest for financial freedom with our alternative closing solution, Platinum Services. What’s the Platinum Services program? Our team of experts have engineered a solution that streamlines the closing process. Platinum Services ensures your clients have a simple, and reliable closing solution for their refinance, transfer and collateral switch transactions. Streamlined end-to-end solution ensures transactions are processed seamlessly. Convenient signing options allow your clients to complete their mortgage transaction from the comfort of their own home.* Our predictible pricing allows you to quote clients’ closing costs up front, which helps them manage their budget. Here are a few things brokers love about us: *In-home signing is not available in Quebec and some areas of British Columbia—the signing would take place at the office of a lawyer or notary. What’s Platinum Services Tips for a successful submission • How to avoid delays • Did you know? The Platinum process FAQ Why FCT Contact Platinum Services Fees • National, excluding Quebec Let’s be honest, nobody likes deal delays. Here are some tips to help your clients confidently navigate the process: *For transactions in Quebec, your client will not receive a welcome email. **In-home signing is not available in Quebec and some areas of British Columbia—the signing would take place at the office of a lawyer or notary. Ensure any unsecured payout statements display the full account number; this is required to pay the creditor. Are there any special instructions? Give the lender a heads up on rate expiry dates or additional information they should keep track of. For example, the client needs to sign on a specific date because they’re jetting off on an extended vacation. Check the maturity date of the existing mortgage—this way we can avoid any penalties and advise them if there are any. Nobody likes surprises when it comes to money. Don’t forget to complete the Request for Statement Authorization (RSA) and submit it together with any required documents to the lender. Refer to our RSA tip sheet, which gives you step-by-step instructions on completing the form. Complete your conditions as soon as possible—this helps us avoid delays. Some lenders require completed conditions before sending the file to the FCT remote signer/notary.** Explain our role in the transaction and an estimate of the timeline to your clients; this gives them peace of mind that they’re in the right hands. Keep an eye out for an email from us notifying you that we have received your client’s deal from the lender - make sure to provide us with the borrower’s email address so we can get started with the welcome email.* It’s helpful to let your clients know that they should expect an email from us with a safe link and they should check their junk folder just in case! What’s Platinum Services Tips for a successful submission • How to avoid delays • Did you know? The Platinum process FAQ Why FCT Contact Platinum Services Fees • National, excluding Quebec What’s Platinum Services For most refinance files, we’re able to proceed with the appointment once we’ve received the client’s response to the welcome email. For all collateral switch and most transfer files, we need the transfer statement on file and the lender must confirm before we can proceed. We send milestone emails showing the progress of your deals—if your client asks for a status, you’ll have that information right at your fingertips. In all provinces except Quebec, your client’s deal could be delayed if they don’t submit the required information on the welcome email. Tips for a successful submission • How to avoid delays • Did you know? The Platinum process FAQ Why FCT Contact Let’s ensure your clients have the Platinum Service experience they deserve. Did you know? Platinum Services Fees • National, excluding Quebec Are you ready to see how easy the Platinum process is? You’ll prepare the file like you normally would, and send it to the lender. We’ll complete your mortgage transaction in just three easy steps. 4 * If FCT receives a transfer request, and upon title search/payout statement it appears to be a collateral mortgage, we’ll contact the lender to inform them; cancel the transfer deal and move it to our refinance program to be processed once the lender instructs us to do so. ** For transactions in Quebec, the notary will reach out directly to the client to arrange the in-person signing appointment. What’s Platinum Services Tips for a successful submission • How to avoid delays • Did you know? The Platinum process FAQ Why FCT Contact Platinum Services 1 Complete searches and statement request FCT completes all necessary searches and requests the payout statement from the other financial institution, if applicable, and conducts due dilligence. If there are any title discrepancies, FCT will resolve these with the lender and the borrower(s).* Pro Tip: You’ll receive a milestone email once we receive the deal from the lender. Give your client a heads up that we have the file—that way they’ll know the process has started and they should expect to receive a welcome email from us. 2 Document signing In all provinces besides Quebec, the borrower(s) will receive a welcome email with a link to submit required information. Once we receive the submission, we determine elligibility for virtual signing. If an in-person signing is required, FCT will contact the client to arrange for a remote signing appointment.** For transactions in Quebec and some areas in British Columbia, the in-person signing appointment takes place in the office of a notary. 3 Payouts, funding and registration Once FCT receives the signed documents, we request funds from the lender and work with them to fund the mortgage and payout any secured or unsecured debts on the closing date. Upon completion, FCT registers the mortgage. Fees • National, excluding Quebec Province Refinance* Additional Fees for Mortgage Registration and Conversion Collateral Switch* Ontario $892.28 $852.28 Alberta $664.00 $5.00 per $5,000.00 $624.00 British Columbia $744.75 $704.75 Manitoba $905.00 $865.00 New Brunswick $849.00 $312.00 for properties requiring conversion $85.00 per additional PID $809.00 Newfoundland $992.00 $0.40 per $100.00 $952.00 Nova Scotia $849.00 $460.00 for properties requiring conversion $809.00 Prince Edward Island $982.00 Conventional – Variable: $0 - $9,999 – $77.25 $10,000-$19,999 – $103.00 $20,000-$49,999 – $154.55 $50,000-$99,999 – $231.80 $100,000-$149,999 – $309.10 $150,000-$199,999 – $386.40 $200,000 & over – $463.65 $942.00 Saskatchewan $700.00 $86.35 for each title over four (4) plus variable registration fees: $0- $249,999.99 - $180.00 $250,000.00 - $500,000.00 - $250.00 $500,000.01 - $750,000.00 - $500.00 $750,000.01 - $1,000,000.00 $740.00 $1,000,000.01 + - $1,000.00 $660.00 Eliminate unpredictable closing fees with Platinum Services. National, excluding Quebec *Includes applicable taxes, all discharges, cancellations, funding, and one signing appointment. Please contact your participating lender for Platinum Transfer program fees. **Except in British Columbia, where applicable premium (plus applicable taxes) will apply on mortgages over $1,000,000.00. Additional premiums apply to all mortgages over $750,000.00** Mortgage Amount Additional Premium Tax on Premium $750,000.01 to less than or equal to $1,000,000 $100.00 ON - $8.00 I NL - $9.00 | SK - $6.00 $1,000,000.01 to less than or equal to $1,500,000 $250.00 ON - $20.00 I NL - $37.50 | SK - $15.00 $1,500,000.01 to less than or equal to $2,000,000 $350.00 ON - $28.00 I NL - $52.50 | SK - $21.00 $2,000,000.01 to less than or equal to $5,000,000 $750.00 ON -$60.00 I NL - $112.50 | SK - $45.00 $5,000,000.01 and over $750.00 plus $0.65 per $1,000.00 Taxes will vary depending on province and the amount over $5,000,000.01 Have questions about our fee structure? We’ve got answers. Don’t hesitate to reach out to a business development manager today! What’s Platinum Services Tips for a successful submission • How to avoid delays • Did you know? The Platinum process Platinum Services Fees • National, excluding Quebec FAQ Why FCT Contact Got questions? We have answers. Here are some of our frequently asked questions. How can I track my deals? Stay up to date on the status of your deals with FCT Broker Tracker. This easy to use web portal allows you to track deals and connect with FCT’s service experts. Click here to get started. Are there any materials that I can give my clients to explain Platinum? Absolutely! We’ve created a brochure that explains the process and what to expect. What happens if my client’s current mortgage matures before you can close? It’s best to advise your client to renew their current mortgage into an open term. This way there’s plenty of time for you, the lender, and FCT to process the new refinance, transfer, or collateral switch. If we receive a file that can’t be completed by the maturity date, we’ll contact you and your client to ensure everyone is aware and outline what the next steps are. Can I witness the document signing instead of FCT? Due to our underwriting requirements, FCT must meet the borrower to complete the signing. You’re welcome to attend the session and answer any questions they might have about their mortgage. What happens if I try to transfer a collateral mortgage? Collateral mortgages can’t be transferred, they need to be discharged and removed from title. Many lenders today will cover the cost of a transfer for the borrower, but when it’s a refinance, they may not do this and your borrower could have unexpected fees upon closing. Unfortunately, borrowers are not always aware of how their mortgage is registered, especially with re-advanceable mortgage products and home equity lines of credit. If FCT receives a transfer request, and upon title search/payout statement it appears to be a collateral mortgage, we’ll contact the lender to inform them; cancel the transfer deal and move it to our refinance program to be processed once the lender instructs us to do so. This is important to be aware of when meeting with your clients as it could affect their closing date and costs. How are unsecured debts disbursed? We’ll pay off as many unsecured debts as we can by electronic funds transfer. If for some reason we can’t do this, we will send cheques directly to the creditor. I signed up for Broker Tracker, why can’t I see all of my deals? If the deal isn’t submitted to the lender under your name and email address, it won’t appear in your profile. This occurs most often when a deal is submitted under the principal broker name. The good news is, your principal broker can use our delegation feature/permissions, this way you’ll be included on all deals going forward. Looking for historical deals? Deals that were funded 31+ days ago won’t be accessible in your profile. How long will it take to get a secured payout statement? This varies by lender; we’ll send you an email after we request the payout statement with an estimated date of receipt. What’s Platinum Services Tips for a successful submission • How to avoid delays • Did you know? The Platinum process FAQ Why FCT Contact Platinum Services Fees • National, excluding Quebec Mortgages can be complex; processing them shouldn’t be. Top broker originated lenders choose Platinum Services for their refinance, transfer and collateral switch transactions and you should too. Working with FCT for mortgage closings means you can offer your clients: A streamlined solution for convenient closings. A predictable process and pricing for their transaction. Status updates at every milsestone as the deal is processed. Peace of mind that our team of expets will get the deal done. What’s Platinum Services Tips for a successful submission • How to avoid delays • Did you know? The Platinum process FAQ Why FCT Contact Platinum Services Fees • National, excluding Quebec Contact Ready to learn how your clients can benefit from Platinum Services? Let’s chat! Connect with one of our business development managers to get started. Have questions about an in-progress deal? We have answers. Give us a call on our broker hotline 1.855.500.3565 or email us brokerenquiries@fct.ca. Here’s a list of mortgage finance companies participating in our Platinum program. entries.text: MCAP “Peace of Mind” Cash Back - Up to 5% Cash Back Offer Insured / Insurable Cash Back Options (Based on total loan amount including insurance premiums if applicable) 1% 2% 3% and 5% Refer to 5yr Best Rate Sheet for pricing details * 5% option only available for insurable deals and existing . insured transfers up to 80% LTV Loan Amount Minimum $45,000 Maximum: Insured - $1,374,999 Insurable - $799,999 Eligibility • Maximum Purchase Price $1,499,999 • Minimum 680 Beacon for at least 1 qualifying applicant. Minimum 600 for all other applicants. • Standard 5yr Fixed and VIP MPower ARM products only. Standard commission applies. • Purchases and Transfers (Standard and Collateral) • Purchase Plus Improvements available • 5% Cash Back option for Insurable loans only. (Existing Insured Transfers also available up to 80% LTV) • Cash Back cannot be used for Down Payment • Broker buy downs from Commission and MPoints allowed • Not available for: Preapprovals, BFS Insured Program, Secondary Homes, Small Rentals, New to Canada • Blended rate options not available • Please indicate the Cash Back percentage and include “MCAP POM” in your submission notes. Payment Options Weekly, Bi-Weekly, Semi-monthly, Monthly Payout Repayment Amount as % of Cash Back Amount (this in addition to regular discharge penalty) 1st year from IAD 100% 2nd year from IAD 80% 3rd year from IAD 60% 4th year from IAD 40% 5th year from IAD 20% Offers may change or be withdrawn at any time. Applicants must meet standard lending criteria. If the mortgage is discharged, refinanced or transferred for any reason, the borrower must pay the standard prepayment penalty; greater of 3mth INT or IRD along with repaying a portion of the cash back amount. Limit one Cash Back Offer per mortgage term. Conversions, Straight Ports, Port & Decreases available upon lender approval. Not available for Assumptions, Port and Increases. All mortgages are subject to approval under MCAP Mortgages policy guidelines. February 2025 MCAP Service Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10515 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11692 MCAP “Peace of Mind” Cash Back - Up to 5% Cash Back Offer “Peace of Mind” Mortgage offered on Insured & Insurable Purchases & Transfers.

entries.summary: January 2025 www.mcap.com MCAP Service Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10515 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11692 Purchase PLUS Improvements Options Guideline/Requirements Have a client interested in making minor refurbishments to their newly purchased home? Make arrangements right now for the money they’ll need. MCAP’s Purchase Plus Improvements Option allows renovation financing for the cost of improvements to be added to any mortgage. www.mcap.com Uninsurable Insured / Insurable CMHC Insured / Insurable Sagen and Canada Guaranty Documentation • Detailed list of improvements, including a copy of contracts outlining the scope of work and cost estimates • Copy of the building permit
entries.text: January 2025 www.mcap.com MCAP Service Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10515 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11692 Purchase PLUS Improvements Options Guideline/Requirements Have a client interested in making minor refurbishments to their newly purchased home? Make arrangements right now for the money they’ll need. MCAP’s Purchase Plus Improvements Option allows renovation financing for the cost of improvements to be added to any mortgage. www.mcap.com Uninsurable Insured / Insurable CMHC Insured / Insurable Sagen and Canada Guaranty Documentation • Detailed list of improvements, including a copy of contracts outlining the scope of work and cost estimates • Copy of the building permit if improvements are structural Appraisal Requirement *Broker ordered appraisals to be ordered from MCAP’s approved list* Broker to provide appraisal with “as is” AND “improved” values. Borrower is responsible for appraisal cost. • Insurable – broker to provide appraisal with “as is” AND “improved” values. Borrower is responsible for appraisal cost. • Insured – if appraisal is required by insurer, they will order and pay for Maximum Improvement Amount Final value determination for lending amount and mortgage default insurance purposes is the LESSER of: a) The improved value of the property as determined by a full appraisal (if required) OR b) The sum of the total cost of itemized improvements added to the base purchase price Lesser of $40,000 OR 20% of the “initial” value of the property Maximum 10% of the “as improved” value Improvements can exceed $40,000 or 20% of the “initial” value of the property, as supported by the insurer Solicitor Instructions • MCAP advances the entire committed amount to the Solicitor at closing • The Solicitor is instructed to hold back the cost of the improvements until release of funds is approved by MCAP • Work must be completed within 180 days of the advance Requirements to Release Funds • Require satisfactory completion of improvements as outlined in the approval quotes. Satisfactory confirmation to be obtained as follows: — Improvements value > $15,000 and/or borrower completed work themselves – an inspection from an MCAP approved appraiser confirming all work has been completed. — Improvements value </= $15,000 – satisfactory before and after photographs and paid invoices confirming approved work is completed as outlined in the original quotes. Full appraisal may be required if documentation does not support this requirement. • Broker orders final inspection if applicable OR submits photos/invoices to be reviewed by MCAP • Cost of inspection is borrower responsibility • Progress draws not available • MCAP will instruct Solicitor to release the remaining funds to the borrower

Be sure to click “Enable Content” at the top of the page when you first open the calculator. This will ensure that the calculations perform correctly. 2. Green fields are available data entry fields. White fields are calculated fields. 3. The worksheet contains a “Clear Form” button at the top that will reset the page and clear any previously entered information. Calculating the Net Operating Income for a non-owner occupied, non-subject property 1. When calculating income for a non-owner occupied, non-subject property on the Rental tab, ensure that you have a copy of the client’s most recent T776 – Statement of Real Estate Rentals in addition to a mortgage statement for each rental property. All of the information necessary to complete the required fields on the Rental NOI worksheet can be found on these 2 documents. 2. The Gross Rent, Owner %, Expenses & NOI will auto-populate from the Rental NOI worksheet. 3. the Rental NOI worksheet allows you to enter information for up to 10 rental properties. 4. The Vacancy amount (5%) on the Rental NOI worksheet will automatically calculate based on the Gross Rents entered.

entries.text: Manulife Bank Select Pre-Authorized Debit (PAD) Agreement The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company AB0607E (11/2024) Page 1 of 2 Use this form to set up an automatic payment for your Manulife Bank Select mortgage. To set up an automatic payment: 1. Complete and sign this form, ensuring you read section 4. 2. Return this completed form AND a personalized cheque marked “VOID”. 1 Information about your Manulife Bank Select Mortgage Mortgage account number Primary account holder’s name Secondary account holder’s name Primary phone number 2 Payment Details First payment date (mmm/dd/yyyy) 3 PAD Request Agreement 4 PAD Agreement and Authorization Attach a personalized cheque, marked VOID, drawn on your account at a Canadian financial institution (hereafter referred to as “Processing Institution”). The Bank Account Holder(s) indicated on the personalized cheque (Pre-Authorized Debit form, or Bank Account Confirmation) are hereafter referred to as “the Payor”. Note: If a personalized cheque is not available, then a Pre-Authorized Debit form or Bank Account Confirmation form completed by your current financial institution is required to verify the account information. This Pre-Authorized Debit (PAD) Agreement is for Personal Purposes. The Payor acknowledges that this agreement is provided for the benefit of the Manulife Bank of Canada (“Manulife Bank”) and the Processing Institution and is provided in consideration of the Processing Institution agreeing to process debits in accordance with the rules of the Payments Canada. The Payor warrants and guarantees that all persons whose signatures are required to sign on this account have signed this agreement below. The Payor hereby authorizes Manulife Bank to draw on this account with the aforementioned Processing Institution, for the following purpose: payment of mortgage installments coming due on such day as described on the mortgage contract provided that such day is a business day. If such day does not fall on a business day, the payment will be due on the next business day. Business day means any day, other than a Saturday, Sunday or statutory holiday or a day on which Manulife Bank is not open for business. This PAD may be cancelled provided notice is received five (5) business days before the next due date of the PAD. A sample cancellation form, or further information on the Payor’s right to cancel this PAD is available from Manulife Bank or by visiting www.payments.ca. The Payor acknowledges that provision and delivery of this agreement to Manulife Bank constitutes delivery by the Payor to the Processing Institution. Any delivery of this agreement to Manulife Bank constitutes delivery by the Payor. The Payor waives any requirement under the rules of Payments Canada that Manulife Bank give prior notice of the amount and date of any debit to be made under this Agreement. The Payor waives the requirement to receive a written notice setting out the details of this Agreement at least ten (10) calendar days before the due date of the first PAD. A personalized cheque for this account has been marked “VOID” and attached to this agreement. In the event that a payment is returned non sufficient funds (“NSF”), the Payor consents to Manulife Bank processing an additional PAD in the amount of $45.00 (subject to change) representing the NSF fee. Manulife Bank will process the additional PAD within thirty (30) days from the date of the original attempted payment. The Payor undertakes to inform Manulife Bank in writing, of any change in the bank account information at least five (5) business days prior to the next due date of the PAD. The Payor acknowledges that the Processing Institution is not required to verify that a PAD has been issued in accordance with the particulars of the Pre‑Authorized Debit (PAD) Agreement including, but not limited to, the amount. The Payor acknowledges that the Processing Institution is not required to verify that any purpose of payment for which the PAD was issued has been fulfilled by Manulife Bank as a condition to honouring a PAD issued or caused to be issued by Manulife Bank on the Payor’s account. Revocation of this agreement does not terminate any contract for goods or services that exist between the Payor and Manulife Bank. The Payor’s authorization applies only to the method of payment and does not otherwise have any bearing on the contract for goods or services exchanged. The Payor hereby acknowledges its understanding, acceptance and participation in a PAD plan. The Payor has certain recourse rights if any debit does not comply with this agreement. For example, the Payor has the right to receive reimbursement for any debit that is not authorized or is not consistent with this agreement. To obtain more information on the Payor’s recourse rights, the Payor may contact Manulife Bank or visit www.payments.ca. Payment frequency (Check one): Monthly Semi-monthly (1st and 15th of each month) Bi-weekly Bi-weekly accelerated Weekly Weekly accelerated The payment amount will be disclosed on the Operating Agreement (mortgage contract). ( ) The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company AB0607E (11/2024) Page 2 of 2 4 PAD Agreement and Authorization (continued) A PAD may be disputed by a Payor under the following conditions: (i) the PAD was not drawn in accordance with the Pre-Authorized Debit (PAD) Agreement; or (ii) the authorization was revoked. The Payor, in order to be reimbursed, acknowledges that a Declaration to the effect that either (i) or (ii) took place, must be completed and presented to the branch of the Processing Institution holding the Payor’s account, up to and including ninety (90) calendar days after the date on which the PAD in dispute was posted to the Payor’s account. The Payor acknowledges that a claim on the basis that the Pre‑Authorized Debit (PAD) Agreement was revoked or any other reason, is a matter to be resolved solely between Manulife Bank and the Payor when disputing any PAD after ninety (90) calendar days. The Payor(s) hereby consent(s) to the collection, use and disclosure of any personal information for the purpose identified in this form. Unless there are contractual limitations, the personal information may be accessed or transferred within or outside Canada and may be subject to the laws of those jurisdictions. The Payor(s) may withdraw consent, subject to legal and contractual restrictions. The Payor(s) also have the right to access and correct any personal information maintained in our files. For more information, consult our Privacy Policy at Manulife Bank.ca. day of to enable Manulife Bank to take , Signature of first payor (as shown on bank statement) Date of signature (mmm/dd/yyyy) Date of signature (mmm/dd/yyyy) Signature of second payor (as shown on bank statement) The Payor’s authorization is given on the payments as per the terms of the (our) mortgage contract.

entries.summary: 1 Projected income for medical professionals This program follows our standard lending guidelines and is designed to qualify medical professionals based on a projected income. This program is available for Manulife Bank Select High Ratio Insured mortgages only. This includes purchase or renewal from another lender. entries.text: 1 Projected income for medical professionals This program follows our standard lending guidelines and is designed to qualify medical professionals based on a projected income. entries.summary: Bridge Financing Program Options Program facts Target Market • A+ clients seeking a short term loan to complete the purchase of a new residence prior to the closing of the sale of the client’s existing home. • Clients must be opening a new Manulife One or Manulife Bank Select mortgage with Manulife Bank to be eligible to apply for bridge financing. Loan to value (LTV) • The bridge loan plus the outstanding debt on the property for sale cannot exceed 100% of the value of the property. • The bridge loan amount together with the mortgage amount on the new property cannot exceed the purchase price of the new property being purchased. • Legal and land transfer costs may be added to this calculati
entries.text: Bridge Financing Program Options Program facts Target Market • A+ clients seeking a short term loan to complete the purchase of a new residence prior to the closing of the sale of the client’s existing home. • Clients must be opening a new Manulife One or Manulife Bank Select mortgage with Manulife Bank to be eligible to apply for bridge financing. Loan to value (LTV) • The bridge loan plus the outstanding debt on the property for sale cannot exceed 100% of the value of the property. • The bridge loan amount together with the mortgage amount on the new property cannot exceed the purchase price of the new property being purchased. • Legal and land transfer costs may be added to this calculation if the bridge loan amount including the legal and land transfer costs does not exceed net sale proceeds of the property for sale. Minimum borrowing limit No minimum Maximum borrowing limit • The client’s equity in the property for sale (net proceeds of the sale of the property that will be due after repayment of any existing debt on the property for sale). • Refer to Loan to value section above for additional requirements. Maximum term • 90 days • Term is tied to the closing date of the property being sold. Preferred lending areas British Columbia, Alberta, Saskatchewan, Manitoba, Ontario, and Atlantic provinces. Program is not available in Quebec. Acceptable properties • Client is required to have full ownership and title to the property being sold. • An executed unconditional Agreement and Purchase of Sale on the existing property must be in place. Ownership Single or joint Interest rate Manulife Bank Prime Rate + 2.00% Repayment • The loan is to be repaid in full upon closing of the sale of the property. • Interest is accrued monthly and payable upon closing. Minimum beacon score Please refer to the lending program that the client is applying under for minimum beacon score requirements. Debt servicing (GDS/TDS) • Refer to the lending program that the client is applying under for the purchase of their new property for debt servicing requirements. • Repayment of a bridge loan can be excluded from calculation of the TDS ratio provided: – There is a firm purchase and sale agreement for the existing property; and – The bridge loan is for a period of no longer than 90 days following the closing date of the new property being purchased. Fee $500 The fee is paid at the time of closing from the proceeds of the sale of the property. Have a question or need additional information? Contact your Business Development Manager. Call us at 1-844-239-467 or email us at brokersales@manulife.ca. The above is for information purposes only and can be changed or withdrawn at any time without notice. All applications are subject to credit approval and the home meeting Manulife Bank’s credit criteria. Manulife, Manulife Bank & Stylized M Design, and Stylized M Design are trademarks of The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company and are used by it, and by its affiliates under license. AB0794E MBPSCS6793EN GAG 06/22 AODA

entries.text: Manulife Bank Select – Standard (Conventional) insurable program Options Program facts Target Market • Available for a home purchase or transfer from another financial institution. • A+ clients must have a positive net worth and high earnings potential. • Clients who are interested in all of their mortgage debt being locked into one term. Loan to value (LTV) Up to 80% Minimum borrowing limit $100,000 (first position accounts only) Maximum borrowing limit • 80% of property value. • Property value must be <$1,000,000. (not applicable if it is a transfer from another institution and the original mortgage was funded prior to November 30, 2016) Preferred lending areas Canada-wide (excluding the Territories) Acceptable properties • Owner occupied single family dwelling (includes detached home, townhouse or condominium), duplex, triplex and fourplex. • Property must be residentially-zoned and utilized. • In-law apartments permitted. • Maximum of two residences per borrower. A third property would need to be treated under the Manulife One investment property lending program. Ownership Single or joint. Terms 3 year fixed rate (closed) or 5 year fixed rate (closed) or 5 year variable rate (closed) Interest Fixed rate mortgage – interest compounds semi-annually. Variable rate mortgage – interest compounds monthly. Rate hold Purchase transactions – 120 days Transfer/Switch from another lender – 90 days Maximum amortization Collateral Charge - Lesser of 25 years or actual remaining amortization (can extend to 25 years) Conventional Charge - Actual remaining amortization (transfers cannot exceed amortization) Repayment options • Weekly (52 payments/year) • Weekly accelerated (52 payments/year but at a higher amount than weekly) • Bi-weekly (26 payments/year) • Bi-weekly accelerated (26 payments/year but at a higher amount than weekly) • Monthly (12 payments/year) Prepayment privileges • Annual lump-sum payment(s) up to 20% of the original mortgage amount. • Increase regular mortgage payments by up to 25% annually. Insurability Insurable Message must be received from insurer Minimum credit score Primary applicant: 700 Debt servicing (GDS/TDS) 39%/44% Options Program facts Qualification Rate Greater of the insured floor rate or the contract rate excluding any interest rate buy downs, (if applicable) plus an additional 2% Property Appraisal Full property appraisal required for rural properties and certain situations. The borrower is responsible for the cost of the appraisal. Bank Account Full feature chequing account that pays a high interest rate on every dollar in the account. Fees Transfer/Switch Only: • For transfer applications the new mortgage must not be materially different from the existing mortgage (same outstanding balance and remaining amortization period). • Closing services by First Canadian Title (FCT) or Fidelity National Financial (FNF), closing services are paid for by Manulife Bank at no cost to the borrower. • Transferring of an existing mortgage to Manulife Bank will require the registration of a new collateral charge mortgage. • Prepayment penalties, or fees charged by the current lender may be capitalized to the mortgage but must meet the following requirements: • Maximum of $3,000 in penalties and/or fees, • Mortgage amount with any capitalized penalties and/or fees does not exceed the original registered mortgage amount Please refer to the Manulife Bank Select product overview for full details on Manulife Bank Select. Have a question or need additional information? Contact your Business Development Manager. Call us at 1-844-239-4677 or email us at brokersales@manulife.ca. The above is for information purposes only and is not an official credit policy. Information is subject to change or may be withdrawn at any time without notice. All applications are subject to credit approval and the home meeting Manulife Bank’s credit criteria. Manulife One is offered through Manulife Bank of Canada. entries.text: 1 Manulife One—Investment property program fact sheet Options Program facts Target market • Individuals seeking financing for their investment residential real estate • Including but not limited to a purchase, refinance, or renewal from another lender • A+ clients that have a positive net worth and high earnings potential Loan to value (LTV) 80% (75% for high rise condominiums) • Max 50% main account (revolving line of credit) • Amount above 50% non re-advanceable term sub-account • Additional sub-account options are available, including re-advancing sub-accounts for borrowings below 50%. Minimum borrowing limit $100,000 (first position accounts only) Maximum borrowing limit $750,000 per deal $1,000,000 maximum on total investment mortgage exposure Preferred lending areas Refer to the list of eligible communities on page 2. Acceptable properties • Non-owner occupied properties—includes single family dwelling (detached home, townhouse, or condominium), duplex, triplex, and fourplex • Property must be residentially-zoned and utilized. • Property must have year-round occupancy. Sub-accounts Up to five fixed-rate term sub-accounts • Amount >50% LTV must be allocated to a non re-advanceable sub-account where the client’s borrowing limit will be reduced by the amount of the monthly principal payment to the sub-account each month. • For borrowings <50% LTV, clients have the option to set up additional sub-accounts and can choose whether they would like them to be re-advanceable or non re-advanceable. Ownership Single or joint Rate type Main account – variable Fixed-rate term sub-account(s) Interest Main account – Interest is calculated daily on the closing balance and posted to the account on the last day of the month. Term sub-account – Interest is compounded semi-annually and charged monthly to the main account. Rate hold Available for fixed rate term sub-account(s) Purchase transactions – 120 days Refinance transactions – 90 days Maximum amortization 30 years Payment frequency Main account – Manulife Bank recommends depositing rental income to account to facilitate repayment. Monthly deposits to the Manulife One account should be equal to or greater than the interest payable each month. Term sub-account(s) – Monthly payment of principal and interest (charged to the main account). Minimum beacon score All provinces except Quebec Primary applicant: 700 Co-applicant: 650 Quebec Primary applicant: 750 Co-applicant: 650 2 Options Program facts Debt servicing (TDS) Max TDS 40% Rental income requirements • Net rental income for the subject property or an owner occupied rental property is calculated as Gross Rental Income x 50% • Net rental income for a non-owner occupied property and not the subject property is calculated as: gross rents less allowable operating expenses (actual expenses as noted on the T776). Net rental income forms part of the total annual income. Negative net rental income to be deducted from the total annual income Qualification rate The Net Operating Income (NOI) worksheet needs to be completed and submitted with the application. The NOI Calculator is available on the Manulife Bank Broker Portal under Quick Links. Qualifying interest rate for the main account will be the greater of the contractual mortgage rate on the main account plus an additional 2% or the uninsured floor rate. Qualifying interest rate for a sub-account will be the greater of the contractual mortgage rate on the sub-account plus an additional 2% or the uninsured floor rate. Property appraisal Full property appraisal required. The borrower is responsible for the cost of the appraisal. Debt consolidation Not permitted to meet total debt servicing requirements Monthly fee $16.95 ($9.95 for seniors age 60 and over) for unlimited transactions and sub-accounts 3 Eligible communities Greater Toronto Area (GTA) Ajax, Ancaster, Aurora, Bolton, Brampton, Burlington, Caledon, Concord, East York, Etobicoke, Georgetown, Halton Hills, King, King City, Kleinburg, Maple, Markham, Milton, Mississauga, Newmarket, Nobleton, North York, Oakville, Pickering, Richmond Hill (including Oak Ridges), Scarborough, Schomberg, Thornhill, Toronto, Vaughan, Whitby, Wichurch-Stouffville, Woodbridge Greater Vancouver Area (GVA) Ajax, Ancaster, Aurora, Bolton, Brampton, Burlington, Caledon, Concord, East York, Etobicoke, Georgetown, Halton Hills, King, King City, Kleinburg, Maple, Markham, Milton, Abbotsford, Anmore, Belcarra, Burnaby, Coquitlam, Delta, Langley, Maple Ridge, Mission, New Westminister, North Vancouver, Pitt Meadows, Port Coquitlam, Port Moody, Richmond, Surrey, Vancouver, West Vancouver, White Rock British Columbia Chilliwack Courtenay Cranbrook Kamloops Kelowna Nanaimo North Cowichan Parksville, Penticton Prince George Prince Rupert Salmon Arm Squamish Terrace Vernon Victoria Williams Lake Alberta Calgary Edmonton Fort McMurray Grande Prairie Lethbridge Medicine Hat Red Deer Saskatchewan Prince Albert Regina Saskatoon Manitoba Brandon Selkirk Steinbach Winnipeg Ontario Barrie Belleville Bracebridge Brantford Brockville Cambridge Centre Wellington Chatham-Kent Cobourg Collingwood Cornwall Elliot Lake Essa Guelph Gravenhurst Hamilton Hawkesbury Huntsville Innisfil Kawartha Lakes Kemptville Kenora Kingston Kitchener Leamington London Midland Nanticoke Norfolk North Bay Orillia Oro-Medonte Oshawa Ottawa Owen Sound Pembroke Petawawa Peterborough Port Hope Sarnia Sault Ste. entries.text: 1 Manulife One—Small Business Owner (SBO) program Options Program facts Target Market • Established small business owners seeking financing for their residential real estate • Purchase or refinance (includes renewal from another lender) • A+ client that has been in business for at least two years and has a good credit history • This program is for affluent small business owners with a sizable net worth. • This is not a stated income program. Loan to value (LTV) Up to 65% • Max 50% main account (revolving line of credit) • Amount above 50% non re-advanceable term sub-account • Additional sub-account options are available, including re-advancing sub-accounts, for borrowings below 50%. Minimum borrowing limit $100,000 (first position accounts only) Maximum borrowing limit $1 million Preferred lending areas British Columbia, Alberta, Saskatchewan, Manitoba, Ontario, Quebec, and Atlantic provinces Acceptable properties • Owner occupied single family dwelling (includes detached home, townhouse or condominium) • Property must be residentially-zoned and utilized. • In-law apartments permitted • Maximum of two residences per borrower. A third property would need to be treated under the Manulife One investment property lending program. Ownership Single or joint Sub-accounts Up to 5 fixed rate term sub-accounts • Amount >50% LTV must be allocated to a non re-advanceable sub-account where the client’s borrowing limit will be reduced by the amount of the monthly principal payment to the sub-account each month. • For borrowings <50% LTV, clients have the option to set up additional sub-accounts and can choose whether they would like them to be re-advanceable or non re-advanceable. Rate type Main account – variable Fixed rate term sub-account(s) Rate hold Available for fixed rate term sub-account(s) Purchase transactions – 120 days Refinance transactions – 90 days Maximum amortization 30 years Payment frequency Main account – Manulife Bank recommends depositing income (payroll) to the account to facilitate repayment Monthly deposits to the Manulife One account should be equal to or greater than the interest payable each month. Term sub-account(s) – Monthly payment of principal and interest (charged to the main account) Prepayment privileges Main account – Repayable at any time, no prepayment charges. Term sub-account – Pre-pay up to 20% of the original principal amount of the sub-account per year without paying a prepayment charge. Minimum beacon score Primary applicant: 700 Co-applicant: 650 These are minimums only, the deal will be underwritten as a whole factoring in all other information. Clients with higher credit scores will have a higher probability of being approved. Primary applicant is the applicant with the highest income. 2 Options Program facts Debt servicing (TDS) For an Incorporated Business: • At least one of the applicants must be self-employed and the income drawn (before any gross-ups or eligible add-backs) from the corporation or business must be a minimum of $25,000. • Two years of Financial Statements are required and cannot show a loss in the most recent year. Financial Statements need to demonstrate the company is viable and it is reasonable to expect that additional income could be withdrawn to support the debt if needed. • Personal income drawn from the corporation and claimed must be a minimum of $25,000 per year and can be any combination of salary (T4) or dividend (T5) income. For a Sole Proprietorship or Partnership • At least one of the applicants must be self-employed and the net business income claimed on the Federal NOA and T1 General must be a minimum of $25,000 (before any gross ups or eligible add-backs). (Note: in Quebec, provincial NOA must also be provided.) • Gross business income minus expenses equals Net Business Income which is located on the T1 General on Lines 135, 137, 139, 141, and 143 and must be a minimum of $25,000 for each of the past two years. The following applies to all Small Business Owner applications: • Actual, verified income is used. • Reasonability assessment should take into consideration that clients possess the means to service the debt. • If TDS > 70%, then the minimum verified net worth must be at least two times the loan amount with at least 1.2 times the loan amount in readily saleable assets, such as real estate (excluding subject property), cash, or investments. Readily saleable means that an asset could be available for debt servicing in a short time frame. Registered assets will be used as follows: • 100% of registered assets in the client’s net worth • 60% of RRSPs and RIFs in net saleable assets • 100% of RESPs, LIFs, LIRA, LRSPs, and LRIFs can be included in the net worth but cannot be used in net saleable assets. Qualification Rate Qualifying interest rate for the main account will be the greater of the contractual mortgage rate on the main account plus an additional 2% or the uninsured floor rate. Qualifying interest rate for a sub-account will be the greater of the contractual mortgage rate on the sub-account plus an additional 2% or the uninsured floor rate. Property Appraisal Full property appraisal required. The borrower is responsible for the cost of the appraisal. Debt consolidation Not permitted to meet total debt servicing requirements Monthly fee $16.95 ($9.95 for seniors, age 60 and older) for unlimited transactions and sub-accounts. Submission tips 1. Under the Employment section, select the “Self-Employed” check box. 2. In the Lender Notes, please indicate that the application is for the Manulife One Small Business Owner lending program. Have a question or need additional information? Contact your Business Development Manager. Call us at 1-844-239-4677 or email us at brokersales@manulife.ca. Manulife One is offered through Manulife Bank of Canada. Manulife, Manulife Bank, Stylized M Design, Manulife Bank & Stylized M Design, and Manulife One are trademarks of The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company and are used by Manulife Bank of Canada under license.
