entries.text: 1 Manulife One – Standard lending program This program follows our standard lending guidelines. Clients may divide their debt into as many as five fixed-term sub-accounts, each with its own term, and in any combination of re-advanceable or non re-advanceable. No property value maximum. Standard rates apply unless it is structured for preferred pricing. Preferred pricing structure (see rate sheet)—including but not limited to purchases, refinances, equity take-outs or renewal from another lender. The account must feature the following characteristics, with no exceptions: • GDS less than 39% / TDS less than 44% • Primary borrower credit score 700+ • Minimum 25% of the advanced amount must be in the main account • Minimum deal size and advanced amount $100,000 Options Program facts Target client • Homeowners, with or without a mortgage, seeking financing for their residential real estate • Including but not limited to a purchase, refinance, equity take-outs or renewal from another lender • A+ clients with a positive net worth and high earnings potential • Clients looking for financial flexibility who have stable income year over year that may fluctuate month over month Loan to value (LTV) 80% • Max 65% main account (revolving line of credit) • Amount above 65% non re-advanceable sub-account Minimum borrowing amount $100,000 (first position accounts only) Maximum borrowing amount Sliding scale applies based on location. Please refer to lending policy for more details. Maximum $3,000,000. • Greater Vancouver Area – 80% of $2.5 million + 50% of the balance (80% of the first amount ranges from $2 million to $2.5 million) • Greater Toronto Area – 80% of $2.25 million + 50% of the balance (80% of the first amount ranges from $2 million to $2.25 million) • Major urban centers – 80% of $1,750,000 + 50% of the balance Preferred lending areas British Columbia, Alberta, Saskatchewan, Manitoba, Ontario, Quebec, and Atlantic provinces Acceptable properties • Owner occupied single family dwelling (includes detached home, townhouse, or condominium), duplex, triplex, and fourplex • Property must be residentially-zoned and utilized • If a borrower has two or more mortgages with Manulife Bank, any subsequent Manulife Bank mortgages must be underwritten under the investment property lending program Ownership Single or joint Sub-accounts • Up to five fixed-rate term sub-accounts • Borrowings exceeding 65% LTV must be allocated to a non re-advanceable sub-account where the client’s borrowing limit will be reduced by the amount of the monthly principal payment to the sub-account each month. • For borrowings less than 65%, LTV clients have the option to set up additional sub-accounts and can choose whether they would like them to be re-advanceable or non re-advanceable. Rate type Main account – Manulife One base rate (variable) Term sub-account – Standard and Preferred rates available (see current rate sheet) 2 WM1125E MBPSCS01637E NJZ 01/25 AODA Options Program facts Interest Main account – Interest is calculated daily on the closing balance and posted to the account on the last day of the month. Term sub-account – Interest is compounded semi-annually and charged monthly to the main account. Rate hold Available for fixed-rate term sub-account(s) Purchase transactions – 120 days Refinance transactions – 90 days Maximum amortization 30 years Payment frequency Main account – Manulife Bank recommends depositing income (payroll) to the account to facilitate repayment. Monthly deposits to the Manulife One account should be equal or greater than the interest payable each month. Term sub-account – Monthly payment of principal and interest (charged to the main account) Prepayment privileges Main account – Repayable at any time, no prepayment charges Term sub-account – Prepay up to 20% of the original principal amount of the sub-account per year without paying a prepayment charge Minimum beacon score All provinces plus the following Quebec cities: Montreal, Quebec City, and Gatineau Primary applicant: 650 Co-applicant: 570 Quebec (except for Montreal, Quebec City, and Gatineau) Primary applicant: 680 Co-applicant: 570 Debt servicing (GDS/TDS) Beacon 650–679: 35% / 42% Beacon greater than or equal to 680: 39% / 44% Qualification rate Qualifying interest rate for the main account will be the greater of the contractual mortgage rate on the main account plus 2% or the uninsured floor rate. Qualifying interest rate for a sub-account will be the greater of the contractual mortgage rate on the sub-account plus 2% or the uninsured floor rate. Property appraisal Full property appraisal required for borrowing limits greater than or equal to $1 million, rural properties, and certain situations. The borrower is responsible for the cost of the appraisal. Debt consolidation Permitted to meet total debt servicing (TDS) requirements. The amount of debt consolidated to meet TDS requirements must be allocated to the term sub-account. For example, if $10,000 in other debt is being paid out with the Manulife One to meet debt servicing requirements, at least $10,000 must be allocated to the term sub-account. If the LTV is greater than 65% and a term sub-account is already being setup, the sub-account amount must be equal to or greater than the amount consolidated. Please refer to the Manulife One product overview for full details on how Manulife One could save your clients thousands of dollars in interest costs and help you grow your business. Have a question or need additional information? Contact your Business Development Manager. Call us at 1-844-239-4677 or email us at brokersales@manulife.ca. Manulife One is offered through Manulife Bank of Canada. Manulife, Manulife Bank, Stylized M Design, Manulife Bank & Stylized M Design, and Manulife One are trademarks of The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company and are used by Manulife Bank of Canada under license.

entries.summary: Manulife Bank Select—Insured Lender Switch Options Program facts Target Market Homeowners who would like to switch or transfer their Insured mortgage from an existing lender over to Manulife Bank Loan to value (LTV) Up to 95% single detached home, townhouse, condominium, or duplex Up to 90% triplex or fourplex Minimum borrowing limit • $100,000 • First position accounts only Preferred lending areas British Columbia, Alberta, Saskatchewan, Manitoba, Ontario, Quebec, and Atlantic provinces Acceptable properties • Owner occupied single family dwelling (includes detached home, townhouse, or condominium), duplex, triplex, and fourplex • Property must be residentially‑zoned and utilized. • In‑law
entries.text: Manulife Bank Select—Insured Lender Switch Options Program facts Target Market Homeowners who would like to switch or transfer their Insured mortgage from an existing lender over to Manulife Bank Loan to value (LTV) Up to 95% single detached home, townhouse, condominium, or duplex Up to 90% triplex or fourplex Minimum borrowing limit • $100,000 • First position accounts only Preferred lending areas British Columbia, Alberta, Saskatchewan, Manitoba, Ontario, Quebec, and Atlantic provinces Acceptable properties • Owner occupied single family dwelling (includes detached home, townhouse, or condominium), duplex, triplex, and fourplex • Property must be residentially‑zoned and utilized. • In‑law apartments permitted Ownership Single or joint Terms Three-year fixed rate (closed) or Five‑year fixed rate (closed) or five‑year variable rate (closed) Interest Fixed rate mortgage portion – interest compounds semi‑annually. Variable rate mortgage portion – interest compounds monthly. Rate hold 120 days Maximum amortization Lesser of 25 years or actual remaining amortization Repayment options • Weekly accelerated (52 payments/year but at a higher amount than weekly) • Bi‑weekly (26 payments/year) • Bi‑weekly accelerated (26 payments/year but at a higher amount than bi-weekly) • Monthly (12 payments/year) • Lump‑sum payment(s) up to 20% of the original mortgage amount, annually • Increase regular mortgage payments by up to 25% annually. Prepayment privileges • Lump‑sum payment(s) up to 20% of the original mortgage amount, annually • Increase regular mortgage payments by up to 25% annually. Minimum beacon score Primary applicant: 650 Co‑applicant: 570 Debt servicing (GDS or TDS) Beacon 650–679: 35%/42% Beacon ≥ 680: 39%/44% Qualification Rate Greater of the insured floor rate or the contract rate excluding any interest rate buydowns, (if applicable) plus an additional 2% Bank Account Full feature chequing account that pays a high interest rate on every dollar in the account Fees • Closing services by First Canadian Title (FCT) or Fidelity National Financial (FNF). Closing services are paid for by Manulife Bank at no cost to the borrower. • Transferring of an existing mortgage to Manulife Bank will require the registration of a new collateral charge mortgage. • Prepayment penalties, or fees charged by the current lender may be capitalized to the mortgage but must meet the following requirements: • Maximum of $3,000 in penalties and/or fees, • Mortgage amount with any capitalized penalties and/or fees does not exceed the original registered mortgage amount. Options Program facts Additional Details • Submit the deal to Manulife Bank as a Manulife Bank ‘Select’ product and application purpose set as “Switch or Transfer”. • If any prepayment penalties, or fees are being capitalized to the mortgage (Up to $3,000), this amount must be included in the application loan amount. • The following information is to be submitted with the application via notes: • Original or existing insurer reference number must be provided • Name of the insurer (CMHC, GE, or Canada Guaranty) Insurer account number • Previous insured mortgage advance date • Previous value or purchase price (should also be entered as the value in the deal submission) • Original and remaining amortization (Remaining amortization should also be entered as the amortization being requested for the deal) • The original loan amount + the premium amount Please refer to the Manulife Bank Select product overview for full details on how Manulife Bank Select could help you grow your business. Have a question or need additional information? Contact your local Business Development Manager. Call us at 1-844-239-4677 or email us at brokersales@manulife.ca. The above is for information purposes only and can be changed or withdrawn at any time without notice. All applications are subject to credit approval and the home meeting Manulife Bank’s credit criteria. Manulife Bank Select is offered through Manulife Bank of Canada. Manulife, Manulife Bank, Stylized M Design, Manulife Bank & Stylized M Design, and Manulife Bank Select are trademarks of The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company and are used by Manulife Bank of Canada under license. Type Restrictions 10.1 – Eligible communities ���������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������������10.1 – Eligible communities Page 3 Section 1 – Income requirements 1.1 – Evidence and verification of income Requirements for employment and income verification have been established to fully comply with regulatory requirements and to set a consistent method of both verifying income sustainability and level. Multiple Borrowers For applications with more than one borrower: • Where the income from one borrower alone is adequate to meet debt servicing requirements, it is not necessary to verify the income from the other borrowers. If income verification is not obtained for a borrower, then $0 must be recorded as the income. • If income from any other borrower is used in the ratios, then that income must also be verified. Note: It is important to note that co-borrower(s) incomes that are not included in the ratios, should not be used as a mitigant. Income verification from co-borrowers should be obtained to stay within maximum ratios. Fixed and Variable Income Sources What constitutes acceptable evidence of income, and acceptable adjustments thereto, is predicated on the type of income stream. The two most frequently encountered situations are noted below: • Fixed source: Income considered to be stable and predictable (i.e. salaried employees, pension) and as such we do not average this income. • Variable source: Income is considered less predictable, susceptible to fluctuations, and therefore requires averaging to determine qualifying income amounts (i.e. part time, seasonal, or casual employment, self-employed borrower’s claiming commission income). If income is declining from year to year, the downward trend should be accounted for (i.e. understand the trend and adjust income accordingly). For other situations, types of acceptable income or special circumstances, refer to Section 1.4. 1.2 – Fixed Income Sources Borrowers are required to provide information to confirm income and employment as outlined in Column A (Employment Verification) and Column B (Verification of Income). Items identified with an asterisk (*) are links and should be read in conjunction with the “Additional Guidelines for Fixed Income Source Employment Validation and Income Determination” later in this section. Note: T1 Generals are to be Federal versions. Income Type Column A – Employment Verification Column B – Verification of Income 3rd party employed – fixed income Permanent employment – full time or part time Paystub* or Letter of Employment (LOE)* and Verbal Verification with Employer Obtain past two years: • NOA or • T4 3rd party employed – variable income Part time or casual employment, contract work, seasonal, commissions, over time, tips, bonuses Letter of Employment (LOE)* and Paystub* and Verbal Verification with Employer Obtain two of the following (or any relevant combination) to support income for the past two years: • NOA plus corresponding T1 General • T4 or T4A • Previous year-end paystub For seasonal workers, EI benefits can be used if the benefits claimed are consistent year over year, as confirmed by the two most recent year T1 Generals or as confirmed by the two most recent year T4E’s. Sports (fixed and/or variable depending on the contract) In addition to regular requirements for 3rd party employed, a copy of the sports contract confirming length of employment. Page 4 Additional Guidelines for Variable Income Source Employment Validation and Income Determination Letter of Employment (LOE) Requirements Dated no more than 60 days from the start date of the application and including: • Employer’s name • Borrower’s name • Signed by letter issuer • Occupation • Gross annual salary • Type of employment (full time, contract, etc.) • Length of time the borrower has been an employee • Ability to earn variable income i.e. over time, bonus, car allowance Notice of Assessments (NOA’s) – dates confirming most recent year POI, Proof of income statement (option ‘C’ print may be used as an alternative to the NOA) Current tax year dictated by employment type: Fixed income, including employed by a 3rd party, pension: July 1st. Self-employed: September 1st Paystub requirements Current year-to-date, dated within 60 days from the start date of the application and displaying the following: • Employer’s name • Borrower’s name • Year-to-date earnings & deductions • Most recent pay amount • Payroll company generated statement is preferred Recently Employed – less than six months, new employer, same industry LOE must confirm no probation and most recent paystub. Recently Employed – less than 12 months, new employer, new industry LOE must confirm no probation and require most recent paystub, plus previous year T1 General and NOA. 1.3 – Variable Income Sources Borrowers are required to provide two pieces of information, one to confirm income and another to confirm employment. At least one item from each of Column A (Employment Verification) and Column B (Verification of Income) must be provided, in addition to the most recent year Notice of Assessment*. Items identified with an asterisk (*) are links and should be read in conjunction with the “Additional Guidelines for Variable Income Source Employment Validation and Income Determination” later in this section. Most recent year Notice of Assessment* is required for all applicants on all Program types. Income Type Column A – Employment Verification Column B – Verification of Income Self-employed sole proprietor – variable income* (averaging required) Business income is claimed as part of the personal income taxes in the Statement of Business Activities (T2125) or Statement of Employment Expenses (T777). • Government issued Business Number • Government confirmation of business registration • Partnership agreement • Business credit report • Active bank account statement showing the name of the business • Government issued business license Two most recent years T1 Generals plus T2125 or T777 and 2nd most recent year NOA Note: Financials cannot show a loss in the most recent year. Page 5 Income Type Column A – Employment Verification Column B – Verification of Income Self-employed incorporated – variable income* (averaging required) • Articles of incorporation • Corporate searches • Most recent year T2 Tax Return including Schedule 50 ** Shareholder information is to be provided in addition if ownership is not evident** Most recent two years business financial statements and Most recent two years NOA and Most recent two years T1 Generals Note: Financials cannot show a loss in the most recent year. Professional Corporation variable income • Articles of incorporation • Most recent two years Notice to reader Accountant prepared financial statements Additional Guidelines for Variable Income Source Employment Validation and Income Determination Notice of Assessments (NOAs) – dates confirming most recent year POI, Proof of income statement (option “C” print may be used as an alternative to the NOA) Current tax year dictated by employment type: Fixed income including employed by a 3rd party, pension: July 1st Self-employed: September 1st Self-employed sole proprietorship The lesser of the most recent year net income or the average of the last two years plus Gross up 15% when there is a difference of at least 15% between the gross and net income or Allowable addbacks averaged and added to the net income: business use of home, capital cost allowance Self-employed incorporated The lesser of the most recent year T4’ed or dividend income or the average of the last two years plus Allowable addbacks averaged and added to the T4’ed or dividend income: amortization and depreciation 1.4 – Other Situations and Income types The following alternate sources of income may be considered. Where any of the following income types are included in the application, criteria outlined in the Eligibility Requirements column must be met using the Required Supporting Documentation as evidence, in addition to the most recent year Notice of Assessment.* Items identified with an asterisk (*) are links and should be read in conjunction with the “Additional Guidelines for Other Situations and Income Types Determination” later in this section. Most recent year Notice of Assessment is required for all applicants on all file types. Income Type Eligibility Requirements Required Supporting Documentation Child Tax Benefit ** Note: Child Tax Credit may not be greater than 25% of employment income. Proof that children are < 15 years old ** Note: Benefit must continue for a minimum of three years beyond application to qualify, to a maximum age of 18. Verification of benefit amount by most recent Annual Benefit Statement Disability/Structured Settlements – fixed income (no averaging) Letter of Entitlement from insurer confirming period, amount, and when applicable, expected return to work date. 12 months Bank Statements confirming deposits or other official receipt. If income is non-taxable*, then T1 General is required. Page 6 Income Type Eligibility Requirements Required Supporting Documentation Foster Care Income*– fixed income (no averaging) – Applicant(s) must reside in subject property ** Note: Foster Care Income may not be greater than 25% of employment income Confirmation from Foster Care Agency confirming borrower(s) have been employed for at least two years, amount of income earned previously and current number of children (not to exceed six, including own children) along with current income earned Most recent paystub and 12 months of bank statements showing deposits Investment Income* – variable • Investment Income from registered investments (RSPs) is to be excluded for borrowers aged < 50 years old. • For borrowers 50 years and older, eligible RSP income is limited to income reported on Line 12900 of the T1 or T4RSP slips. • Statement of RRIF income (if applicable) • Non-RRSP investment income may otherwise be included subject to receipt of a copy of recent investment portfolio (less than four weeks old) and two years of investment income as reported on current T-1 or other slips. • Investment income is allowable only for loans with LTV of ≤ 80%. • RESPs are excluded from the investment income calculation. Investment statement 2nd most recent NOA plus two years most recent T1 Generals or two most recent years T5s or two most recent years T4RIFs Maternity or Parental Leave – fixed income • Letter from employer confirming return to work date and income upon return. • If there is no set return to work date, use 60% of previously earned income. Bank deposit or other receipt confirming EI and employer top ups Pension – fixed income In addition to the most recent year NOA, one of the following: • Most recent T4A • Three months bank statements • Most recent pension stub or statement showing the pension amount • Letter of Entitlement stating pension amount Rental Income – fixed income Current lease agreement confirming annual lease (not monthly or vacation) and rental amount paid (preferred document) Where lease agreement not available, underwriter may consider a minimum of three months current statements confirming rental deposits ongoing. Most recent T1 Generals and T776 (Statement of Real Estate Rentals) and Mortgage Statement for subject property Page 7 Income Type Eligibility Requirements Required Supporting Documentation Rental income – fixed income Non-owner occupied – apply the Net Operating Income calculations Current lease agreement confirming annual lease (not monthly or vacation) and rental amount paid (preferred document) Where not available, underwriter may consider minimum of 3 months bank statements confirming rental deposits Most recent T1 Generals and T776 (Statement of Real Estate Rentals) and Mortgage Statement for subject property and Property tax statement for rental property Support or Alimony Payments – fixed income Fully Executed Separation Agreement or Court Order detailing support amounts Benefit must continue for a minimum of 3 years beyond application to qualify, to a maximum age of 18 Most recent T1 General if income is being claimed or 12 months of Bank Statements showing support deposits US Income (fixed or variable income depending on the role) Apply same guidelines as 3rd party employed replacing CRA documents with the following: W2 Wage and Tax Statement and Tax Return Transcript If US income is current and to be used for debt servicing calculations, apply the Bank of Canada monthly average exchange rate for the month of the application. Additional Guidelines for Variable Income Source Employment Validation and Income Determination Foster Care Income Borrowers must reside in subject property. Maximum 90% LTV when income exceeds 50% of total qualifying income. Investment Income Investment statement no older than 30 days from start date of application Non-taxable income Income < $30,000 gross up 25%,

entries.text: Purchase Plus Improvements Program Manulife Bank now offers a Purchase Plus Improvements program for High Ratio Manulife Bank Select. If you have first-time homebuyers with less than 20% of the purchase price of the home for a down payment who are also looking to make improvements to their new home, introduce them to Manulife Bank Select. The criteria detailed below for this program is in addition to the Manulife Bank Select High Ratio lending program. For full details, please refer to the High Ratio Lending Program overview document on the Broker Portal located under the Programs & Policies tab. Options Program facts Lending value Based on the lesser of the improved property value or the purchase price plus costs associated with the improvements Maximum renovation costs Maximum Renovation Costs eligible beyond the purchase price must be supported by cost estimates disclosed, and approved, by the mortgage insurer Down payment • Requirements are based on the improved property value. • The improved value is used to determine the maximum loan amount. Requirements • Copies of the cost estimates of proposed improvements • Holdback to be held by a lawyer and will be released once an inspection report confirms the improvements have been completed. Property appraisal • Inspection report is required. The borrower is responsible for the inspection report costs. Submitting a deal When submitting an application for a Purchase Plus Improvement deal on D+H Expert, you will need to select “Purchase + Improvements” from the “Application Purpose” drop-down menu. In the Lender Notes section, please include details of the improvements (i.e. bathroom, flooring, etc.) as well as the cost. Submit the application and upload the quote for the proposed improvements to the Broker Portal. We’re here to help. If you need assistance or require further information, please contact your Manulife Bank Business Development Manager directly. Call us at 1-844-239-4677 or email us at brokersales@manulife.ca. entries.name: manulife-bank-sbo-submission-checklist-incorporated-business-wm1161e.pdf
entries.summary: 1 Small Business Owner lending program – submission checklist for an incorporated business Program prerequisites • Maximum borrowing limit of $1 million • Loan to value (LTV) cannot exceed 65%. Any amount over 50% LTV must be allocated to a non re-advanceable sub-account. • Maximum of 2 residences per borrower under this program • Borrower must be in business for a minimum of 2 years. • Full appraisal is required. • Borrower cannot have Income Tax arrears. • Minimum income requirement of $25,000 • If Total Debt Service (TDS) is >70%, the Net Worth of the borrower must meet the asset validation requirements for the program. • Verification of Net Worth must be at least 2 times the loan amount,
entries.text: 1 Small Business Owner lending program – submission checklist for an incorporated business Program prerequisites • Maximum borrowing limit of $1 million • Loan to value (LTV) cannot exceed 65%. Any amount over 50% LTV must be allocated to a non re-advanceable sub-account. • Maximum of 2 residences per borrower under this program • Borrower must be in business for a minimum of 2 years. • Full appraisal is required. • Borrower cannot have Income Tax arrears. • Minimum income requirement of $25,000 • If Total Debt Service (TDS) is >70%, the Net Worth of the borrower must meet the asset validation requirements for the program. • Verification of Net Worth must be at least 2 times the loan amount, with at least 1.2 times the loan amount in readily saleable assets, such as real estate (excluding subject property), cash, or investment. Registered assets will be used as follows: bullet 100% of registered assets in the client’s Net Worth bullet 60% of RRSPs and RIFs in Net Saleable Assets bullet RESPs cannot be used in Net Saleable Assets • Reasonability assessment should take into consideration that clients possess the means to service the debt. When submitting deals under Manulife Bank’s Small Business Owner Program (SBO), a completed list of the documents is key to ensure a smooth underwriting process. On the next page is a checklist of the required documents that must accompany your deal at the time of submission. 2 Corporations checklist: Personal & Business documentation and credit requirements The Lender requires satisfactory copies of: The last 2 years Personal Notice of Assessments (NOAs) Complete matching 2 years T1 Generals and 2 years Financial Statements (third party prepared) The Lender will utilize the 2-year average or the most recent year NOA, whichever amount is lower. If the current year is substantially higher (more than 20%), the Lender will use the lower and gross up by 10% if needed. Please note, should the Notice of Assessments show tax arrears, the Lender requires confirmation prior to advance that the tax arrears have been paid in full from a non-borrowed source. Two years of Financial Statements are required and cannot show a loss in the most recent year. Financial Statements need to demonstrate that the company is viable and it is reasonable to expect that additional income could be withdrawn to support the debt if needed. Personal income drawn from the corporation and claimed must be a minimum of $25,000 per year and can be any combination of salary (T4) or dividend (T5) income. The Lender requires appropriate documentation evidencing a minimum of 2 full years continuous. Validation of ownership The Lender requires any 1 of the following: Company’s latest Financial Statement Copy of articles of incorporation (provides number of shares at incorporation and jurisdiction of incorporation) Corporate Searches Most recent Business T2 Tax Return, including Schedule 50 Shareholder Information is to be provided in addition to one of the above if ownership is not evident in the above. Full asset disclosure A complete list of all assets owned by the borrower needs to be included on the application. We will only condition for proof if the GDS/TDS exceeds 70%. The above is for information purposes only and can be changed or withdrawn at any time without notice. All applications are subject to credit approval and the home meeting Manulife Bank’s credit criteria. Manulife One and Manulife Bank Select are offered through Manulife Bank of Canada. Manulife, Manulife Bank, Stylized M Design, and Manulife Bank & Stylized M Design are trademarks of The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company and are used by Manulife Bank of Canada under license. WM1161E MBPSCS01529EN JAC 11/2024 AODA

entries.text: 1 Small Business Owner lending program – submission checklist for Sole Proprietorship and Partnership Program prerequisites • Maximum borrowing limit of $1 million • Loan to value (LTV) cannot exceed 65%. Any amount over 50% LTV must be allocated to a non re-advanceable sub-account. • Maximum of 2 residences per borrower under this program • Borrower must be in business for a minimum of 2 years. • Full appraisal is required. • Borrower cannot have Income Tax arrears. • Minimum income requirement of $25,000 • If Total Debt Service (TDS) is >70%, the Net Worth of the borrower must meet the asset validation requirements for the program. • Verification of Net Worth must be at least 2 times the loan amount, with at least 1.2 times the loan amount in readily saleable assets, such as real estate (excluding subject property), cash, or investment. Registered assets will be used as follows: bullet 100% of registered assets in the client’s Net Worth bullet 60% of RRSPs and RIFs in Net Saleable Assets bullet RESPs cannot be used in Net Saleable Assets • Reasonability assessment should take into consideration that clients possess the means to service the debt. When submitting deals under Manulife Bank’s Small Business Owner Program (SBO), a completed list of the documents is key to ensure a smooth underwriting process. On the next page is a checklist of the required documents that must accompany your deal at the time of submission. 2 Sole Proprietorship/Partnership checklist: Personal & Business documentation and credit requirements The Lender requires satisfactory copies of: The last 2 years Personal Notice of Assessments and Complete matching 2 years T1 Generals (all Pages) For calculation of income, average of most recent 2 years income plus either: a. grossing up eligible income by 15% or b. applying eligible add-backs. Gross Business Income minus expenses equals Net Business Income which is located on the T1 General on Lines 135, 137, 139, 141, and 143 and must be a minimum of $25,000. Please note, should the Notice of Assessments show tax arrears, the Lender requires confirmation prior to advance that the tax arrears have been paid in full from a non-borrowed source. For verification of eligible add-backs: • Depreciation • Capital cost allowance • Business use of home expenses A two-year average of consistent add backs would be used: Statement of Business Activity (T2125) or Statement of Employment Expenses (T777) Validation of ownership The Lender requires any 1 of the following: Government confirmation of business registration, business credit report, or partnership agreement Active bank account statement in the name of the business T1 General (first 4 pages) Government issued business license Full asset disclosure A complete list of all assets owned by the borrower needs to be included on the application. We will only condition for proof if the GDS/TDS exceeds 70%. The above is for information purposes only and can be changed or withdrawn at any time without notice. All applications are subject to credit approval and the home meeting Manulife Bank’s credit criteria. Manulife One and Manulife Bank Select are offered through Manulife Bank of Canada. Manulife, Manulife Bank, Stylized M Design, and Manulife Bank & Stylized M Design are trademarks of The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company and are used by Manulife Bank of Canada under license. entries.text: For more products and programs available at MERIX Financial, and for real time access to your deals and conditions status updates, log in at: explore.merixfinancial.com/login www.merixfinancial.com 1-866-809-5800 Page 1 of 2 December 2024 Subject to change at anytime without prior notice. ​Paradigm Quest Inc. operating as MERIX Financial Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #11017 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11725 Product Detail Product Description The Adjustable Rate Mortgage (ARM) is a closed mortgage that offers your borrowers peace of mind knowing that they can convert to a Fixed Rate Mortgage at any time during the term. Purpose • Insured, Insurable, Uninsurable • Purchase, Refinance, Purchase Plus Improvements, Switch/Transfers, Collateral Switch/Transfers, Business for Self ALT-A Property Type • Owner-occupied (maximum 4 units) • MERIX Small Rental Program (maximum 4 units). 5 year term only. • Second Homes (maximum 1 unit) Term • 5 Year closed • 3 Year closed Rate • 120 Day Rate Hold • Based on current Prime Rate of MERIX — Payments are adjusted with MERIX’s prime rate changes to ensure amortization remains unchanged. — Homeowners will be notified of any interest rate changes in advance. • Interest is compounded semi-annually Maximum LTV • 95% for 1-2 unit owner occupied properties • 90% for 3-4 unit owner occupied properties • 80% for 2-4 unit rental properties • 80% for 1-4 unit rental properties Minimum Loan Amount • $45K • Business for Self ALT-A, Second Home & Rentals Programs: $50K Maximum Loan Amount • Insured: $1,374,999 • Insurable: $799,999 • Uninsurable: $2M* • Business For Self ALT-A: $1,349,999 * Maximum loan amount is subject to property location. Amortization • 5 to 30 years – Uninsurable • 5 to 25 years – Insured/Insurable • 30 years permitted for Insured only under the following conditions: — Property is owner occupied by an immediate family member AND either: • Minimum one borrower is a First Time Homebuyer OR • New construction property Payment Options • Monthly, Semi-monthly, bi-weekly, weekly (accelerated) Adjustable Rate Mortgage For more products and programs available at MERIX Financial, and for real time access to your deals and conditions status updates, log in at: explore.merixfinancial.com/login www.merixfinancial.com 1-866-809-5800 Page 2 of 2 December 2024 Subject to change at anytime without prior notice. ​Paradigm Quest Inc. operating as MERIX Financial Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #11017 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11725 Adjustable Rate Mortgage Product Detail Prepayment Options • Prepay up to 20% of the original principal amount • Increase payments up to 20% of the original principal amount Early Payout • 3 months interest penalty based on the greater of the contract rate or MERIX Prime Borrower Qualification Insured, Insurable, Uninsurable • All applicants must qualify using the greater of contract rate + 2% or the Benchmark rate • Maximum GDS/TDS ratios allowed is 39%/44% • Mortgage Default Insurance is available for homes with a purchase price of less than $1.5 Million • Minimum 600 credit score (650 if property value >= $1 Million) • Refinance: Minimum 650 credit score for all applicants Conversion • Convert to a new Fixed Rate term equal to or greater than term remaining on ARM. No penalty applicable

entries.text: For more products and programs available at MERIX Financial, and for real time access to your deals and conditions status updates, log in at: explore.merixfinancial.com/login www.merixfinancial.com 1-866-809-5800 December 2024 Subject to change at anytime without prior notice. ​Paradigm Quest Inc. operating as MERIX Financial Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #11017 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11725 Product Detail Product Description This program is suited to home buyers who wish to purchase a home where part or all of the down payment is from borrowed sources. Purpose • Insured • Purchase, Purchase Plus Improvements Property Type • Owner Occupied • Partially Owner Occupied • 2 units Term • 3 Year Fixed closed • 4 Year Fixed closed • 5 Year Fixed closed • 3 Year ARM closed • 5 Year ARM closed LTV • 90.01 - 95.00% LTV Minimum Loan Amount • $50K Maximum Loan Amount • Insured: $1,374,999 Amortization • 5 - 25 years • 30 years permitted for Insured only under the following conditions: — Property is owner occupied by an immediate family member AND either: • Minimum one borrower is a First Time Homebuyer OR • New construction property Borrower Qualification Insured, Insurable • Maximum GDS/TDS ratios allowed is 39%/44% — Repayments must be included in the TDS calculation • Qualified on the greater of Contract Rate + 2% or 5.25% • Guarantors are to occupy the subject property. Down Payment Any portion of the down payment may be borrowed. Repayment of borrowed funds to be included in the TDS per standard Debt Servicing requirements. Eligible sources are to be arm’s length to the transaction and can include but are not limited to: — Personal loans — Lines of credit — Credit cards — Gifts from non-immediate family (considered borrowed) Ineligible: Sources of down payment which are not arm’s length to the transaction are not eligible. These can include but are not limited to loans or incentives from the builder, realtor, mortgage broker, solicitor or vendor, or any loan to the borrower that impacts the property selling price. Borrowed Down Payment

Bridge financing allows customers to borrow funds based on the net equity in their current
entries.text: For more products and programs available at MERIX Financial, and for real time access to your deals and conditions status updates, log in at: explore.merixfinancial.com/login www.merixfinancial.com 1-866-809-5800 November 2023 Subject to change at anytime without prior notice. ​Paradigm Quest Inc. operating as MERIX Financial Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #11017 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11725 Bridge Financing Program Product Detail Product Description A bridge loan is a temporary loan that facilitates closings when the purchase of a customer’s new home closes before the sale of their current home. Bridge financing allows customers to borrow funds based on the net equity in their current home in situations where that equity is tied up until the closing date of their current home. Details • 120 day term • Rate: P+2% • Bridge loan fee: $250 • Minimum bridge loan: $5,000 • Calculation of net proceeds is the sale price less existing mortgage(s) balances less 7% of sale price for closing costs. • Collateral mortgage to be registered where the below is applicable: — Bridge loan is > $150,000 AND/OR — Bridge loan term exceeds 90 days — Discharge fee applicable upon bridge loan payout • Bridge loan payment to be included in TDS when: — Bridge loan term exceeds 90 days — Payment calculation based on outstanding balance, 5 year benchmark rate and a 25 year amortization • Bridge loans are not permitted to facilitate on time closings as a result of land registry system delays in Alberta, Saskatchewan and Manitoba. • The completion date of the purchase must be prior to the completion date of the sale. Requirements • Copies of the Agreement of Purchase/Sale for both the existing property and the subject property being purchased must be obtained and the following confirmed: — That both Agreements of Purchase/Sale are binding contracts — That all conditions have been removed — Receipt of existing mortgage statement to confirm available equity. • The following documents must be received before the new mortgage is advanced: — The Solicitor’s Acknowledgement — The Declaration and Direction — The Promissory Note — The Assignment of Funds — The Statement of Adjustments and Direction to Pay/Trust Ledger confirming net sale proceeds equal to or greater than the bridge loan. • Where the sale property is a private/double ended/non-arms length transaction, additional documentation may be required. Underwriter to advise upon review of file submission.

entries.text: For more products and programs available at MERIX Financial, and for real time access to your deals and conditions status updates, log in at: explore.merixfinancial.com/login www.merixfinancial.com 1-866-809-5800 Page 1 of 2 February 2025 Subject to change at anytime without prior notice. ​Paradigm Quest Inc. operating as MERIX Financial Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #11017 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11725 Business for Self-Alt A Program Product Detail Product Description The Business for Self (BFS) Insured program is designed for self- employed borrower(s) that cannot provide traditional sources of income verification but have a proven 2 year history of managing their credit and finances responsibly. Purpose • Insured, Insurable • Purchase, Purchase Plus Improvements Property Guidelines • Full appraisal required for Insurable applications • Readily marketable residential dwellings, located in markets with demonstrated ongoing re-sale demand • Maximum 4 units where 1 unit must be owner occupied • Property value = maximum $1,499,999 Term • 5 Year Fixed closed • 5 Year ARM closed Maximum LTV • Up to 90% • Borrower paid default insurance is required for LTV’s over 65% Minimum Loan Amount • $50K Maximum Loan Amount • Insured : $1,349,999 • Insurable </= 65% LTV: $649,999 Amortization • Minimum 5 years • Maximum 25 years • 30 years permitted for Insured only under the following conditions: — Property is owner occupied by an immediate family member AND either: • Minimum one borrower is a First Time Homebuyer OR • New construction property Borrower Qualification • All applications must qualify using the greater of contract rate + 2% or the Benchmark rate • Minimum 650 credit score for each applicant. Where LTV </= 80% at least one applicant to have 680 • Borrower(s) operating a business for a minimum of 2yrs. • Maximum GDS/TDS 39% /44% • Minimum 2 trade lines with at least 2yr history • No mortgage, instalment or revolving credit delinquencies in the past 12 months • No reported mortgage defaults in the past 7yrs • No previous bankruptcy • No judgements or unpaid collections in the last 12 months • No property or income tax arrears For more products and programs available at MERIX Financial, and for real time access to your deals and conditions status updates, log in at: explore.merixfinancial.com/login www.merixfinancial.com 1-866-809-5800 Page 2 of 2 February 2025 Subject to change at anytime without prior notice. ​Paradigm Quest Inc. operating as MERIX Financial Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #11017 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11725 Business for Self-Alt A Program Product Detail Qualifying Income Income stated on application will undergo a Reasonability Test based on industry, length of operation and size of business. • Borrower must provide proof of self-employment for a minimum of 2 yrs. • Most recent Notice of Assessment (NOA) • Disclosure of annual gross BFS revenue, type of business and ownership structure in submission notes. • Commission income NOT eligible. To confirm minimum full 2yrs Self Employment • Sole Proprietorships & Partnerships - one of the following is required: — Business License — GST/HST Return Summary — Accountant prepared T1 Generals and Statement of Business Activities for the last 2yrs — Audited Financial Statements for the last 2yrs prepared & signed by a Chartered Accountant. • Corporations – one of the following is required: — Articles of Incorporation — Audited Financial Statements for the last 2yrs prepared & signed by a Chartered Accountant. Note: If there is income being used from sources other than self- employment (i.e. rental, investment, pension, commission) this income needs to be confirmed via full standard documentation requirements.

Self-employed line 150 NOA income may grossed up by 15% or allowable add backs may be used (u
entries.text: For more products and programs available at MERIX Financial, and for real time access to your deals and conditions status updates, log in at: explore.merixfinancial.com/login www.merixfinancial.com 1-866-809-5800 September 2024 Subject to change at anytime without prior notice. ​Paradigm Quest Inc. operating as MERIX Financial Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #11017 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11725 Business For Self-Fully Qualifying Program Product Detail Product Description The Business for Self fully qualifying program is designed for the self- employed that have traditional verification of income. Self-employed line 150 NOA income may grossed up by 15% or allowable add backs may be used (use of home, automobile expenses, capital cost allowance) if the borrower is a sole proprietor or half owner in a partnership. Self-employed income may not be grossed up if the client owns an incorporated business Credit Requirements • Beacon/FICO score: — 600+ up to 80% LTV — 620+ (program specific requirements apply) for 80.01% to 95% LTV • Maximum GDS/TDS: GDS = 39%, TDS = 44% Documentation • Documents required are most recent 2 years NOAs and corresponding T1 Generals including Statements of Business Activities to confirm net vs. gross business for self-income • Self-employed line 150 NOA income may grossed up by 15% or allowable add backs may be used if the client is a sole proprietor or half owner in a partnership • Self-employed income may not be grossed up if the client owns an incorporated business • Minimum two years in same type of work, even if not in a self-employed capacity. • Income can be grossed up by 15%. Should the borrower have eligible deductions in excess of 15% of the income on their NOA, the borrower can provide the lender with audited financial statements or financial statements prepared by a practicing accountant to support the higher income level in lieu of the 15% gross up. Borrowers must provide the financial statements in addition to the required two most recent years NOAs • Eligible Add backs to net income before taxes: Business use of home, reasonable motor vehicle expenses and capital cost allowances • Self-employed individuals of an incorporated business are required to submit most recent 2 years NOAs and financial statements for the company Eligible Transactions • Purchase, Purchase Plus Improvements, Second homes, Owner Occupied- Rental (1-4 units), Rentals (2-4 units), Family Plan, Flexible Down Payment, BC HOME, NS DPAP Ineligible Transactions • Business for Self (ALT A), Vacation Homes, Seasonal Homes, New to Canada, Non-Permanent Residents, Newcomers to Canada, Cashback, Refinance, Rentals (1 unit)

Cashback is a great idea for those who could use the money right away for completing minor renovations, buying furniture and more. Purpose • Insured, Insurable • Purcha
entries.text: For more products and programs available at MERIX Financial, and for real time access to your deals and conditions status updates, log in at: explore.merixfinancial.com/login www.merixfinancial.com 1-866-809-5800 Page 1 of 2 February 2025 Subject to change at anytime without prior notice. ​Paradigm Quest Inc. operating as MERIX Financial Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #11017 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11725 Cashback Product Product Detail Product Description The Cashback Mortgage offers homeowners a cash lump sum at closing. Cashback is a great idea for those who could use the money right away for completing minor renovations, buying furniture and more. Purpose • Insured, Insurable • Purchase, Switch/Transfers, Collateral Switch/Transfers, Purchase Plus Improvements • Cash back cannot be used for down payment Cashback Options • 1%, 2%, 3%, and 5%* * 5% option only available for insurable deals and existing insured transfers up to 80% LTV Term • 5 Year Fixed closed • 5 Year ARM closed Rate • 120 Day Rate Hold — Pre-approvals / rate holds are not available for this product. • Interest is compounded semi-annually. Maximum LTV • 95% for 1-2 unit owner occupied properties • 90% for 3-4 unit owner occupied properties • 80% for 2-4 unit rental properties • 80% for 1-4 unit rental properties Minimum / Maximum Loan Amount • Minimum: $50K • Maximum: $1,374,999 Amortization • 5 to 25 years – Insured/Insurable • 30 years permitted for Insured only under the following conditions: — Property is owner occupied by an immediate family member AND either: • Minimum one borrower is a First Time Homebuyer OR • New construction property Payment Options • Monthly, Semi-monthly, bi-weekly, weekly (accelerated) Prepayment Options • Prepay up to 20% of the original principal amount • Increase payments up to 20% of the original principal amount For more products and programs available at MERIX Financial, and for real time access to your deals and conditions status updates, log in at: explore.merixfinancial.com/login www.merixfinancial.com 1-866-809-5800 Page 2 of 2 February 2025 Subject to change at anytime without prior notice. ​Paradigm Quest Inc. operating as MERIX Financial Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #11017 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11725 Cashback Product Product Detail Early Payout • FIXED RATE: The greater of 3 months’ interest or IRD AND a percentage repayment of the cash back. • ARM: 3 months’ interest AND a percentage repayment of the cash back • Repayment of cash back amount is based on the following formula: — The product of: • (X) the amount of the cash back amount advanced by us at the time of the original funding or transfer and • (Y) a ratio equal to (a) the Mortgage Term remaining at the relevant time (expressed in months), divided by (b) the original Mortgage Term (expressed in months) — Example: You receive $10,000 Cash back • You want to prepay your five (5) year term loan with 32 months left in the term. • The cash back repayment is $10,000 x (32/60) = $5,333.33 Even if there is no prepayment charge, you must still pay the cash back repayment amount. Borrower Qualification Insured, Insurable • All applicants must qualify using the greater of contract rate + 2% or the Benchmark rate. • Maximum GDS/TDS ratios allowed is 39%/44%. • Minimum 680 credit score for at least 1 qualifying applicant. Minimum 600 for all other applicants. Conversion • Convert to a new 5 Year Fixed Rate term — 5-year fixed rate mortgage only — Cash back repayment amount at the time of conversion would become the new cash back amount on the new fixed term (Calculation noted above to be applied). — The new cash back amount would be subject to the same calculation should the new term be paid out prior to maturity. Other • Broker must indicate “Cashback” in their notes, including requested cashback percentage • Cashback cannot be used for down payment. • Broker Buydown from commission is permitted. • Transactions ineligible for Cashback: — Uninsured, Pre-approvals, Secondary homes, BFS insured program, New to Canada, Assumptions, Blend and increases, Small rentals, and Quick closes.

entries.text: For more products and programs available at MERIX Financial, and for real time access to your deals and conditions status updates, log in at: explore.merixfinancial.com/login www.merixfinancial.com 1-866-809-5800 Page 1 of 3 December 2024 Subject to change at anytime without prior notice. ​Paradigm Quest Inc. operating as MERIX Financial Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #11017 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11725 Collateral Charge Switch / Transfer Program Product Detail Product Description In partnership with First Canadian Title (FCT), MERIX Financial is pleased to offer borrowers the ability to move their existing collateral mortgage charge to MERIX Financial at a competitive rate. Purpose • Transfer in of a collateral mortgage charge into a first mortgage with MERIX • Transfer in of 2 separate charges into a first mortgage with MERIX (Insurable/Uninsurable ONLY) Property Type • Standard property guidelines 1 – 4 units • Owner occupied / Secondary Homes / Rentals Term • 3 Year Fixed closed* • 4 Year Fixed closed* • 5 Year Fixed closed • 3 Year ARM closed* • 5 Year ARM closed • *Rentals not permitted for these terms Maximum LTV • Up to 95% Minimum Loan Amount • $50K • Note: $45K is permitted for owner-occupied and partially owner- occupied properties where fees are borrower paid Maximum Loan Amount • Insured: $1,374,999 • Insurable: $799,999 • Uninsurable: $2M* *Maximum loan amount is subject to property location. Amortization Minimum 5 years Insured: — Maximum: Original scheduled amortization minus time elapsed since the original mortgage registration Insurable: — Maximum 25 years • Remaining amortization = Original amortization less time elapsed since original mortgage registration OR • Single charge mortgages are eligible for amortization extension to 25 years • Two separate charges being combined to one charge are subject to a blended amortization • Underwriter will confirm final amortization once file is submitted Uninsurable: — Maximum 30 years For more products and programs available at MERIX Financial, and for real time access to your deals and conditions status updates, log in at: explore.merixfinancial.com/login www.merixfinancial.com 1-866-809-5800 Page 2 of 3 December 2024 Subject to change at anytime without prior notice. ​Paradigm Quest Inc. operating as MERIX Financial Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #11017 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11725 Collateral Charge Switch / Transfer Program Product Detail Qualifying Rate Insured: • Contract Rate Insurable/Uninsurable: • Greater of contract rate + 2% or the Minimum Qualifying Rate • Insurable Loans funded prior to November 30, 2016 – Contract rate can be used for 5 year fixed terms. ALL OTHER terms – greater of contract rate + 2% or the Minimum Qualifying Rate Borrower Qualification • Insurable/Uninsurable applications must qualify using the greater of contract rate + 2% or the Benchmark rate • Existing Insured Collateral Transfers qualify using contract rate • Minimum 650 credit score for all applicants • GDS/TDS 39% / 44% • Maximum GDS/TDS 39% /44% NOTE: Adding or removing a Covenant is permitted, provided the following criteria is met: • No monies are being exchanged • The individual is related to the borrower • Eligible relationships include: — Child — Spouse (including common law) — Parent — Siblings — Grandparents — Grandchildren — Deceased individual (only when title was held as Joint Tenants. To be confirmed via Death Certificate) Removal is NOT: • A spouse due to separation/divorce • A transaction in which there is an exchange of monies • Arms-length transactions FCT to process title change and register the MERIX mortgage in the name of all the borrowers Documentation • Fully qualified income documentation required • AVM or Appraisal • Payout Authorization Form, Current Renewal Agreement, Fire Insurance particulars, Insurer Certificate Number (if applicable), & proof of municipal & school taxes are paid and up to date For more products and programs available at MERIX Financial, and for real time access to your deals and conditions status updates, log in at: explore.merixfinancial.com/login www.merixfinancial.com 1-866-809-5800 Page 3 of 3 December 2024 Subject to change at anytime without prior notice. ​Paradigm Quest Inc. operating as MERIX Financial Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #11017 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11725 Collateral Charge Switch / Transfer Program Product Detail Fees • Discharge fee paid by MERIX up to $300 • FCT Legal Fees: — Paid by MERIX - reduce commission by 10 bps — Paid by client - standard commission • Fees vary by province / premium for mortgages above $750,000 • FCT Legal fee can be capitalized into loan amount (with restrictions) • Pre-payment penalties and/or fees charged by the current lender may be capitalized to the mortgage loan under the following circumstances only: — Not to exceed a maximum of $3,000 in penalties or fees, AND — Mortgage amount with capitalized penalties and/or fees is not to exceed the original registered mortgage amount Appraisal Cost • When appraisal is required: — Broker to order from an approved MERIX appraiser — MERIX will reimburse a maximum of $375 towards the appraisal cost on FUNDED files — Broker to provide a copy of the invoice along with a complete the Switch Program Appraisal Reimbursement Form and return to your BDM or BRM Broker Requirements • Insurer Certificate Number (for all existing insured applications: CMHC, Sagen or Canada Guaranty) • FCT Payout Authorization Form • Request “Collateral Charge Transfer” in notes section of application • For extended amortization requests, the following must be provided UPFRONT: — Broker to request in submission notes — Broker confirmation that current mortgage is registered as a collateral charge — Broker to provide a copy of mortgage statement, current within 90 days - must include current balance and amortization

Amortization • 5 to 30 years – Uninsurable • 5 to 25 years – Insured/Insurable • 30 years permitted for Insured only under the following conditions: — Property is owner occupied by an immediate family member AND either: • Minimum one borrower is a First Time Homebuyer OR • New construction property Payment Options • Monthly, Semi-monthly, bi-weekly, weekly (accelerated) For more products and programs available at MERIX Financial, and for real time access to your deals and conditions status updates, log in at: explore.merixfinancial.com/login www.merixfinancial.com 1-866-809-5800 Page 2 of 2 December 2024 Subject to change at anytime without prior notice. ​Paradigm Quest Inc. operating as MERIX Financial Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #11017 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11725 Fixed Rate Mortgage Product Detail Prepayment Options • Prepay up to 20% of the original principal amount • Increase payments up to 20% of the original principal amount Early Payout • Greater of 3 months interest or interest rate differential Borrower Qualification • Insured, Insurable, Uninsurable • All applicants must qualify using the greater of contract rate + 2% or 5.25% • Maximum GDS/TDS ratios allowed is 39%/44% • Mortgage Default Insurance is available for homes with a purchase price of less than $1.5 Million • Minimum 600 credit score (650 if property value >= $1.0 Million) • Refinance: Minimum 650 credit score for all applicants

entries.text: For more products and programs available at MERIX Financial, and for real time access to your deals and conditions status updates, log in at: explore.merixfinancial.com/login www.merixfinancial.com 1-866-809-5800 Page 1 of 3 December 2024 Subject to change at anytime without prior notice. ​Paradigm Quest Inc. operating as MERIX Financial Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #11017 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11725 Medical Professionals Program Product Detail Product Description The MERIX Medical Professionals Program allows borrowers who have recently completed or are in the process of completing their residency/ fellowship in the last 12 to 24 months to obtain financing for the purchase of a new home. The program qualifies borrowers on projected income that is aligned with their medical specialization. Purpose • Insured and Insurable loans only • Purchase • Ports and replacement • Insurable Transfers (Standard and Collateral) • Insured Transfers (Standard and Collateral) – Sagen insured only Product/Term • 1 - 5 Year Fixed • 3 and 5 Year ARM (Not Eligible: Secondary Homes, BFS ALT-A Insured, Borrowed Downpayment and New to Canada Programs) Loan to Value Maximum 90% LTV Minimum/Maximum Loan Amount • Minimum: $45,000 • Maximum: — Insured: $1,349,999 — Insurable: $799,999 Occupancy Owner Occupied or Partially Owner Occupied – maximum 2 units Amortization • Minimum 5 years • Maximum 25 years • 30 years permitted for Insured only under the following conditions: — Property is owner occupied by an immediate family member AND either: • Minimum one borrower is a First Time Homebuyer OR • New construction property Qualifying Rate Greater of the contract rate + 2% or the Minimum Qualifying Rate GDS/TDS 39%/44% Credit Requirements • Minimum 650 credit score for each applicant Down Payment • Minimum 10% — Minimum 5% from own resources, remainder can be gifted from an immediate family member Mortgage Insurance LTV Premium 80.01 – 85% 3.10% 85.01 – 90% 4.10% For more products and Professionals Program Criteria Requirements Borrower Eligibility The Medical Professionals program is eligible for individuals in the following fields: Physicians: • Medical professionals registered or enrolled in a recognized medical residency or fellowship in Canada • Newly practicing physicians who began practicing within the last 24 months • Foreign-trained physicians legally entitled to practice in Canada, licensed by a provincial college, and who completed their program within the last 24 months Dentists: • Dental students in their final year of residency/fellowship or recognized program of study in Canada • Newly practicing Dentists who completed their program within the past 24 months Veterinarians: • Veterinary students in their final year of residency/fellowship or recognized program of study in Canada • Newly practicing Veterinarians who completed their program within the past 12 months NOTE: Qualifying guarantors must be a spouse only, and must occupy the subject property Income Criteria Qualifying income is set based on the following: • Physicians: — Residents/Fellows in 1st/2nd year: $185,000 — Residents/Fellows in 3rd year: $225,000 — Newly Practicing: May be qualified based on a projected income with confirmation of specialization. See schedule on Pg. 3 for more details. • Dentists: $175,000 • Veterinarians: $86,000 Medical Professionals Program: Income Schedule With confirmation of specialization, newly practicing Physicians may be qualified based on the following schedule. If specialization is not listed, an amount of $250,000 may be used: Medical Specialization Qualifying Income Anesthesiology $ 300,000.00 Cardiology $ 359,000.00 Cardiovascular / Thoracic Surgery $ 300,000.00 Clinical Immunology / Allergy $ 300,000.00 For more products and programs available at MERIX Financial, and for real time access to your deals and conditions status updates, log in at: explore.merixfinancial.com/login www.merixfinancial.com 1-866-809-5800 Page 3 of 3 December 2024 Subject to change at anytime without prior notice. ​Paradigm Quest Inc. operating as MERIX Financial Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #11017 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11725 Medical Professionals Program Critical Care Medicine $ 300,000.00 Dermatology $ 300,000.00 Diagnostic Radiologists $ 300,000.00 Emergency Medicine $ 300,000.00 Endocrinology / Metabolism $ 300,000.00 Family Medicine $ 225,000.00 Gastroenterology $ 309,000.00 General Internal Medicine $ 300,000.00 General Surgery $ 300,000.00 Geriatric Medicine $ 300,000.00 Hematology $ 300,000.00 Medical Genetics $ 300,000.00 Medical Microbiology & Infectious Diseases $ 300,000.00 Medical Oncology $ 300,000.00 Nephrology $ 300,000.00 Neurology $ 300,000.00 Neurosurgery $ 300,000.00 Nuclear Medicine $ 300,000.00 Obstetrics/Gynecology $ 300,000.00 Occupational Medicine $ 300,000.00 Ophthalmology $ 379,000.00 Orthopedic Surgery $ 300,000.00 Otolaryngology $ 300,000.00 Pediatrics $ 300,000.00 Physical Medicine & Rehabilitation $ 300,000.00 Plastic Surgery $ 300,000.00 Psychiatry $ 300,000.00 Public Health & Preventive Medicine $ 300,000.00 Radiation Oncology $ 300,000.00 Respirology $ 300,000.00 Rheumatology $ 300,000.00 Urology $ 300,000.00

entries.text: For more products and programs available at MERIX Financial, and for real time access to your deals and conditions status updates, log in at: explore.merixfinancial.com/login www.merixfinancial.com 1-866-809-5800 Page 1 of 2 December 2024 Subject to change at anytime without prior notice. ​Paradigm Quest Inc. operating as MERIX Financial Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #11017 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11725 New to Canada Mortgage Criteria Requirements Product Description The New to Canada Program is ideal for qualified borrowers who have immigrated to Canada from another country or have been transferred to Canada by an employer can qualify for mortgage insurance with as little as 5% down payment. Purpose • Purchase, Purchase Plus Improvements Property Type • 1-4 Units • Owner Occupied • Partially Owner Occupied Term • 1-5 Year Fixed closed • 3 and 5 Year ARM closed Maximum LTV • High Ratio and Insurable only • Up to 95% LTV High Ratio (Maximum 90% LTV for 3-4 Units) • Up to 80% LTV Insurable Minimum Loan Amount • $45K Maximum Loan Amount • Insurable: $799,999 • Insured: $1,374,999 Amortization • Minimum 5 years • Maximum 25 years • 30 years permitted for Insured only under the following conditions: — Property is owner occupied by an immediate family member AND either: • Minimum one borrower is a First Time Homebuyer OR • New construction property Payment Options • Monthly, Semi-monthly, bi-weekly, weekly (accelerated) Prepayment Options • Prepay up to 20% of the current principal amount • Increase payments up to 20% of the original principal amount Down Payment • Minimum 5% down payment from own resources or gifted from an immediate relative (i.e.: parent, sibling, grandparent, legal guardian, legal dependent, spouse) • Remainder of down payment can be gifted from a corporate subsidy • Government grants are eligible for Permanent Residents only For more products and programs available at MERIX Financial, and for real time access to your deals and conditions status updates, log in at: explore.merixfinancial.com/login www.merixfinancial.com 1-866-809-5800 Page 2 of 2 December 2024 Subject to change at anytime without prior notice. ​Paradigm Quest Inc. operating as MERIX Financial Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #11017 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11725 New to Canada Mortgage Criteria Requirements Borrower Qualification • Non-Permanent: Foreign workers with valid Canadian employment authorization (Work Permit) in compliance with the Prohibition on the Purchase of Residential Property by Non Canadians Act: — Purchasing outside of a Census Metropolitan Area/Census Agglomeration OR — Purchasing within of a Census Metropolitan Area/Census Agglomeration • Permit valid for a minimum of 183 days at time of entering into a binding purchase and sale agreement • Have not purchased more than one residential property in Canada while the prohibition is in effect • Permanent: Verification of landed immigrant (permanent resident) status is required — Immigrated or relocated to Canada within the last 60 months — 3 months minimum full time employment in Canada (borrowers transferred under a corporate relocation are exempt) — Minimum 2 years employment history required • Individuals with diplomatic immunity are not eligible Alternative Sources of Credit Requirements One of the following is required: • Option 1: US or UK International to have a credit bureau demonstrating a strong credit profile: • Option 2: 2 alternative sources of credit demonstrating timely payments (no arrears) for the past 12 months (from a Canadian source). The two alternative sources required are: – Rental payment history confirmed via letter from landlord with supporting bank statements (Letter from landlord indicating the following is required: name of tenant, monthly rent, length of tenancy, payment history) AND – Hydro/utilities, telephone, cable, cell phone and auto insurance to be confirmed via letter from service provider or 12 months of billing statements. • Option 3: – LTV > 90%: 12 months verifiable bank statements from a recognized Canadian financial institution or a financial institution from the country of origin confirming satisfactory savings and repayment habits – LTV </=90%: 6 months bank statements from a recognized financial institution for borrower’s primary account • Option 4: A letter of reference from the borrower’s recognized financial institution Appraisal Requirement • Full appraisal from an approved MERIX appraiser required for all insurable loans • In the case an appraisal is required, client is responsible for the appraisal cost

entries.text: For more products and programs available at MERIX Financial, and for real time access to your deals and conditions status updates, log in at: explore.merixfinancial.com/login www.merixfinancial.com 1-866-809-5800 Page 1 of 2 January 2025 Subject to change at anytime without prior notice. ​Paradigm Quest Inc. operating as MERIX Financial Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #11017 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11725 Purchase Plus Improvements Program Product Detail Product Description The Purchase Plus Improvements program is suited for borrower(s) that require improvements to their newly purchased home. The purchase and the improvements are combined into one mortgage and a single advance. Uninsurable Insured / Insurable CMHC Insured / Insurable - Sagen and Canada Guaranty Documentation • Detailed list of improvements, including a copy of contracts outlining the scope of work and cost estimates • Copy of the building permit if improvements are structural Appraisal Requirement • Broker ordered appraisals to be ordered from MERIX Financial’s approved list Broker to provide appraisal with “as is” AND “as complete” values. Borrower is responsible for appraisal cost. • Insurable – broker to provide appraisal with “as is” AND “as complete” values. Borrower is responsible for appraisal cost. • Insured – if appraisal is required by insurer, they will order and pay for Maximum Improvement Amount Final value determination for lending amount and mortgage default insurance purposes is the LESSER of: a) The improved value of the property as determined by Insurer / full appraisal (if required) OR b) The sum of the total cost of itemized improvements added to the base purchase price Lesser of $40,000 OR 20% of the “initial” value of the property. Maximum 10% of the “as improved” value Improvements can exceed $40,000 or 20% of the “initial” value of the property, as supported by the insurer. Solicitor Instructions • MERIX advances the entire committed amount to the Solicitor at closing. • The Solicitor is instructed to hold back the cost of the improvements until release of funds is approved by MERIX. • Work must be completed within 180 days of the advance. • Work must be completed within 270 days of the advance. For more products and programs available at MERIX Financial, and for real time access to your deals and conditions status updates, log in at: explore.merixfinancial.com/login www.merixfinancial.com 1-866-809-5800 Page 2 of 2 January 2025 Subject to change at anytime without prior notice. ​Paradigm Quest Inc. operating as MERIX Financial Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #11017 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11725 Purchase Plus Improvements Program Product Detail Requirements To Release Funds • Require satisfactory completion of improvements as outlined in the approval quotes. Satisfactory confirmation to be obtained as follows: — Improvements value > $15,000 and/or borrower completed work themselves – an inspection from a MERIX approved appraiser confirming all work has been completed. — Improvements value </= $15,000 – satisfactory before and after photographs and paid invoices confirming approved work is completed as outlined in the original quotes. Full appraisal may be required if documentation does not support this requirement. — Broker orders final inspection if applicable OR submits photos/ invoices to be reviewed by MERIX. — Cost of inspection is borrower responsibility. — Progress draws not available. — MERIX will instruct Solicitor to release the remaining funds to the borrower. Borrower Qualification • All applicants must qualify using the greater of contract rate + 2% or the Benchmark rate. • Maximum GDS/TDS ratios allowed is 39%/44%. • Mortgage Default Insurance is available for homes with a purchase price of less than $1.0 Million. • Minimum 600 credit score for all applicants (650 if property value >= $1 Million). • Refinance: Minimum 650 credit score for all applicants. • Gifted down payments from immediate family member can be used, provided they are properly verified, non-repayable and all other characteristics of the borrower are acceptable. • Government grant may be considered for insured/insurable if pre- approved by insurer. • Where down payment is borrowed, repayments must be included in the TDS calculation.

Purpose • Insured, Insurable, Uninsurable • Purchase,
entries.text: For more products and programs available at MERIX Financial, and for real time access to your deals and conditions status updates, log in at: explore.merixfinancial.com/login www.merixfinancial.com 1-866-809-5800 Page 1 of 2 December 2024 Subject to change at anytime without prior notice. ​Paradigm Quest Inc. operating as MERIX Financial Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #11017 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11725 Second Homes Program Criteria Requirements Product Description • A program designed to assist homeowners with financing up to 95% loan-to-value for the purchase of a secondary home that will be occupied at some point during the year. Purpose • Insured, Insurable, Uninsurable • Purchase, Refinance, Purchase Plus Improvements, Switch/Transfers, Collateral Switch/Transfers Property Type • Eligible properties must be owner-occupied (or occupied by a relative on a rent-free basis) up to a maximum of 1 unit • Property must be suitable for and available for year round occupancy, properties located on an island must have a year-round bridge or ferry access, borrower’s ability to occupy the property must not be restricted or limited at any time. • Some examples of features suitable properties typically have include: — A permanent heat source — Acceptable year-round water source — A permanent primary source of electricity, please note that generators are considered a supplementary source of electricity — Insulated for year-round occupancy — Indoor kitchen and bathroom facilities — Accessible year-round by a vehicle that is licensed for use on municipal, provincial and territorial roadways Note: Vacation properties are eligible as long as they meet all the property requirements above. Vacation properties such as time-share interests, life leases, properties in rental pools, properties constructed for seasonal use or with seasonal access, and mobile homes are not eligible. Term • 3 Year Fixed closed • 4 Year Fixed closed • 5 Year Fixed closed • 3 Year ARM closed • 5 Year ARM closed Maximum LTV • Up to 95% Minimum Loan Amount • $50K For more products and programs available at MERIX Financial, and for real time access to your deals and conditions status updates, log in at: explore.merixfinancial.com/login www.merixfinancial.com 1-866-809-5800 Page 2 of 2 December 2024 Subject to change at anytime without prior notice. ​Paradigm Quest Inc. operating as MERIX Financial Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #11017 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11725 Second Homes Program Criteria Requirements Maximum Loan Amount • Insured: $1,374,999 • Insurable: $799,999 • Uninsurable based on maximum LTV tiers for property location and Product/Program selected Amortization • 30 years – Uninsurable • 25 years – Insured/Insurable • 30 years permitted for Insured only under the following conditions: — Property is owner occupied by an immediate family member AND either: • Minimum one borrower is a First Time Homebuyer OR • New construction property Payment Options • Monthly, Semi-monthly, bi-weekly, weekly (accelerated) Prepayment Options • Prepay up to 20% of the original principal amount • Increase payments up to 20% of the original principal amount Early Payout • 3 - 5 Year Fixed: Greater of 3 months interest or Interest Rate Differential • 3 and 5 Year ARM: 3 months interest based on the greater of the contract rate or MERIX Prime Borrower Qualification Insured, Insurable, Uninsurable • Maximum GDS/TDS ratios allowed is 39%/44% • Mortgage Default Insurance is available for homes with a purchase price of less than $1,499,999 • Minimum 600 credit score (650 if property value > = $1 Million) • Refinance: Minimum 650 credit score for all applicants
