Purpose • Insurable, Uninsurable • Purchase, Refinance, Purchase Plus Improvements, Switch/Transfers, Collateral Switch/Transfers Property Type Non-owner occupied condo, single family, duplex, triplex and fourplex rentals • Contact MERIX for details on fire code requirements (retrofit), applicable in Ontario Only • Located within 30 kms from markets with populations exceeding 25,000 • Maximum 5 acres — Insured / Insurable (2-4 units): minimum 750 sqft — Uninsurable (1-4 units): minimum 750 sqft, apt condo 500 sq ft Excludes • Condo Conversions • Condo that is part of any type of condo hotel and/ or legally tied to a rental pool agreement • Agricultural Land Reserve • Maximum 5 rental properties with MERIX, some exceptions may apply • Maximum 10 rental properties (including those held at other financial institutions) • Portable, assumable and transferable upon approval Term • 5 Year Fixed closed • 5 Year ARM closed Rate • 120 Day Rate Hold — Pre-approvals / rate holds NOT permitted • All loans are subject to a surcharge in rate. Maximum LTV • 80% - Insurable and Uninsurable Minimum Loan Amount • Insurable: $50K • Uninsurable: $50K Maximum Loan Amount • Insurable: $799,999.99 • Uninsurable: $2M — Subject to property location — Max. ETO: $200K For more products and programs available at MERIX Financial, and for real time access to your deals and conditions status updates, log in at: explore.merixfinancial.com/login www.merixfinancial.com 1-866-809-5800 Page 2 of 2 September 2024 Subject to change at anytime without prior notice. ​Paradigm Quest Inc. operating as MERIX Financial Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #11017 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11725 Small Rental Program Product Detail Amortization • Minimum – 5 years • Maximum – 30 years – Uninsurable • Maximum – 25 years – Insurable Down Payment • From own resources (no gifts) • CMHC Insurable loans – partial gifted down payment allowed, minimum 10% to be from own resources Borrower Qualification Insured, Insurable, Uninsurable • Minimum Credit Score: — Purchase/Transfer: — Minimum 650 for all applicants credit — Refinance: — Minimum 680 credit score primary applicant, 650 for all other applicants — Existing MERIX clients: Minimum 650 for all applicants • Debt Service Calculations: — GDS/TDS 39%/44% — Qualifying income from SUBJECT rental property: — Add 50% of income — 100% of Condo Fees — Qualifying income for OTHER rental property(s): • Option 1: – Add 50% of rental income – 50% of condo fees in TDS – Add PI in TDS • Option 2: – Net rental income - via net rental calculator OR T776 Statement of Real Estate Rentals - adding back the actual amount of interest expense and subtracting the annual P&I payment – Net surplus - add to income – Net deficit - add to liabilities or reduce from employment income – Exclude PITH + condo fees from TDS • For Private/Double Ended/Non Arms-Length transactions, a full appraisal OR mortgage default insurance is required (premium to be paid by borrower or may be added to the mortgage.) • No bankruptcy or judgments

entries.text: Neo Mortgage Services Inc. | FSRA License No. 13444 6-Month Insured Purchase Secure a fixed rate now and switch to a longer closed-term mortgage anytime, penalty-free. Debt Servicing GDS: Maximum 39% TDS: Maximum 44% Down Payment All down payments must be verified. Down payment must come from the borrower’s own resources or may be gifted funds. Credit Requirements The primary applicant must have a minimum beacon score of 680. All other applicants must have a minimum beacon score of 640. Payment Frequency Weekly, bi-weekly, or monthly. Accelerated options available for weekly and bi-weekly frequencies. Prepayment Options 20% + 20% prepayment privileges: 20% increase in payment, incremental fixed amount can be added to principal and interest payments. Lump sum up to 20% of the original principal amount (minimum $100). Lump sum prepayments can be made on any regular payment date provided the aggregate does not exceed 20% in any year. Renewal Renewal is allowed at maturity or up to 60 days early, with no penalty, as long as the first mortgage payment is made. Renewal must be into a standard 5-year mortgage term, with either a Fixed or ARM rate. A 1% administrative fee on the current balance applies if not renewing with Neo. Title Insurance Required for all mortgage transactions. Loan Purpose Purchases High-Ratio only (LTV of >80%) Not Portable or Assumable No Pre-Approvals Terms 6-Month Fixed Maximum LTV Insured: 95% LTV Loan Amount Minimum: $100,000 Purchase Price Maximum: $1,500,000 Amortization Minimum: 10 years Maximum: 25 years 30-year amortization available for First Time Home Buyers or new construction properties (Insured only). Qualifying Rate Must qualify based on the greater of the contract rate +2% or the Bank of Canada's 5- year benchmark rate. Rate Hold 120 days Float Down Policy: One-time rate float down may be requested during the rate hold period. All requests must be received at least 10 business days prior to closing and are subject to standard rates only. No float-downs to promotional rates. Property Type O wner-occupied, maximum 4 units Rental, maximum 2-4 units (no single unit) Second home, maximum 1 unit No mobile homes January 2026 Mortgage approval is subject to our policies and guidelines. Products are subject to change without notice. www.neofinancial.com

entries.text: Neo Mortgage Services Inc. | FSRA License No. 13444 Port Policy May 2025 Mortgage approval is subject to our policies and guidelines. Products are subject to change without notice. www.neofinancial.com Porting Options Customers can transfer their existing mortgage to a new property with the following optionss q Straight Port – Keep the same loan amounti q Port and Increase – Increase the loan amounti p Available for fixed-rate terms only (not applicable to ARM terms)i q Port and Decrease – Decrease the loan amount. Customers have 30 days between selling their current home and closing on the new one. Mortgage Insurance Considerations p If the existing loan had customer-paid insurance, and as a result of a port the insurance premium increased, the customer is expected to pay for the increase in premiumi p If the existing loan does not have customer-paid insurance, but the new loan does require customer-paid insurance, the customer is expected to pay for the insurance premiumi p When the new loan is to support the purchase of a new property, the application is submitted to the insurer as a Purchase. If the customer is porting the loan to a property they already own, it must be submitted to the insurer as a refinance. New Loan Amount x Penalty Original Loan Amount Penalties & Fees Prepayment penalties (or a portion) are reimbursed when the new loan is advanced. If both closings occur on the same day, the payout penalty is waived. Note: ARM loans are reviewed on a case-by-case basis for eligibility for a 3-month interest waiver. Penalty reimbursement breakdown q Straight Port – Full penalty reimbursementi q Port and Increase – Full penalty reimbursementi q Port and Decrease – Partial reimbursement based on: ( ) Interest Rate Adjustments Port and Increase – A blended rate is calculated as: Port and Decrease – The existing interest rate remains unchanged. The Neo Mortgage Port Policy allows a borrower to transfer their existing mortgage—including its rate, terms, and remaining balance—to a new property without incurring prepayment penalties. (New Money x Current Rate for Product) + (Old Balance x Existing Rate on Loan) (New Money + Old Balance)

Loan Purpose Straight Transfer and Collateral Transfer Terms 1-5 year Fixed 5-year ARM Maximum LTV Insured: ≤ 95% LTV Insurable: ≤ 80% LTV Eligible Products Prime Insured / Insurable Prime Uninsured (Conventional) Loan Amount Minimumß á Standard: $100,00Î á Collateral: $250,000 Amortization ? Straight: Amortization cannot exceed the remaining.6 ? Collateral: Amortization can be extended back to 25 years (insurable). If an insured transfer, the lesser of the remaining amortization or 25 years applies. Insurable deals only: When transferring two or more charges from the same lender into a new first charge, the amortization must not exceed the lesser of the existing amortizations. However, if one of the incoming charges is a Standard Charge, the standard amortization guidelines apply. For Collateral Charges, the amortization may be extended back to 25 years. Property Type á Owner-occupied, maximum 4 unith á Second home, 1 unit onlf á Rental available on Standard transfers only, insurable 2-4 units Note: Exceptions may be considered for the transfer of a standard mortgage previously insured whereby the occupancy use has changed. *Additional fees may apply which are not covered under the program. **Minimum mortgage size of $300,000. March 2025 Mortgage approval is subject to our policies and guidelines. Products are subject to change without notice. www.neofinancial.com Eligible Changes* ? Standard: Not eligible for covenant changes ? Collateral: Addition or removal of a legal spouse or common-law not as a result of marital separation/divorce. As well as a child, parent, sibling, or grandparent/ grandchild. Eligible Fees (included) Certain costs related to the current lender or mortgage may be capped into the new loan, up to a maximum of $3,000, as long as the total loan amount does not surpass the originally registered amount. Eligible fees includeV á Prepayment penaltf á Administration feeh á Accrued interest from the last payment Broker Compensation ? Standard: Full compensation eligible based on the term selected ? Collateral: 10bps reduction in compensation based on term selected**. The Neo Mortgage Transfer Program allows borrowers to transfer a standard charge mortgage from another approved lender with ease. Transactionally insured high-ratio transfers are exempt from the Mortgage Qualifying Rate (MQR). Note: Qualification under the contract rate may be eligible subject to grandfathered eligibility guidelines where we are transferring existing insurance.

entries.text: RFA • 145 KING STREET WEST, SUITE 400, TORONTO, ON M5H 1J8 • 416.477.4617 • RFA.CA FSRA# 12980 | 02.07.2025 P U R C H A S E P L U S I M P R O V E M E N T S H O L D B A C K R E Q U I R E M E N T • The full committed mortgage amount will be advanced to the solicitor at closing with instructions to hold back the cost of the improvements until an inspection by an RFA approved appraiser (at the cost of the client) confirms all work has been completed. • Once we receive this report, we’ll review and provide our acceptance. Upon acceptance, RFA will authorize the solicitor to release the holdback. • All improvements must be completed within 180 days of the advance. L E N D I N G VA L U E Uninsurable, Insurable, & Insured Based on the lesser of the: • The sum of the itemized improvements added to the base purchase price; or • The “improved” value of the property as determined by a full appraisal* *Client paid appraisal for Uninsurable/Insurable. An additional appraisal is not required for Insured if the insurer completes and shares the appraisal with RFA. Note: Lending value for Insured/Insurable mortgages are subject to Mortgage Insurer approval. T H E D E A L • An appraisal completed by FNF, NAS, RPS (Brookfield), Solidifi, or an appraiser from our approved appraiser list outlining the current “as is” value and the “improved” value. • Detailed list of improvements, including copies of the contracts outlining the scope of work and cost estimates M A X I M U M I M P R O V E M E N T A M O U N T Uninsurable • The lesser of $40,000 or 20% of the “initial” property value Insurable • CMHC: Maximum 10% of the “as improved” value of the property. • Sagen/Canada Guaranty: Improvements can exceed • $40,000 or 20% of the “initial” value, as supported by the Insured, without progress draws. Subject to borrowers meeting RFA’s minimum beacon/credit and income requirements. Before • Mortgage amount $450,000, 5 year fixed rate of 4.59%, 25 Year Amortization, Monthly Payments $2,513.19 After • Mortgage amount $490,000 ($40K more), 5 Year Fixed Rate of 4.59%, 25 Year Amortization, Monthly Payments $2,736.59 • For a difference of $223.40 a month your client can upgrade their new home and increase its property value! The examples herein are for illustrative purposes only and do not necessarily reflect the final terms and conditions of the mortgage obtained. The monthly payment amounts illustrated above depict Principal & Interest payments only. Interest rates, terms, products and promotions are subject to change without notice.

entries.text: B E F O R E Y O U C O N T I N U E , F I N D O U T T H E S E K E Y D E TA I L S Standard Charges • Eligible for owner occupied mortgages, second homes, and rentals. • Available for 3-5 year Fixed and 5 year ARM mortgages. • Amortization may be reduced as a result of prepayment privileges, and your client can return to their original amortization minus the time elapsed since original mortgage was registered. • We’ll allow capitalizing up to $3000 in penalties and fees. Mortgage amount with capitalized penalties and/or fees cannot exceed the original registered mortgage amount. • No fee transfer is available. • Appraisal reimbursement may be available. Collateral Charges • Eligible for owner occupied mortgages and second homes. • Available for 3-5 year Fixed or ARM Mortgages. • Amortization may be reduced as a result of prepayment privileges, and your client can return to their original amortization minus the time elapsed since original mortgage was registered. • We’ll allow capitalizing up to $3000 in penalties and fees. Mortgage amount with capitalized penalties and/or fees cannot exceed the original registered mortgage amount. • Fees payable by client or broker. • Appraisal reimbursement may be available. Rental Transfers Regardless of when the mortgage was first originated the following applies: • Insurable: 2-4 unit rentals only, tiered insurable rental rates apply (5 year fixed and ARM), full appraisal required (may be eligible for appraisal reimbursement) • Uninsurable: 1 unit rental only, uninsured rates apply, full appraisal required M O RTG AG E I N S U RA N C E R U L ES Insured (Client Paid Insurance Premium) • Effective October 2023, as per OSFI’s clarification on B-20 Guidelines, a transactionally insured loan (borrower paid) can be qualified on debt serviceability using the contract rate, provided the loan amount is not increased and continues to be amortized in accordance with the amortization period approved by the insurer. • Qualify on contract rate for 5 year fixed term • Max amortization ≤25 years • Existing amortization (am) remains (even if >25 years). Remaining am not to exceed the original am minus the time elapsed since original mortgage was registered. Insurable before Nov. 30, 2016 • Qualify on contract rate for 5 year fixed term. • No restriction on property value (>$1M). • Amortization must be reduced to ≤25 years. • If refinanced prior to Nov 30, 2016 we can still qualify the transfer on contract rate. Insurable funded after Nov. 30, 2016 • Qualify on greater of contract rate or Bank of Canada benchmark rate. • Property value must be <$1M. • Amortization must be reduced to ≤25 years. • If refinanced after Nov 30/2016 and property <$1M we can proceed if full appraisal shows <$1M. • If refinanced after November 30, 2016 and current property value is >$1M we cannot proceed as an insurable transfer. Application must be processed as an uninsurable transfer. H O W D O E S T H I S I M PA C T M Y C L I E N T ’ S T R A N S F E R ? KE Y D E TA ILS FO R D EA L N OT ES 1. Was the mortgage previously insured? If so, note the insurer/mortgage reference #. 2. When was the mortgage originally funded? 3. What type of charge is the mortgage (standard or collateral)? 4. Provide the original purchase price, date, insurer, and insurer reference # if applicable. 5. Are you using grandfathered mortgage insurance rules? Information is subject to change at any time and without notice. HERE’S WHAT YOU NEED TO KNOW Chevron-circle-right RFA • 145 KING STREET WEST, SUITE 400, TORONTO, ON M5H 1J8 • 416.477.4617 • RFA.CA FSRA# 12980 | 02.07.2025 My client has 26 years remaining on their amortization which was originally completed as a 30 year AM on a 4 year term. Can they keep their AM the same if the loan is currently insured? Yes! The client may transfer with their remaining amortization. Client originally purchased a home 5 years ago, but it is now valued at >$1M. Are they still eligible for a transfer? Yes! We can transfer it as an Insurable mortgage under the previous rules, given the mortgage was originally funded prior to Nov. 30, 2016. The existing lender is charging fees for my client to transfer out of their current mortgage. Does RFA include the existing lender fees? Yes! We’ll allow capitalizing up to $3000 in fees* charged by the existing financial institution: • Will allow capitalizing up to $3000 in penalties and fees. Mortgage amount with capitalized penalties and/or fees is not to exceed the original registered mortgage amount. The examples herein are for illustrative purposes only, each deal will be reviewed based on its specific details. Contact your RVP or Underwriter for more info. COMPENSATION LOAN AMOUNT ≥ $ 1 5 0 , 0 0 0 L E N D E R PA I D F E E S C L I E N T PA I D F E E S Collateral Transfers 105 bps • Eligible for appraisal rebate* • Up to $3,000 capitalization of penalties and fees 115 bps • Eligible for appraisal rebate* • Up to $3,000 capitalization of penalties and fees Straight Transfers 115 bps • Eligible for appraisal rebate* • Up to $3,000 capitalizationof penalties and fees N/A LOAN AMOUNT < $ 1 5 0 , 0 0 0 Collateral Transfers 95 bps • No appraisal rebate • Up to $3,000 capitalization of penalties and fees 115 bps • No appraisal rebate • Up to $3,000 capitalization of penalties and fees Straight Transfers 105 bps • No appraisal rebate • Up to $3,000 capitalizationof penalties and fees 115 bps • No appraisal rebate • Up to $3,000 capitalization of penalties and fees *The compensation illustrated is based on a 5 year term. *We reimburse up to $400 for Insiders & up to $300 for non-Insiders appraisals with loan amounts greater than $150K for our approved lending regions (transfers only). Please have all reimbursement requests for appraisals sent directly to sales@rfamortgages.ca. The broker should include the following information in their email: Mortgage number, funded date, borrower last name & attachment with proof of fee amount. Information is subject to change at any time and without notice. 01 02 03 RFA • 145 KING STREET WEST, SUITE 400, TORONTO, ON M5H 1J8 • 416.477.4617 • RFA.CA FSRA# 12980 | 02.07.2025

Box 351 STN C Kitchener, ON N2G 3Y9 p 866-809-5800 f 866-371-0132 e loanupdate@mortgageservicing.ca www.RMGmortgages.ca April 2022 RMG Mortgages is a division of MCAP Financial Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10600 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11790 RMG mortga ges Bridge Financing Request Borrower Name:   Mortgage Number:   Requested # of Days:   SOLICITOR FOR PURCHASE:   Purchase date:   SOLICITOR FOR SALE:   Sale date:   Existing Property Address Sale Price:   Less Estimated Closing Costs 7%: $:   ( RMG guideline 7%. For exception please forward statement of adjustments from Solicitor ) Less Existing Mortgage (use secured LOC limit if applicable*): $   * client may be expected to draw from available secured LOC to minimize required bridge loan amount = Maximum funds available for bridge: $   New Property Address Purchase Price:   Less Deposit on Offer:   Less New RMG Mortgage:   = Down Payment Required: $   Bridge Financing Requested: $   ( can also include closing costs and/or land transfer taxes if applicable ) Please complete in full and upload via Gateway along with firm offers for both properties and copy of current mortgage statement. • Interest rate: Prime + 2% • Administration Fee $300.00 (to be deducted from Bridge Loan advance) • Note that discharge fees are applicable based on province. • Minimum Loan Amount $5,000.00 • All bridge loans exceeding $150,000 and/or 90 days must be secured by Collateral Charge on the property being sold. – Bridge loans > 90 days to be included in TDS. Payment calculation: bridge loan amount at 5 year benchmark rate over 25 year amortization. • Maximum term 120 days (discuss with BDM if exception required) • Where the sale property is a private/double ended/non-arms length transaction, additional documentation may be required. Underwriter to advise upon review of file submission.

RMG Mortgages is a division of MCAP Financial Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10600 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11790 RMGbrokersupport@RMGmortgages.ca www.RMGmortgages.ca 1 866 809 5800 Product Details Purpose • Purchase, Switch/Transfer and Refinance • Purchase Plus Improvements Mortgage Insurer CMHC, Sagen or Canada Guaranty Loan Amount • Minimum: $45,000 • Maximum: - High Ratio - $1,374,999 - Insurable - $799,999 - Uninsurable - Maximum based on geographical area Purchase Price • Maximum: - High Ratio - $1,499,999 - Insurable - $999,999 - Uninsurable - N/A Amortization • Uninsurable – 30 years • Insured/Insurable - 25 years • 30 years permitted for Insured only under the following conditions: - Property is owner occupied by an immediate family member AND either: ▪Minimum one borrower is a First Time Homebuyer OR ▪New construction property Term • 3 and 5 year Rate Hold 120 days Rate Qualification High Ratio / Insurable / Uninsurable: - Applications must qualify using the greater of contract rate + 2% or the minimum qualifying rate - Existing High Ratio Transfers / Collateral Transfers qualify at contract rate Loan to Value • 95% LTV – High Ratio/Purchases/Transfers • 80% LTV – Insurable/Uninsurable Credit Scores • Property < $1M - minimum 600 • Property >= $1M - minimum 650 • Refinance - minimum 650 all applicants • Rental Program - minimum 650 all applicants Occupancy • Owner occupied, max 4 units where 1 is owner occupied • Second Homes (maximum 1 unit) • Insurable Rentals ( 5 year term only ) GDS/TDS 39%/44% Standard Adjustable Rate Mortgage Page 2 of 2 December 2024 Mortgage approval is subject to our policies and guidelines. Please note that RMG Mortgages does not operate in Québec. RMG Mortgages is a division of MCAP Financial Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10600 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11790 RMGbrokersupport@RMGmortgages.ca www.RMGmortgages.ca 1 866 809 5800 Product Details Conversion Conversion at any time to a fixed rate mortgage offered by RMG Mortgages provided that the term chosen for conversion is not shorter than the remaining term on the ARM. The conversion rate will be the best published rate for the borrower’s rate category and term chosen at the time of conversion. Repayment 3 months interest at prevailing interest rate OR RMG Prime (whichever is greater) Portable/Assumable Yes (subject to RMG approval) Payment Frequency Weekly, Bi-weekly, Semi-monthly, Monthly Prepayment 20/20 Rate Buy Down 20 bps of finder fee allows 5 bps off customer rate* * Buy downs may be limited, contact your BDM for details. Appraisal Requirement • In the case an appraisal is required, client is responsible for the appraisal cost. Appraisal fee rebate program is available. • Appraisal rebate requests must be processed within 6 months of invoice date. • Must be from an approved RMG Appraiser. Other Regional and property type restrictions may apply, contact your BDM for details

RMG Mortgages is a division of MCAP Financial Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10600 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11790 RMGbrokersupport@RMGmortgages.ca www.RMGmortgages.ca 1 866 809 5800 RMG Business-for-Self (BFS) Insured Program The RMG Business-for-Self (BFS) Insured/Insurable Program is designed to help BFS borrowers obtain mortgage financing who cannot provide traditional income verification but have a proven 2yr history of managing their credit and finances responsibly. Product Details Purpose Insured/Insurable Purchases only. Client paid insurance premium applicable for LTVs above 65%. • Purchase • Ports and Replacements • Purchase Plus Improvements (Not Eligible: Secondary Homes, New to Canada Program, Refinances, AVM, Cash Backs) RMG Products • Standard 5yr Fixed • Standard 5yr ARM LTV • Maximum 90% • Borrower paid default insurance is required for LTVs over 65% Property Value • Maximum: - Insured: $1,499,999 - Insurable: $999,999 Loan Amount • Minimum $50,000 • Maximum: - Insured : $1,349,999 - Insurable </= 65% LTV: $649,999 Down Payment • From own resources • Gifted down payment permitted. Minimum 5% down payment must be from own savings. Rate • Insured / Insurable rates apply ( no rate premiums ) Qualifying Rate Greater of the contract rate +2% or the Bank of Canada 5yr Minimum qualifying rate Amortization • Minimum 5yrs • Maximum 25yrs • 30 years permitted for Insured only under the following conditions: - Property is owner occupied by an immediate family member AND either: ▪Minimum one borrower is a First Time Homebuyer OR ▪New construction property Insurance Premium LTV Premium Top-Up Premium Up to 65% N/A N/A 65.01% - 75% 2.60% 6.50% 75.01% - 80% 3.30% 7.00% 80.01% - 85% 3.75% 7.50% 85.01% - 90% 5.85% 9.00% Page 2 of 2 February 2025 Mortgage approval is subject to our policies and guidelines. Products subject to change without notice. RMG Mortgages is a division of MCAP Financial Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10600 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11790 RMGbrokersupport@RMGmortgages.ca www.RMGmortgages.ca 1 866 809 5800 RMG Business-for-Self (BFS) Insured Program The RMG Business-for-Self (BFS) Insured/Insurable Program is designed to help BFS borrowers obtain mortgage financing who cannot provide traditional income verification but have a proven 2yr history of managing their credit and finances responsibly. Product Details Borrower Eligibility • >80% LTV: Minimum 650 credit score for all applicants • ≤80% LTV: Minimum 680 credit score for at least 1 applicant. Minimum 650 for all other applicants • Borrower(s) operating a business for a minimum of 2yrs. • Maximum GDS/TDS 39% /44% • Minimum 2 trade lines with at least 2yr history • No mortgage, instalment or revolving credit delinquencies in the past 12 months • No reported mortgage defaults in the past 7yrs • No previous bankruptcy • No judgements or unpaid collections in the last 12 months • No property or income tax arrears Qualifying Income Income stated on application will undergo a Reasonability Test based on industry, length of operation and size of business. • Borrower must provide proof of self-employment for a minimum of 2yrs. • Most recent Notice of Assessment (NOA) • Disclosure of annual gross BFS revenue, type of business and ownership structure in submission notes • Commission income NOT eligible To confirm minimum full 2yrs Self Employment • Sole Proprietorships & Partnerships - one of the following is required - Business License - GST/HST Return Summary - Accountant prepared T1 Generals and Statement of Business Activities for the last 2yrs - Audited Financial Statements for the last 2yrs prepared & signed by a Chartered Accountant • Corporations – one of the following is required: - Articles of Incorporation - Business/Corporation Search - 2 years T2 Corporation Income Tax Returns - Business License - Business Credit Report - Audited Financial Statements for the last 2yrs prepared & signed by a Chartered Accountant Note: If there is income being used from sources other than self-employment (i.e. rental, investment, pension, commission) this income needs to be confirmed via full standard documentation requirements. Property Guidelines • Full appraisal required for Insurable applications (≤65% LTV). RMG Appraisal rebate program eligible • Readily marketable residential dwellings, located in markets with demonstrated ongoing re-sale demand • SFD = minimum 750 sq. ft. • Condo = minimum 500 sq. ft. • Maximum 4 units where 1 unit must be owner occupied

RMG Mortgages is a division of MCAP Financial Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10600 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11790 RMGbrokersupport@RMGmortgages.ca www.RMGmortgages.ca 1-866-809-5800 Borrowed Down Payment Program The RMG Mortgages Borrowed Down Payment Program gives brokers a solution to offer to homeowners where they may need to borrow all or some of their down payment. Through this program, homeowners have the option of down payment flexibility to help achieve their homeownership goals sooner. Details Purpose • Insured Purchases only • Purchase Plus Improvements • Port/Replacement Insurer Info • CMHC, Sagen or Canada Guaranty • Standard Premium: 4.50% • Top Up Premium: 6.60% RMG Products • Standard 3-5 Year Fixed • Standard 3 and 5 Year ARM (Not Eligible: LRB or RMG Peace of Mind Cashback ) Purchase Price Maximum $1,499,999 LTV 90.01-95% Loan Amount • Minimum: $50K • Maximum: $1,374,999 Occupancy Owner Occupied or Partially Owner Occupied – maximum 2 units (Not Eligible: Secondary Home Program) Amortization • Minimum 5 years • Maximum 25 years • 30 years permitted for Insured only under the following conditions: - Property is owner occupied by an immediate family member AND either: ▪Minimum one borrower is a First Time Homebuyer OR ▪New construction property Qualifying Rate Greater of the contract rate + 2% or the Minimum Qualifying Rate GDS/TDS 39% / 44% Credit Requirements • Minimum 650 credit score for all applicants • Minimum 2 trade lines with at least 2-year history • No previous bankruptcy or judgements • No income tax arrears Downpayment • Any portion of the down payment may be borrowed • Repayment of borrowed funds included in TDS as per standard debt servicing requirements (see Guidelines – Debt Servicing in Gateway) • Eligible sources are to be arm’s length and may include but are not limited to: - Personal loans - Lines of credit - Credit cards - Gifts from non-immediate family members • Non-arm’s length sources are ineligible and may include but are not limited to: • Loans or incentives from the builder, realtor, mortgage broker, solicitor or vendor • Any loan to the borrower that impacts the property selling price

entries.text: December 2024 Mortgage approval is subject to our policies and guidelines. Products subject to change without notice. RMG Mortgages is a division of MCAP Financial Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10600 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11790 RMGbrokersupport@RMGmortgages.ca www.RMGmortgages.ca 1 866 809 5800 Collateral Charge “Transfer” Program Insured / Insurable / Uninsurable In partnership with First Canadian Title (FCT), RMG Mortgages is pleased to offer homeowners the ability to move their existing collateral mortgage charge to RMG Mortgages at a competitive rate at no cost to the borrower. RMG’s Collateral Charge Transfer Program is designed for clients that prefer a competitive rate with a regular charge mortgage. Program Details Purpose • Transfer in of a collateral mortgage charge into a regular first mortgage charge • Transfer in of 2 separate charges into a first mortgage charge (Insurable/ Uninsurable ONLY) Property • Standard property guidelines apply • Owner occupied: 1-4 units • Secondary Homes: 1 unit only • Rentals (Insured/Insurable only): 1-4 units - Single unit rentals must meet the below criteria: Д Insured - original loan funded as owner occupied property Д Insurable - original loan funded prior to November 30th, 2016 Product • Insured/Insurable/Uninsurable • Standard 3-5 year Fixed • Standard 3 and 5 year ARM • Low Rate Basic Fixed/ARM • Rentals: - Insured/Insurable only - Standard 5 year fixed/ARM only Rate & Commission Options FCT Legal Fee: Paid by RMG - Best Rates - 10bps less from Standard Commission - Discharge Fee: Paid by RMG up to $250 - Appraisal Fee: Rebate Program OR FCT Legal Fee: Client to Pay - Can be capitalized into loan amount or client can pay with own funds (refer to “Capitalization”) - Best Rates - Standard Commission - Discharge Fee: Paid by RMG up to $250 Appraisal Fee: Rebate Program Rate Qualification • High Ratio - Contract Rate • Insurable / Uninsurable - Greater of Contract Rate + 2% or the minimum qualifying rate *Insurable Transfers: If the mortgage loan is being transferred from one of the MCAP group of brands (MCAP, Eclipse, Partner Mortgage) the loan purpose of the current registered mortgage must have been a purchase transaction only (not a refinance). December 2024 Mortgage approval is subject to our policies and guidelines. Products subject to change without notice. RMG Mortgages is a division of MCAP Financial Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10600 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11790 RMGbrokersupport@RMGmortgages.ca www.RMGmortgages.ca 1 866 809 5800 Collateral Charge “Transfer” Program Insured / Insurable / Uninsurable In partnership with First Canadian Title (FCT), RMG Mortgages is pleased to offer homeowners the ability to move their existing collateral mortgage charge to RMG Mortgages at a competitive rate at no cost to the borrower. RMG’s Collateral Charge Transfer Program is designed for clients that prefer a competitive rate with a regular charge mortgage. Program Details Loan to Value • up to 95% - High Ratio • up to 80% - Insurable / Uninsurable Credit Scores Minimum 650 for all applicants Amortization • Minimum 5 years Insured: • Maximum: Original scheduled amortization minus time elapsed since the original mortgage registration Insurable: • Maximum 25 years Д Remaining amortization = Original amortization less time elapsed since original mortgage registration OR Д Single charge mortgages are eligible for amortization extension to 25 years Д Two separate charges being combined to one charge are subject to a blended amortization Д Blended amortization calculator located in “Tools” - underwriter will confirm final amortization once file is submitted Uninsurable: • Maximum 30 years Payment frequency Weekly, Bi-weekly, Semi-Monthly, Monthly Pre-payment 20/20 Loan Amount • Minimum - Standard Products - $45,000 - Low Rate Basic Products - $75,000 - Where RMG is paying FCT fee - $50K for standard products • Maximum: - Insured - $1,374,999 - Insurable - $799,999 - Uninsurable - $2M* • Uninsurable - maximum loan amount based on geographical area • RMG requires the most current collateral mortgage statement confirming the up-to-date total loan amount for all components (including HELOC balances). • At funding, the RMG mortgage will payout the existing mortgage component (this may include a payment of the penalty/fees up to a maximum of $3000 if applicable). December 2024 Mortgage approval is subject to our policies and guidelines. Products subject to change without notice. RMG Mortgages is a division of MCAP Financial Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10600 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11790 RMGbrokersupport@RMGmortgages.ca www.RMGmortgages.ca 1 866 809 5800 Collateral Charge “Transfer” Program Insured / Insurable / Uninsurable In partnership with First Canadian Title (FCT), RMG Mortgages is pleased to offer homeowners the ability to move their existing collateral mortgage charge to RMG Mortgages at a competitive rate at no cost to the borrower. RMG’s Collateral Charge Transfer Program is designed for clients that prefer a competitive rate with a regular charge mortgage. Program Details Covenant Changes – Removal/Addition of a covenant • Will ONLY be permitted under the following scenarios, where there is no exchange of monies: - Removal/Addition - of a child, parent, spouse (including common law spouse), siblings, grandparents, grandchildren or deceased individual where title held as joint tenants (confirmed via death ceritificate) • Will NOT be permitted for marital breakdowns, arms-length transactions, deceased individuals, where title is not held as joint tenants or transactions where monies are being exchanged • If unable to meet requirements, confirmation that covenant change has been completed prior to transfer must be obtained OR application will have to be processed as a Refinance. • Additional FCT/FNF Legal Fees may apply depending on scenario & province. (contact BDM for fee details) Broker Checklist • Confirm registration type and ensure the mortgage is a collateral charge prior to submitting for approval • Provide valid existing insurer reference number if existing insured • Insurable/Uninsurable - Transfer in of 2 separate charges into a first mortgage charge - both charges to be from approved Canadian financial institutions. No Privates. A small additional FCT fee may apply to discharge 2 mortgages (not available for Insured loans). Contact your BDM for details • Must indicate “RMG Collateral Transfer Program” in notes • Request “Collateral Charge Transfer” in notes section of application • For extended amortization requests, the following must be provided UPFRONT: - Broker to request in submission notes - Broker confirmation that current mortgage is registered as a collateral charge - Broker to provide a copy of mortgage statement, current within 90 days - must include current balance and amortization Provinces All Provinces except Quebec

The program qualifies borrowers on projected income that is aligned with their medical specialization. Product Details Mortgage Insurance LTV Standard Premium 80.01 - 85% 3.10% 85.01 - 90% 4.10% Borrower Eligibility The Medical Professionals program is eligible for individuals in the following fields: Physicians: • Medical professionals registered or enrolled in a recognized medical residency or fellowship in Canada • Newly practicing physicians who began practicing within the last 24 months • Foreign-trained physicians legally entitled to practice in Canada, licensed by a provincial college, and who completed their program within the last 24 months Dentists: • Dental students in their final year of residency/fellowship or recognized program of study in Canada • Newly practicing Dentists who completed their program within the past 24 months Veterinarians: • Veterinary students in their final year of residency/fellowship or recognized program of study in Canada • Newly practicing Veterinarians who completed their program within the past 12 months NOTE: Qualifying guarantors must be a spouse only, and must occupy the subject property RMGbrokersupport@RMGmortgages.ca www.RMGmortgages.ca 1-866-809-5800 August 2024 Mortgage approval is subject to our policies and guidelines. Products subject to change without notice. entries.text: New To Canada Program Page 1 of 3 December 2024 Mortgage approval is subject to our policies and guidelines. Products subject to change without notice. RMG Mortgages is a division of MCAP Financial Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10600 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11790 RMGbrokersupport@RMGmortgages.ca www.RMGmortgages.ca 1 866 809 5800 The RMG Mortgages New To Canada Program makes it possible for individuals relocating to Canada to purchase a home sooner with as little as 5% down. Through this program, RMG helps new Canadians purchase their first home, build equity, and become economically established in Canada. Financing is available to borrowers with permanent and non- permanent residence status and where there is limited Canadian credit history and where foreign credit bureaus may not be available. Program Details Purpose Purchases, Purchase Plus Improvements, Ports and Replacements Borrower Eligibility • Non-Permanent: Foreign workers with valid Canadian employment authorization (Work Permit) in compliance with the Prohibition on the Purchase of Residential Property by Non-Canadians Act • Permanent: Verification of landed immigrant (permanent resident) status is required - Immigrated or relocated to Canada within the last 60 months - 3 months minimum full time employment in Canada (borrowers transferred under a corporate relocation are exempt) - Minimum 2 years employment history required • Individuals with diplomatic immunity are not eligible Purchase Price • Maximum - Insured: $1,499,999 - Insurable: $999,999 Minimum/Maximum Loan Amounts Minimum: • Standard Fixed/ARM - $45K • Low Rate Basic - $75K Maximum: • Insurable - $799,999 • Insured - $1,374,999 Loan to Value • High Ratio and Insurable only • Up to 95% LTV High Ratio (Maximum 90% LTV for 3-4 Units) • Up to 80% LTV Insurable Beacon Scores Minimum of 0 or 600+ (Scores of 1 to 599 are NOT eligible.) (Borrowers without a Canadian credit history are to comply with “Alternative Sources of Credit” requirements) GDS/TDS 39%/44% (all debts outside of Canada are to be included in the ratios) Down Payment • Minimum 5% down payment from own resources or gifted from an immediate relative (i.e.: parent, sibling, grandparent, legal guardian, legal dependent, spouse). • Remainder of down payment can be gifted from a corporate subsidy. • Government grants are eligible for Permanent Residents only. Rate Qualification High Ratio / Insurable: - Applications must qualify using the greater of contract rate + 2% or the minimum qualifying rate New To Canada Program Page 2 of 3 December 2024 Mortgage approval is subject to our policies and guidelines. Products subject to change without notice. RMG Mortgages is a division of MCAP Financial Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10600 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11790 RMGbrokersupport@RMGmortgages.ca www.RMGmortgages.ca 1 866 809 5800 The RMG Mortgages New To Canada Program makes it possible for individuals relocating to Canada to purchase a home sooner with as little as 5% down. Through this program, RMG helps new Canadians purchase their first home, build equity, and become economically established in Canada. Financing is available to borrowers with permanent and non- permanent residence status and where there is limited Canadian credit history and where foreign credit bureaus may not be available. Program Details Amortization • Minimum 5 years • Maximum 25 years • 30 years permitted for Insured only under the following conditions: - Property is owner occupied by an immediate family member AND either: ▪ Minimum one borrower is a First Time Homebuyer OR ▪ New construction property Product/Term • 1 – 5 year Fixed • 3 and 5 year Adjustable • Low Rate Basic Fixed / Adjustable Alternative Sources of Credit Requirements One of the following is required: • Option 1: US or UK International to have a credit bureau demonstrating a strong credit profile: • Option 2: 2 alternative sources of credit demonstrating timely payments (no arrears) for the past 12 months (from a Canadian source). The two alternative sources required are: - Rental payment history confirmed via letter from landlord with supporting bank statements (Letter from landlord indicating the following is required: name of tenant, monthly rent, length of tenancy, payment history) AND - Hydro/utilities, telephone, cable, cell phone and auto insurance to be confirmed via letter from service provider or 12 months of billing statements. • Option 3: - LTV > 90%: 12 months verifiable bank statements from a recognized Canadian financial institution or a financial institution from the country of origin confirming satisfactory savings and repayment habits - LTV </=90%: 6 months bank statements from a recognized financial institution for borrower’s primary account • Option 4: A letter of reference from the borrower’s recognized financial institution Occupancy • 1-4 Units • Owner Occupied • Partially Owner Occupied Portable / Assumable Yes (subject to RMG approval) Payment Frequency Weekly, Bi-weekly, Semi-monthly, Monthly Prepayment 20/20 Rate Buy Down • Broker has the discretion to buy down the rate 5 bps with every 20 bps in commission • Maximum buy downs may apply Other Regional and property type restrictions may apply, contact your BDM for details New To Canada Program Page 3 of 3 December 2024 Mortgage approval is subject to our policies and guidelines. Products subject to change without notice. RMG Mortgages is a division of MCAP Financial Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10600 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11790 RMGbrokersupport@RMGmortgages.ca www.RMGmortgages.ca 1 866 809 5800 The RMG Mortgages New To Canada Program makes it possible for individuals relocating to Canada to purchase a home sooner with as little as 5% down. Through this program, RMG helps new Canadians purchase their first home, build equity, and become economically established in Canada. Financing is available to borrowers with permanent and non- permanent residence status and where there is limited Canadian credit history and where foreign credit bureaus may not be available. Primary Identification • An employee identity card with a photograph from an employer well known in the community; • A signed automated banking machine (ABM) card or client card issued by a member of the Canadian Payments Association; • A signed credit card issued by a member of the Canadian Payments Association; • A signed Canadian Institute for the Blind (CNIB) client card with a photograph; A birth certificate issued in Canada; • A Social Insurance Number (SIN) card issued by the Government of Canada; • A Certificate of Canadian Citizenship. • Citizenship and Immigration Form IMM1000; or • Citizenship and Immigration Form IMM1442; Note: 1 In Ontario, Manitoba, Nova Scotia and Prince Edward Island, the Provincial Health Card is NOT an acceptable form of identification. Please request another piece of Primary identification. Secondary Identification • A valid driver’s licence issued in Canada; • A valid driver’s licence issued in Canada; • Current Canadian Passport; • Nexus card • A Federally issued Firearms Licence • Certificate of Canadian Citizenship (containing your photograph) or Certification of Naturalization (containing your photograph); • Federally issued Permanent Resident Card; • A Certificate of Indian Status issued by the Government of Canada; • A Provincial Health Insurance Card (with photo)1; A Provincial Government issued Photo ID Card; or document or card with your signature and photograph on it issued by: • The Insurance Corporation of British Columbia; • Alberta Registries; • Saskatchewan Government Insurance; • The Department of Service Nova Scotia and Municipal Relations; • The Department of Transportation and Public Works of the Province of Prince Edward Island; • Service New Brunswick; • The Department of Government Services and Lands of the Province of Newfoundland and Labrador; • The Department of Transportation of the Northwest Territories; or • The Department of Community Government and Transportation of the Territory of Nunavut. Acceptable forms of Identification • Each Borrower, Guarantor and Signatory, where applicable, must present to you for examination: • One piece of “Primary Identification” and one piece of “Secondary Identification” or • Two pieces of “Primary Identification • No other form of identification is acceptable. All identification must be original, valid, legible and in good condition. Each Borrower, Guarantor and Signing Officer, where applicable, is required to be physically present when you ascertain his or her identity.

RMG Mortgages is a division of MCAP Financial Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10600 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11790 RMGbrokersupport@RMGmortgages.ca www.RMGmortgages.ca 1 866 809 5800 Uninsurable Insured / Insurable CMHC Insured / Insurable Sagen and Canada Guaranty Documentation • Detailed list of improvements, including a copy of contracts outlining the scope of work and cost estimates • Copy of the building permit if improvements are structural Appraisal Requirement *Broker ordered appraisals to be ordered from RMG’s approved list* Broker to provide appraisal with “as is” AND “improved” values. Borrower is responsible for appraisal cost. • Insurable – broker to provide appraisal with “as is” AND “improved” values. Borrower is responsible for appraisal cost. • Insured – if appraisal is required by insurer, they will order and pay for Maximum Improvement Amount Final value determination for lending amount and mortgage default insurance purposes is the LESSER of: a) The improved value of the property as determined by a full appraisal (if required) OR b) The sum of the total cost of itemized improvements added to the base purchase price Lesser of $40,000 OR 20% of the “initial” value of the property Maximum 10% of the “as improved” value Improvements can exceed $40,000 or 20% of the “initial” value of the property, as supported by the insurer Solicitor Instructions • RMG advances the entire committed amount to the Solicitor at closing • The Solicitor is instructed to hold back the cost of the improvements until release of funds is approved by RMG • Timeline from close date to complete improvements: — Uninsurable: 180 Days — Insured/Insurable: 270 Days Requirements to Release Funds • Require satisfactory completion of improvements as outlined in the approval quotes. Satisfactory confirmation to be obtained as follows: — Improvements value > $15,000 and/or borrower completed work themselves – an inspection from an RMG approved appraiser confirming all work has been completed. — Improvements value </= $15,000 – satisfactory before and after photographs and paid invoices confirming approved work is completed as outlined in the original quotes. Full appraisal may be required if documentation does not support this requirement. • Broker orders final inspection if applicable OR submits photos/invoices to be reviewed by RMG • Cost of inspection is borrower responsibility • Progress draws not available • RMG will instruct Solicitor to release the remaining funds to the borrower

See rate sheet for details Property Type Eligible: • Non owner-occupied properties: – Purchases – 2-4 units – Transfers – 1-4 units ▪ Transfers of one-unit properties must meet the following criteria: ◦ Insured - existing loan funded as owner-occupied property ◦ Insurable - existing loan funded prior to November 30th, 2016 • Readily marketable residential dwellings, located in markets with demonstrated ongoing re-sale demand • Minimum 750 sqft, apt condo 500 sq ft • Maximum 5 acres Ineligible: • Agricultural Land Reserve • Condo Conversions • Condos part of any type of condo-hotel or tied to a rental pool agreement Loan to Value Insured - Maximum 95% LTV (transfers only) Insurable - Maximum 80% LTV Minimum/ Maximum Loan Amount Insured/Insurable: • Minimum: $50K • Maximum: $799,999.99 Amortization • Minimum 5 years • Maximum 25 years Down Payment From own resources (no gifts) CMHC Insurable Loans - partial gifted down payment allowed, minimum 10% to be from own resources Qualification Requirements Minimum Credit Score: • Minimum 650 for all applicants • No bankruptcy or judgments Debt Service Calculations: • GDS/TDS 39%/44% Rental Program RMGbrokersupport@RMGmortgages.ca www.RMGmortgages.ca 1-866-809-5800 December 2024 Mortgage approval is subject to our policies and guidelines. Products subject to change without notice. RMG Mortgages is a division of MCAP Financial Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10600 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11790 RMG Insured/Insurable Rental Program allows borrowers to obtain financing for the purchase of a property or transfer of an existing mortgage which will be fully rented. Product Details Rental Property Qualifications Rental Income and debt servicing guidelines SUBJECT rental property: • 50% of rent added to income • 100% of Condo Fees OTHER rental property(s): • Option 1: – 50% of rent added to income – PI + 50% of condo fees included in TDS • Option 2: – Net rental income - via net rental calculator OR – T776 Statement of Real Estate Rentals – Exclude PI + condo fees from TDS Taxes and heat can be excluded for subject and non-subject properties. Calculating Net Rental Income Net Rental Income can be calculated using one of the following: • T776 Statement of Real Estate Rentals – Add back the actual amount of interest expense and subtract the annual PI payment • Net Rental Income Calculator supported by active lease/tenancy agreements • Net surplus – add to other income • Net deficit – add to liabilities or deduct from borrower’s income Documentation Requirements Rents Added to Annual income: • One of the following: – Active Leases or Tenancy Agreements – Economic Rent from an RMG approved appraiser Net Rental Income: • Net Rental Income Calculator supported by Active lease agreements or Economic Rents from an RMG approved appraiser OR • Both of the following: – Active Leases or Tenancy Agreements – Based on length of ownership: Recent 1-2 years T1 Generals supported by NOAs Other • Maximum 5 rental properties within MCAP group of companies • Maximum 10 properties inclusive of all borrowers and property types (owner occupied, secondary homes and rentals) • Appraisal Rebate Program available • Available with Secure Start (mortgage life insurance) • Portable, assumable and transferable upon approval

This product is one more option available to RMG clients who wish to access the equity in their home! Program Amortization • 5 years minimum • 30 years maximum Borrower Qualification • Minimum credit score of 650 for all applicants • GDS/TDS 39%/44% - Based on loan amount for ALL Components (1st Charge + RMG Safeguard) • No bankruptcy or judgments • No major derogatory credit • Guarantors must reside in the subject property Qualification Rate The RMG Safeguard will be qualified at the greater of: • The contract rate + 2% or • The Minimum Qualifying Rate • Current payment on RMG 1st mortgage along with qualifying payment on RMG Safeguard will be used to calculate GDS/TDS Home Warranty Not available on RMG Safeguard Mortgage Mortgage Life Insurance Not available Pre-payment Privileges • Lump sum up to 20% of the original principal amount (minimum $100) • 20% increase in payment, incremental fixed amount can be added to principal and interest payment Early Payout • RMG Safeguard Fixed: up to 5th anniversary, greater of 3 months interest or IRD • RMG Safeguard ARM: 3 months interest (at prevailing interest rate or RMG Prime, whichever is greater) Property Taxes • Property Taxes are collected from the bank account assigned to the 1st mortgage charge • Client also has option to pay their own property taxes through the municipality Renewals / Conversions • Existing RMG Safeguard can renew into RMG Safeguard Fixed or RMG Safeguard ARM • RMG Safeguard ARM can convert into RMG Safeguard Fixed Assumptions • Available - must assume both the 1st and RMG Safeguard at the same time • Assumption fee waived Portability (Existing RMG Safeguard) • Not available for RMG Safeguard • Can Port & Increase existing RMG 1st Mortgage using additional funds to pay out RMG Safeguard Other • Some Safeguard mortgages may be subject to a rate premium. To determine if a rate premium applies to your client’s mortgage, please visit the Resource section of Gateway for the Safeguard Rate Premium Calculator • Cannot transfer the first mortgage to another institution without paying out the RMG Safeguard • Broker commission of 110bps + incentive based on the approved RMG Safeguard loan amount. • Legal fees paid by borrower

RMG Mortgages is a division of MCAP Financial Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10600 Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11790 *Please see product fact sheet for details. **Some exceptions available for transfer ins. See Transfer Program fact sheet for details. Subject to change without notice With RMG’s Secondary Home Program, financing up to 95% loan-to-value can be obtained for the purchase of a secondary residence that will be occupied at some point during the year in addition to a current primary residence. Program Details Eligible RMG Products* • Insured, Insurable, Uninsurable • Fixed Rate: 3-5 Year Term • 3 and 5 Year ARM • Low Rate Basic Fixed/ARM • RMG Safeguard Purpose • Purchase, Refinance, Purchase Plus Improvements, Transfer Property Type • Eligible properties must be owner-occupied (or occupied by a relative on a rent-free basis) up to a maximum of 1 unit • Property must be suitable for and available for year round occupancy, properties located on an island must have a year-round bridge or ferry access, borrower’s ability to occupy the property must not be restricted or limited at any time. • Some examples of features suitable properties typically have include: – A permanent heat source – Acceptable year-round water source – A permanent primary source of electricity, please note that generators are considered a supplementary source of electricity – Insulated for year-round occupancy – Indoor kitchen and bathroom facilities – Accessible year-round by a vehicle that is licensed for use on municipal, provincial and territorial roadways. Note: Vacation properties are eligible as long as they meet all the property requirements above. Vacation properties such as time-share interests, life leases, properties in rental pools, properties constructed for seasonal use or with seasonal access, and mobile homes are not eligible. Loan Amount • Minimum : $50,000 – Low Rate Basic Fixed/ARM - $75,000 • Maximum : – Insured - $1,374,999 – Insurable - $799,999 – Uninsurable based on maximum LTV tiers for property location and Product / Program selected Maximum Loan-to-Value (LTV) • Purchase / Transfer - 95% LTV • Refinance - 80% LTV Down Payment Gifted or own resources Mortgage Insurance All insured Secondary Home Mortgages must be insured through CMHC, Sagen or Canada Guaranty and approvals received prior to commitment. GDS/TDS Ratios GDS/TDS 39%/44% Credit Score • Fixed and ARM - 600 minimum (650 - property value > = $1 million) • Refinance - Minimum 650 credit score for all applicants Secondary Home Program RMGbrokersupport@RMGmortgages.ca www.RMGmortgages.ca 1-866-809-5800 December 2024 Mortgage approval is subject to our policies and guidelines. Pre-approvals Not eligible for pre-approvals Maximum Amortizations ** • Uninsurable – 30 years • Insured/Insurable - 25 years • 30 years permitted for Insured only under the following conditions: – Property is owner occupied by an immediate family member AND either: ▪Minimum one borrower is a First Time Homebuyer OR ▪New construction property Payment Options Monthly, semi-monthly, bi-weekly, weekly 20% + 20% Prepayment Privilege • Lump sum up to 20% of the original principal amount (minimum $100) • 20% increase in payment, incremental fixed amount can be added to principal and interest payment Early Payout Early Payout Premium: • Fixed Rate: – 1–5 Year - greater of 3 months interest or Interest Rate Differential • ARM: – 3 and 5 Year Term - 3 months interest based on greater of contract rate or RMG Prime • Low Rate Basic: – Fixed - greater of 3% of the remaining mortgage balance or Interest Rate Differential – ARM - 3% of the remaining mortgage balance Other • Available with Secure Start (mortgage life insurance) • Portable and assumable upon approval

entries.text: November, 2023 Mortgage approval is subject to our policies and guidelines. Products subject to change without notice. RMG Mortgages is a division of MCAP Financial Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10600 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11790 RMGbrokersupport@RMGmortgages.ca www.RMGmortgages.ca 1 866 809 5800 SMART Reasons to bring your next deal to RMG Mortgages Details Low Rate Basic • Designed for clients that intend to last the duration of the entire term • Insured and Insurable loans only. (Refinances offered to existing RMG Low Rate Basic clients only) • 20/20 prepayment privilege • Should the client break the mortgage a 3% penalty or IRD will result. If they come back to RMG on the new transaction however within 90 days, we will provide a sizeable rebate back to the client to normalize what the penalty would have been if its was greater of 3 months interest or IRD. Broker Portal • RMG now has a broker portal (Gateway) where you can look over the details of your deals all in realtime. Appraisal Compensation • RMG may require an appraisal on the following property types: private sales, rural properties, double ended transactions and power of sale / foreclosure / spousal-partner buyouts. • An EFT will be transmitted post funding upon receipt of a paid invoice uploaded to the file on Gateway subject to the following based on mortgage amount: - < $200,000= no rebate - $200,000 to $299,999 = up to $250 rebate - $300,000 to $399,999 = up to $350 rebate - $400,000 and up = up to $400 rebate • Appraisal rebate requests must be processed within 6 months of invoice date. – Uninsurable increase and blend funded deals NOT eligible for Appraisal Rebate Program – Effective June 1st, 2023, all uninsurable deals will no longer qualify for appraisal rebates. RMG will continue to cover AVM costs as required. Competitive Cash Back Program • Cash back can also be used on a purchase transaction for closing costs or to payout debts. • We segment our options where you can choose from 1%, 1.5%, 2% and 3% Extended Amortization Extended Amortization up to 30 years available on uninsurable loans, please contact your BDM for details. Competitive Standard & Collateral Transfer Programs • RMG Mortgages covers the FCT Title Insurance Policy, Appraisal fee, Legals and up to $250 in discharge fees. • Capitalize up to $3000.00 for accrued interest, admin/transfer fees pre payment penalties & legal fees (Collateral). Government Grant Program Depending on the location, some municipalities offer government grant loans. RMG will consider these types of grants as a source of down payment. November, 2023 Mortgage approval is subject to our policies and guidelines. Products subject to change without notice. RMG Mortgages is a division of MCAP Financial Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10600 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11790 RMGbrokersupport@RMGmortgages.ca www.RMGmortgages.ca 1 866 809 5800 SMART Reasons to bring your next deal to RMG Mortgages Details RMG Eclipse • Consider RMG Eclipse for your non-conforming deals with beacons as low as 500 and allowing 50 / 50 DSR • 1-day discharged from bankruptcy or Eclipse can payout consumer proposal, property tax and income tax arrears • Penalty is greater of IRD on posted or 3-months interest • No property tax holdback, no management fee`s and no bona fide sales clause • Ask for you BDM for full details Spousal Buyouts • RMG will consider a private purchase up to 95% LTV to remove a registered partner/spouse. • Documents required: Finalized separation agreement, offer to purchase • Full appraisal regardless of LTV Bridge Loans • RMG will provide Bridge loans at Prime + 2% with a $300 administration fee ($200 RMG admin fee + $100 FCT admin fee). • The net equity minus real-estate costs will be eligible for advance. • Maximum 120 days • Bridge advances in excess of $150,000.00 or greater than 90 days must be secured by way of a collateral mortgage. (60 days for Manitoba) • Minimum bridge $5000 Lending Area and Square Footage • Square footage min of 750 (condo’s 500) or less in major urban centers • No restricted lending areas (except in QC, NT, YT, NU) • Maximum insurable loan $924,999.99 • Maximum uninsurable loan $950,000 (Exceptions on larger loans are considered on a case by case basis, contact your BDM) Purchase Plus and Improvements • CMHC Insured: Can exceed $40,000 but not over 10% of the “as improved” value • Sagen/CG Insured: Can exceed 20% or $40,000 of the “initial” value of the property, as supported by the insurer • Uninsurable: up to 20% of “initial” value up to maximum of $40,000 (whichever is less) • Progress draws not available FCT Platinum refinance program Contact your Business Development Manager for provincial guidelines November, 2023 Mortgage approval is subject to our policies and guidelines. Products subject to change without notice. RMG Mortgages is a division of MCAP Financial Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10600 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11790 RMGbrokersupport@RMGmortgages.ca www.RMGmortgages.ca 1 866 809 5800 SMART Reasons to bring your next deal to RMG Mortgages Details Second Home Purchase • We will allow up to 95% LTV on a second home owner occupied purchase • Single family only (1 unit) • No rental income can be considered for qualifying purposes • Amortization 5 - 25 years for insurable / 5 - 30 years for uninsurable • Vacation, recreational or cottage properties are eligible. Must be Type A properties only. Payments, Prepayments and Property Tax Flexibility • RMG allows the clients to pay their own taxes through the municipality, or have it deducted with the mortgage regardless of the LTV. • All payment frequencies are setup for accelerated, however non- accelerated can be requested • IAD adjustments are available to line up the payment date to the clients preference • An increase in payment of 20% can be made at any time post funding, as well as Lump Sum payments in increments of $100 or more each time by contacting our Servicing Team. Online Customer Portal MyRMG Customer Portal allows customer self service Home System Service Plan ( Home Appliance Plan - Alberta ) • Covers eligible home repairs (appliance repairs for Alberta ) up to $10,000 annually • Free for 1st 6 months • Hassle free cancellations • Not available in: SK, QC, NF, NB, NWT, NU, YU Contact your BDM for details BFS - Less than 2 years The RMG BFS Program is designed to help previously employed new business owners obtain mortgage financing to Purchase a home or Transfer their existing mortgage. (CMHC High Ratio & Insurable loans ONLY).

The Broker is required to answer any mortgage related questions, including any of the mortgage processes as well as questions pertaining to legal documents. Property Standard property guidelines apply • Owner occupied: 1-4 units • Secondary Homes: 1 unit only • Rentals (Insured/Insurable only): 1-4 units - Single unit rentals must meet the below criteria: Д Insured - original loan funded as owner occupied property Д Insurable - original loan funded prior to November 30th, 2016 Products • Insured/Insurable/Uninsurable • Standard 3-5 Year Fixed • 3 and 5 Year Standard ARM • Low Rate Basic Fixed/ARM • Rentals: - Insured/Insurable only - Standard 5 year fixed/ARM only Minimum/Maximum Loan Amounts Minimum: • Standard Fixed/ARM - $45K • Second Home & Rentals - $50K • Low Rate Basic - $75K Maximum: • Insurable Low Ratio - $799,999 • Insured High Ratio - $1,374,999 • Uninsurable - $2M* *Max loan amount is subject to property location. Capitalization You may capitalize accrued interest, administrative/transfer fees and pre-payment penalties as per the following: • Maximum of $3,000.00. New mortgage not to exceed the amount originally registered. Fees RMG Mortgages cover the cost of the FCT Title Insurance Policy and legal’s, and will pay up to $250 in discharge fee’s. Appraisal If an appraisal is required, it must be ordered through an approved RMG Appraiser. Appraisal Rebate Program is available - upload invoice on Gateway. Appraisal rebate requests must be processed within 6 months of invoice date. RMG Mortgages is a division of MCAP Financial Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10600 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11790 RMGbrokersupport@RMGmortgages.ca www.RMGmortgages.ca 1 866 809 5800 Broker Information Sheet Transfer-In/Switches FCT Requirements After FCT has received information from RMG Mortgages, FCT will call the customer to arrange a convenient time to sign the required documentation. FCT will replace the follow-up process that the Brokers were previously responsible for, working with the RMG Mortgages Funding Team. This will not include managing the customer’s expectations and questions. The FCT Remote Signor will also be responsible for arranging a second customer appointment if there are any documents that are missing or have been filled out incorrectly. Funding the Files The funds are disbursed directly to FCT, then forwarded to the existing mortgage holder. Should there be a delay in the funding date (outside of our payout statement date), RMG Mortgages will deduct per diems from the customer’s account via a one-time EFT payment. Per diem is the daily interest charged on a customer’s current mortgage balance. Taxes and Penalties Any payout penalties are the responsibility of the customer and RMG Mortgages requires the funds to be in the form of a certified cheque or money order. The remote signor will collect these outstanding funds at the time of the customer’s appointment. Service Level Agreement Our commitment to our brokers/customers is to process mortgages that are transferred in from another lender within a three-week turnaround time (with the dependency that all appropriate documentation and information is provided as required, with alignment of the schedules of all parties involved.)

entries.text: October 2023 *Mortgage approval is subject to RMG Mortgages policies and guidelines. Please note that RMG Mortgages does not operate in Québec. RMG Mortgages is a division of MCAP Financial Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10600 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11790 RMGbrokersupport@RMGmortgages.ca www.RMGmortgages.ca 1 866 809 5800 RMG Transfer Sheet RMG Transfer Sheet Transfers If the loan qualifies for AVM, your underwriter will automatically select it and RMG will cover the cost. On the chance that the property isn’t AVM eligible, use RMG’s appraisal rebate program to cover the cost of the appraisal. Use the RMG Transfer Program when a homeowner wants to transfer their standard mortgage charge from another approved Canadian financial institution. • Did you know we’ll cover up to $250 of the other lender’s discharge fee? That’s in addition to covering the legal fees. • Does your client have a HELOC behind their standard charge first mortgage that they want to keep? That HELOC can remain in place and won’t jump to first when we pay out the first mortgage. A transfer is done when RMG takes an assignment of the first mortgage charge so priority doesn’t change. • Cap up to $3,000 in accrued interest, pre-payment penalties and other administrative fees on to the mortgage. That means in most cases, there are no out of pocket cost to the clients Easy valuation process: • If the mortgage you’re transferring is currently insured, no property valuation is required • If we’re transferring an insurable or uninsurable mortgage: ° If the loan qualifies for AVM, your underwriter will automatically select it and RMG will cover the cost. ° On the chance that the property isn’t AVM eligible, use RMG’s appraisal rebate program to cover the cost of the appraisal Collateral Transfers The Collateral Charge “Transfer” Program can be used to transfer in two separate charges into one standard mortgage charge. The mortgage must be insurable or uninsurable and the charges must come from approved Canadian financial institutions. • RMG will cover up to $250 of the other lender’s discharge fee. We will only cover one discharge fee if transferring two charges. • The homeowner can cover the legal costs with First Canadian Title one of two ways: ° Client covers the legal costs. Standard rates and commission apply. Legal costs can be capped back onto the mortgage. ° RMG covers the legal costs and the commission will be reduced by 10 bps. • Cap up to $3,000 in accrued interest, pre-payment penalties and other administrative fees on to the mortgage. Easy valuation process: • If the loan qualifies for AVM, your underwriter will automatically select it and RMG will cover the cost. • If an appraisal is needed, use RMG’s appraisal rebate program to help cover the cost. RMG Transfer Tips Common Transfer mistakes and how to avoid them: 1. To help speed up the process for your clients, make sure you know what kind of charge the current mortgage has before submitting. Closings get delayed when we find out part way through the process that what we thought was a standard transfer should be a collateral transfer. 2. Don’t send in a transfer closing in a week. We need time to close a transfer. It takes a minimum of 3-4 weeks to complete the entire process. 3. Double-check the client’s contact information. FCT needs proper contact info to set up the signing meeting. 4. Don’t let the client ignore the other lender. Coach your client to ask for an open term. If the transfer doesn’t close on time, this protects the client from getting locked into a new term. 5. Check the amortization (am) on the original deal. If the client prepaid their mortgage or accelerated payments, the am remaining may not match what was registered minus the time elapsed. Example: Client’s mortgage was registered 5 years ago with a 25-year am. Client made prepayments. Current am on mortgage statement is now 13 years. However, you can submit the am at 20 on the transfer since that’s what was registered minus time elapsed. October 2023 *Mortgage approval is subject to RMG Mortgages policies and guidelines. Please note that RMG Mortgages does not operate in Québec. RMG Mortgages is a division of MCAP Financial Corporation Ontario Mortgage Brokerage #10600 | Ontario Mortgage Administrator #11790 RMGbrokersupport@RMGmortgages.ca www.RMGmortgages.ca 1 866 809 5800 RMG Transfer Sheet RMG Transfer Sheet Grandfathering Do you know the grandfathering rules when doing a transfer/collateral transfer? Using the grandfathering rules means you could access lower insured or insurable rates for your client. • Insured loans ° Qualify on the contract rate ° Value is based on the original purchase price – even if the current value is >$1M, if funded before Oct 16th, 2016 ° Existing amortization will be used even if greater than 25 years • Conventional Insurable loans funded before November 30, 2016 ° Contract rate can be used to qualify for 5-year fixed rate terms. All other terms qualify at the greater of the contract rate + 2% OR the minimum qualifying rate ° Value is based on current value – even if current value is > $1M Conventional insurable loans funded after November 29, 2016 must qualify under current insurability guidelines to obtain insurable rates. Otherwise, uninsurable rates apply. Amortization – Collateral Transfers Confused about what am to use? Insured Insurable Uninsurable • Minimum: 5 years • Maximum: Original scheduled amortization minus the time elapsed since the original mortgage was registered • Minimum: 5 years • Maximum: 25 years • The amortization is not to exceed the original amortization minus the time elapsed since the original mortgage was registered Where 2 charges are being transferred to one first mortgage charge: Amortization not to exceed the remaining amortization of the primary amortizing charge (or the lesser of the amortizations where multiple components exist) • Minimum: 5 years • Maximum: 30 years • The remaining amortization may be extended to 30 years at time of transfer

entries.text: Financial Institution/Lender information: I To Tel No., Request for Mortgage Discharge Statement Date: I Fax No. NOTICE OF CONFIDENTIALITY The information transmitted is intended only for the person or entity to which it is addressed and may contain confidential and/or privileged material. Any review, re-transmission, dissemination or other use of, or taking of any action in reliance upon, this information by persons or entities other than the intended recipient is prohibited. If you received this in error, please contact the sender immediately by telephone (collect if required). A courier will be sent to retrieve the documents or, alternatively, immediately destroy this transmission, including all attachments, without copying, distributing or disclosing same. Consent for Electronic Disclosure: This is to confirm your request and consent for the enclosed information, documents and/or notices to be provided to you at the designated fax number indicated above in this electronic format only. We recommend that you keep a copy of this fax document for your records. Re: Mortgagor(s): Property Address: -------------------------------- Province: Existing Mortgage /Account No.: --------- Anticipated Payout Date: Mortgage Maturity Date: I/We hereby authorize you to provide a mortgage Discharge Statement for the above-noted mortgage/account to the service provider noted on the cover letter. The Discharge Statement should reflect the following information as of the date of this Request: The outstanding principal balance of the mortgage/account, accrued interest, the per diem rate at which interest accrues after the date of this Request, any tax account debit or credit, whether the loan is in good standing, and if the mortgage agreement contains a re-advanceable provision and/or if additional principal advances can be made after the date of this Request. The default insurer's reference number must also appear on the Discharge Statement, if applicable. The Discharge Statement should be prepared on the basis that any prepayment privilege has been applied prior to the calculation of any prepayment charges, if such prepayment privilege is permitted under the mortgage agreement upon full repayment. If there are multiple credit products associated with the mortgage security, please provide a separate Discharge Statement for each product. Renewal: • If the mortgage is due for renewal on or prior to the Anticipated Payout Date listed above, consider this Request your instructions to not renew the mortgage. Should you have to renew the mortgage, consider this Request your instructions to renew the mortgage for the shortest term available under the mortgage/renewal agreement that allows for prepayment without penalty. Re-advanceable Credit Products: • If the mortgage secures a line of credit or other re-advanceable credit product, accept this Request as my/our direction and authorization to: • upon receipt of this Request, freeze the credit limit on the product(s) so no further credit can be extended/utilized, pending receipt of payout funds; and • upon receipt of the payout funds, close any and all such credit lines or other re-advanceable credit product. Authorization to Release Information: • I/We authorize you to release to the service provider noted on the cover letter any information that its employees, agents or representatives may reasonably request with respect to the mortgage/account. • I/We authorize the service provider noted on the cover letter to release a copy of the Discharge Statement to my/our mortgage broker and to discuss the transaction with the mortgage broker, when applicable. After the payout has been received, please forward the discharge documentation to the service provider noted on the cover letter. Instructions on where to send the discharge documentation will also be provided with the payout funds. Borrower Borrower 1347616 (01/23)
