entries.text: SMA Product Factsheet Business for Self Plus (BFS+) Program ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. All offers and programs referenced in this Factsheet can be changed or withdrawn at any time without notice and may not be combined with other offers, programs or promotions. All applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and, if applicable, mortgage insurers’ lending criteria. Scotiabank can amend any of the features, options, benefits or requirements contained in this Factsheet without notice. The information in this Factsheet is accurate as of the date of printing and is subject to change (12/24) Page 1 of 2 KEY FEATURES This program provides additional mortgage solutions to clients with moderate personal incomes who also have income in their operating companies. This program allows the use of the operating company’s Net Income After Taxes (NIAT) to lower the applicant’s TDSR. FAST FACTS Borrower Qualifications • Applicant must be permanent Canadian Resident, paying taxes in Canada • Business must be Canadian registered and pay Canadian taxes • Clients with incorporated companies only. • All the shareholders of the company must be on the mortgage. • To use NIAT, the business must be 100% owned by the borrower(s) on the application Maximum Net Income After Tax (NIAT) Allowance • Based on Scotiabank’s internal credit rating system, we will consider an allowance of 40% - 60% of NIAT less dividends paid. Your Broker Relationship Manager or Broker Lending Manager will advise. Eligible Loan Purpose • Purchase • Refinance • Switch • Switch with additional funds All transactions are uninsured only and available under the Scotia Total Equity® Plan Eligible Properties • Primary Residence • Secondary Residence / Vacation Property homes • Rental Properties Maximum Loan to Value Ratio TDSR <=60% Max LTV 80% TDSR >60% Max LTV 65% • No ACE exceptions permitted Scotia Total Equity Plan (STEP Program) • Maximum Scotia STEP Global Limit is 80% of lending value Amortization • Maximum 30 years Down Payment • Standard requirements apply SMA Product Factsheet Business for Self Plus (BFS+) Program ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. All offers and programs referenced in this Factsheet can be changed or withdrawn at any time without notice and may not be combined with other offers, programs or promotions. All applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and, if applicable, mortgage insurers’ lending criteria. Scotiabank can amend any of the features, options, benefits or requirements contained in this Factsheet without notice. The information in this Factsheet is accurate as of the date of printing and is subject to change (12/24) Page 2 of 2 Documentation Requirements • Personal: 2 years Notice of Assessment and 2 years T1 Generals • The mortgage and property title must be in the individual borrower’s name, not a company name. Note: RRSP withdrawals and foreign sources of income are not eligible. and all the following: • Business: 2 years of professional accountant prepared financial statements including a signed ‘Notice to Reader’ and compilation engagements for fiscal periods ending on or after Dec. 14, 2021 • Articles of incorporation or corporate search from provincial registry or T2 and schedule 50 • Confirmation of business activities to help understand:  What the company does  What industry the company is in  How long the company has been in business Note: Business must show positive income in all years of evaluation period and all documentation must be provided at time of application Payment Frequencies • Any day monthly (except 29th,30th, and 31st), weekly, bi-weekly Scotia Mortgage Protection • Life • Critical Illness • Disability • Job Loss Exclusions • Pre-approvals • Sole Proprietorships and Partnerships • Mobile Homes Mortgages under any other specialty financing programs:  StartRight® for Permanent Residents  StartRight® for Temporary Residents  Scotia Secondary Home Financing® Program  Family Home Purchase Program  Purchase Plus Improvements

entries.text: SMA Product Factsheet Instant Funding Program ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. Mortgage applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s and, if applicable, mortgage insurer’s standard credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and maximum permitted loan amounts. Scotiabank can amend, modify, or change any of the features, options, benefits, features, or requirements contained in this Factsheet at any time without notice. (04/22) Page 1 of 2 KEY FEATURES • No lawyer/notary required (all provinces except Quebec) for eligible transactions; all closing documents can be signed at Scotiabank branch • Fixed fee structure offers a cost-effective option to customers. Incentives may be available where program fees are covered by Scotiabank. FAST FACTS Eligible Properties • Residential properties with a maximum of 4 units Eligible Loan Purposes • Single advance refinances, including switch with additional funds Eligible Programs • Scotia Total Equity ® Plan (Scotia STEP Program) • Standalone Mortgage Documentation Requirements • Spousal Status Questionnaire • Recent account statement for unsecured debts to be paid down or paid out and closed from loan proceeds • A Request for Mortgage Statement for each registered mortgage (non-BNS) to be paid out with loan proceeds • New Brunswick only: Customer’s birth certificate; if unavailable, it can be obtained by vendor at an additional cost. Requesting Instant Funding Program • Submit your application and include a comment in Broker Notes: “Instant Funding Program” or “IFP deal” • Provide your client with a copy of the Fee Disclosure Schedule located on the Scotia Mortgage Authority® website (www.scotiamortgageauthority.com) found in the ‘Tools + Resources’ - ‘Forms’ section • Confirm with your client the following to ensure the appropriate documentation is completed and include comments in your notes:  Are there any existing Scotiabank mortgages or secured lines of credit registered on title? If yes, would the client like us to pay them out?  Are there mortgages, secured lines of credit or other secured credit products with any other financial institution tied to the property? If the client is not sure, they should contact their other financial institution to confirm. All credit products with any other financial institution secured against the property will have to be paid out and the mortgage(s) securing them will have to be discharged. • Complete the applicable Spousal Status Questionnaire, Request for Mortgage Statement, and Payout Statement Authorization form located on the Scotia Mortgage Authority® website (www.scotiamortgageauthority.com) found in the ‘Tools + Resources’ - ‘Forms’ section and submit the completed form with your application, along with valid property insurance policy and the standard documents required to underwrite your deal • Confirm your client’s legal names on the application are consistent with the way they appear on title and advise Scotiabank of any discrepancies. All parties on title to the property are required to be included on the mortgage and must sign all documents relating to the transaction • Confirm the legal description and address of your client’s property and clearly indicate these details when you submit your deal. A copy of your client’s tax bill will reveal the legal description needed to move your deal along smoothly • All outstanding conditions must be satisfied prior to requesting Instant Funding Program documentation SMA Product Factsheet Instant Funding Program ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. Mortgage applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s and, if applicable, mortgage insurer’s standard credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and maximum permitted loan amounts. Scotiabank can amend, modify, or change any of the features, options, benefits, features, or requirements contained in this Factsheet at any time without notice. (04/22) Page 2 of 2 • If the title search reveals discrepancies, Scotiabank may contact you to discuss the options to resolve the title defects. It may be necessary to contact the client for more information and/or further clarification and the transaction may no longer qualify for the Instant Funding Program. • Advise the client that they are responsible for any prepayment charges applicable to the repayment of their existing mortgage(s) with their financial institution, including Scotiabank and that they should verify the prepayment charges with their financial institution before closing. Client Signing • Advise your client they will be required to meet with a Client Solutions Advisor (CSA) at a Scotiabank branch of their choice to review and sign all applicable documents; the CSA will contact the client to book an appointment. Exclusions • Purchases • Switches (with no additional funds) • Loans with secondary financing • Out of province • For a full list of exclusions, contact your Broker Relationship Manager or Broker Lending Manager.

FAST FACTS Customer Eligibility • Must have a new or existing BNS Day
entries.text: SMA Product Factsheet Scotia Mortgage+ Program ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. Scotiabank can amend, modify or change any of the options, benefits, features or requirements in this Factsheet at any time without notice. (09/23) Page 1 of 1 KEY FEATURES The Scotia Mortgage+ Program is a bundled mortgage offer focused on deepening the customer relationship at mortgage origination. When a customer obtains a new mortgage with Scotiabank, the Scotia Mortgage+ Program allows them to access a preferred mortgage rate as an incentive for expanding their banking relationship with us to more than just the mortgage. FAST FACTS Customer Eligibility • Must have a new or existing BNS Day-to-Day account with a pre-authorized BNS mortgage payment, plus at least one other retail or small business solution in place prior to mortgage signing documents being issued (not including an additional mortgage component or real estate secured line of credit). • For joint mortgage applications, Day-to-Day, retail, and small business solutions in single borrower's name are eligible for the offer. • Optional Creditor Insurance is available on all borrowing solutions. • New mortgage must be under STEP. Available Solutions and Deal Types • New purchase, switch and BNS refinance applications. • Closed-term mortgage solutions. • All property types are eligible for the offer. • Standard rate-hold policies apply. Additional Notes • Broker should indicate that their customer is interested in the Mortgage+ program in their file submission notes. • If the customer opts to not open the required solutions, the application will be re-adjudicated at standard rates.

entries.text: SMA Product Factsheet Total Net Worth Lending Program ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. All offers and programs referenced in this Factsheet can be changed or withdrawn at any time without notice and may not be combined with other offers, programs or promotions. All applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and, if applicable, mortgage insurers’ lending criteria. Scotiabank can amend any of the features, options, benefits or requirements contained in this Factsheet without notice. The information in this Factsheet is accurate as of the date of printing and is subject to change. (12/24) Page 1 of 3 KEY FEATURES This program is designed to provide additional mortgage solutions to customers with moderate incomes who have a significant amount of verifiable liquid assets and strong credit scores. Credit will be reviewed using Scotiabank®’s internal rating system; BRM/BLM will advise. FAST FACTS Borrower Qualifications • Canadian Resident, claiming taxes in Canada • Partial percentage of liquid assets may be utilized in joint ownership situations where a co-owner of the assets is not a borrower on the mortgage application. Asset Requirements • Minimum liquid assets of $250,000 • Must have $1 in liquid assets for every $1 in mortgage needed above standard qualified amount (i.e., if a client qualifies for a $400,000 mortgage using standard debt servicing ratios but requires a mortgage of $950,000, they must have verified liquid assets of $550,000 in addition to down payment.) • Assets must be unencumbered (not pledged as security for another credit facility)  Note: Pledged assets do not pertain to previously funded TNW applications • All account holders where assets are held must be on the mortgage • No foreign income can be used • No foreign assets can be used, except those expressly stated on the eligible asset list Eligible Assets • GIC • TFSA • ESOP • Mutual Funds • Chequing & Saving Accounts • Stocks of a publicly traded Canadian or U.S company • Stocks of a non-Canadian company owned within an investment account/portfolio held in a Canadian financial institution • U.S Government T-Bills, Treasure Notes, and Treasury Bonds • Canada/Provincial Savings Bonds • Equity in existing properties being sold (a firm purchase and sale agreement is required) • RIFs (discounted by 30%), only if RIF income is not used for income qualification • Segregated Funds (Guaranteed Investment Funds (GIFs) • Traded REIT Investments • Liquid assets held in holding companies, if the following requirements are met:  Transactions and business deals are limited to activities within Canada  Assets are liquid and align with the list of eligible assets  All owners of the Hold Co must be on the mortgage applications (Articles of Incorporation are required) • Retirement Accounts (discounted by 30% to account for withholding taxes) SMA Product Factsheet Total Net Worth Lending Program ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. All offers and programs referenced in this Factsheet can be changed or withdrawn at any time without notice and may not be combined with other offers, programs or promotions. All applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and, if applicable, mortgage insurers’ lending criteria. Scotiabank can amend any of the features, options, benefits or requirements contained in this Factsheet without notice. The information in this Factsheet is accurate as of the date of printing and is subject to change. (12/24) Page 2 of 3 Note: Locked-in accounts, margins accounts, panda bonds, cryptocurrency, non-traded REIT Investments, RESPs, assets held in a trust account, savings bonds from sanctioned countries, and equity in real estate that will continue to be owned are not considered eligible assets. Eligible Properties • Primary Residence • Secondary/Vacation Properties • Rental Properties Amortization • Maximum 30 years Eligible Loan Purposes • Purchase • Refinance • Switch and Switch with additional funds Note: All transactions are uninsured only. Maximum Loan to Value Ratio • 80% - If TDSR ≤ 60% (LTV sliding scales apply) • 65% - If TDSR > 60% (LTV sliding scales apply) Scotia Total Equity® Plan (Scotia STEP® Program) • Maximum Scotia STEP® Global Limit is 80% of lending value Documentation Requirements • Standard income confirmation and documentation policies apply. Applications with verifiable gross income of less than $50,000/year or income solely from RRSPs are ineligible. AND • Evidence that average liquid asset amount meets, or is in excess of, the liquid assets required, over a 12-month history of ownership (e.g. Annual Investment Statements, Quarterly Statements, Monthly Statements). Note: The minimum requirement is the lowest amount on deposit during the 12-month history. • If funds were recently transferred in from an eligible liquid margin account, the minimum requirement is 30 days. Supportive documentation must be obtained to confirm funds were previously held in an eligible margin account. • The most recent 30 days of history must show evidence the funds are on deposit at a Canadian Financial Institution. • Liquid Assets used to satisfy the net worth requirement cannot be used as investment income to qualify under the same Total Net Worth Lending application Down Payment Requirements • Down payment requirements are in addition to the liquid asset requirement • Minimum 20% (10% must be from own resources) • Gifted funds are not permitted • Secured secondary financing is not permitted Payment Frequencies • Any day monthly (except the 29th, 30th, and 31st), bi-weekly, or weekly SMA Product Factsheet Total Net Worth Lending Program ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. All offers and programs referenced in this Factsheet can be changed or withdrawn at any time without notice and may not be combined with other offers, programs or promotions. All applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and, if applicable, mortgage insurers’ lending criteria. Scotiabank can amend any of the features, options, benefits or requirements contained in this Factsheet without notice. The information in this Factsheet is accurate as of the date of printing and is subject to change. (12/24) Page 3 of 3 Scotia Mortgage Protection • Life • Critical Illness • Disability • Job Loss Exclusions • Mortgage default insurance • Mortgages under the following programs  StartRight® for Permanent Residents and Temporary Residents  Family Home Purchase Program  Purchase Plus Improvements  Mobile Homes

entries.text: SMA Product Factsheet Projected Income for Dentists, Veterinarians, and Optometrists Program ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. All offers and programs referenced in this Factsheet can be changed or withdrawn at any time without notice and may not be combined with other offers, programs or promotions. All applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and, if applicable, mortgage insurers’ lending criteria. Scotiabank can amend any of the features, options, benefits or requirements contained in this Factsheet without notice. The information in this Factsheet is accurate as of the date of printing and is subject to change. Default insurance premium calculations provided for information purposes only and are subject to change. (03/25) Page 1 of 3 KEY FEATURES A program that allows borrower qualification to be based on the use of future (projected) income for Dentists, Veterinarians, and Optometrists who are in their final year of residency, fellowship or study (as applicable) or are newly practicing and have completed their program within the last 12 months for veterinarians and optometrists, or within the last 24 months for dentists. FAST FACTS Borrower Qualifications • Dental/Veterinary/Optometry students who are in the final year of residency, fellowship, or recognized program of study in Canada, as applicable. • Newly practicing doctors earning income in a related field who completed their program of study within the following:  Newly practicing dentists within the last 24 months  Newly practicing veterinarians within the last 12 months  Newly practicing optometrists within the last 12 months Income Verification • Eligible borrowers are permitted to qualify based on their projected income for qualification purposes as indicated in the table below. Note: Verification of the applicant’s actual income is also required, following standard income verification policies. The source of income must be related to their field of study. Applications with zero income or income from unrelated fields are not permitted. Actual, verified income is required within the file. Profession Qualification Income Dentistry – General & Specialty $175,000 Veterinary Medicine $86,000 Optometry $96,000 Eligible Loan Purposes • Purchase • Refinance Eligible Properties • Owner occupied Principal Residence • Maximum of two units Maximum Loan to Value Ratio • Insured: 90% (LTV sliding scales apply) • Uninsured: Max LTV 80% Actual income is >=25% than the projected income used to qualify Max LTV 65% Actual income is <25% of the projected income used to qualify SMA Product Factsheet Projected Income for Dentists, Veterinarians, and Optometrists Program ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. All offers and programs referenced in this Factsheet can be changed or withdrawn at any time without notice and may not be combined with other offers, programs or promotions. All applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and, if applicable, mortgage insurers’ lending criteria. Scotiabank can amend any of the features, options, benefits or requirements contained in this Factsheet without notice. The information in this Factsheet is accurate as of the date of printing and is subject to change. Default insurance premium calculations provided for information purposes only and are subject to change. (03/25) Page 2 of 3  No exceptions to LTV are permitted.  ScotiaLine® with access card: 65% LTV Scotia Total Equity® Plan (Scotia STEP® Program) • Maximum Scotia STEP® Global Limit is 80% LTV of lending value Amortization • Up to 30 years for eligible applicants Payment Frequencies • Any day monthly (except 29th, 30th, or 31st), bi-weekly, or weekly Scotia Mortgage Protection • Life • Critical Illness • Disability • Job Loss Maximum GDSR/TDSR • Standard GDSR/TDSR requirements apply, based on projected income used to qualify • Debt for student loans and line of credit must be included in TDSR even if not currently under repayment  For student loans/SPSP/ScotiaLine® for Students: Calculate the payment using the minimum qualifying rate for 5- year term mortgages and a 15-year amortization.  For non-BNS student revolving credit products: Calculate the payment using 1.5% of the outstanding balance. Documentation Requirements • If in final year of residency/fellowship or study: Obtain confirmation of the applicant’s enrollment in residency / fellowship / program of study and identifying what year they are currently in.  For Optometrists: The applicant must be enrolled at either the University of Waterloo or University of Montreal. Exceptions will not be granted. OR • If newly practicing: Obtain confirmation of program completion. • For Dentists:  Confirmation of program completion of a Doctor of Dental Surgery program in Canada within the last 24 months is required. Confirmation must display date of completion. • For Veterinarians:  Confirmation of program completion of a Doctor of Veterinarian Medicine program in Canada within the last 12 months is required. Confirmation must display date of completion. • For Optometrists:  Confirmation of program completion of a Doctor of Optometry program from either University of Waterloo or University of Montreal within the last 12 months is required. Confirmation must display date of completion Down Payment • Insured: Minimum of 10% with a minimum of 5% of the lending value from own resources. • Uninsured: Minimum 20% with a minimum 15% of the lending value from own resources. Notes: For both insured and uninsured applications: SMA Product Factsheet Projected Income for Dentists, Veterinarians, and Optometrists Program ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. All offers and programs referenced in this Factsheet can be changed or withdrawn at any time without notice and may not be combined with other offers, programs or promotions. All applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and, if applicable, mortgage insurers’ lending criteria. Scotiabank can amend any of the features, options, benefits or requirements contained in this Factsheet without notice. The information in this Factsheet is accurate as of the date of printing and is subject to change. Default insurance premium calculations provided for information purposes only and are subject to change. (03/25) Page 3 of 3 • Secondary financing secured on the subject property is not permitted. • Gifted down payments are acceptable and may form all or part of the down payment. Exclusions • Mobile Homes • Progress Draw Mortgages • Mortgage applications under the following programs:  StartRight® Mortgage for Permanent and Temporary residents  Scotia Rental Property Financing® Program  Scotia Secondary Home Financing® Program  On Reserve Lending  Renovation Financing  Purchase Plus Improvements  Manufactured Homes

The applicant’s actual income may vary. The projected income will only be used if the applicant’s current income is lower than the estimated projected income amount. Mortgage applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s and, if applicable, mortgage insurer’s standard credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and maximum permitted loan amounts. Scotiabank can amend, modify or change any of the features, options, benefits or requirements contained in this Factsheet at any time without notice. (05/25) Page 1 of 4 KEY FEATURES A program that allows borrower qualification to be based on the use of future (projected) income for medical professionals who are in the process of completing or have recently completed their residency/fellowship within the last 36 months. FAST FACTS Borrower Qualifications • Medical professionals registered or enrolled in a recognized medical residency or fellowship in Canada • Newly practicing physicians earning income in a related field who began practicing within the last 36 months • Foreign-trained physicians who are licensed by a provincial college and are Canadian citizens or permanent residents can utilize projected income for 36 months post completion of their program Eligible Loan Purposes • Purchase • Refinance • Switch Eligible Properties • Owner occupied Principal Residence • 1- and 2-unit dwellings Maximum GDSR/TDSR • Standard GDSR/TDSR requirements apply, based on projected income used to qualify • Debt for student loans and lines of credit must be included in TDSR, even if not currently under repayment Maximum Loan to Value Ratio • Insured: 90% (LTV sliding scales apply) • Uninsured: Max LTV 80% Actual income is >=25% than the projected income used to qualify Max LTV 65% Actual income is <25% of the projected income used to qualify o No exceptions to LTV are permitted. o ScotiaLine® with access card: 65% LTV Scotia Total Equity® Plan (Scotia STEP® Program) • Maximum Scotia STEP® Global Limit is 80% of lending value Payment Frequencies: • Any day monthly (except the 29th, 30th, and 31st), bi-weekly, or weekly Amortization • Up to 30 years for eligible applicants Scotia Mortgage Protection • Life • Critical Illness • Disability • Job loss SMA Product Factsheet Projected Income for Physicians Program * The projected income is an average estimated based on available industry data and is subject to change. The applicant’s actual income may vary. The projected income will only be used if the applicant’s current income is lower than the estimated projected income amount. Mortgage applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s and, if applicable, mortgage insurer’s standard credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and maximum permitted loan amounts. Scotiabank can amend, modify or change any of the features, options, benefits or requirements contained in this Factsheet at any time without notice. (05/25) Page 2 of 4 Documentation Requirements • If in residency/fellowship: Obtain confirmation of enrolment of residency/fellowship including the medical specialty and identifying current year of enrollment Note: Exceptions will be considered for first year Medical Residents where confirmation from the provincial college website is not available before the mortgage closing (i.e., medical student is offered residency in March, mortgage closing date is May 1st, and residency begins in July). In such situations, obtain a letter of acceptance from the program provider, plus detail in the file verbal confirmation from the program coordinator/registrar office confirming acceptance and upcoming residency. OR • If newly practicing physician: Obtain confirmation of program completion including stream of specialization and date of completion, provided they completed their residency/fellowship program within the last 36 months OR • If newly practicing physician is foreign-trained and is within 24 month of completion or registered with a provincial college: Obtain confirmation of completion with specialization (if applicable) and confirmation of active college registration showing that client is legally entitled to practice in Canada Down Payment • Insured: Minimum 10% with a minimum 5% of the lending value from own resources. • Uninsured: Minimum 20% with a minimum 10% of the lending value from own resources. Notes: For both insured and uninsured applications: o Secondary financing secured on the subject property is not permitted. o Gifted down payments are acceptable and may form all or part of the down payment. Income Verification • Medical residents/fellows in first or second year are permitted to qualify based on a projected income of $185,000*, or • Physicians in at least their third year of residency/fellowship will be qualified based on projected income of $225,000*, or • Residents/fellows in their last year or newly practising physicians who completed their residency/fellowship within the last 36 months can qualify based on their field or specialization as indicated in the Projected Income for Qualification Purposes chart on the following page. • Note: Verification of the applicant’s actual income is also required, following standard income verification policies. The source of income must be related to their field of study. Applications with zero income or income from unrelated fields are not permitted. Document verification of actual, verified income within the file. Projected Income for Qualification Purposes • NOTE: If specialization is not reflected in the categories listed below, default projected income for qualification of $225,000.00 is to be used. Confirmation of enrollment/completion is still required. Currently enrolled Medical Residents/Fellows Qualification Income* Medical residents/fellows in their first and second year $185,000 Medical residents/fellows in at least their third year Family medicine - medical residents/fellows in their last year Medical residents/fellows in their last year $225,000 $225,000 Refer to medical specialty’s qualification income below Medical Specialities Anesthesiology $300,000 Cardiology $359,000 Cardiovascular / Thoracic Surgery $300,000 SMA Product Factsheet Projected Income for Physicians Program * The projected income is an average estimated based on available industry data and is subject to change. The applicant’s actual income may vary. The projected income will only be used if the applicant’s current income is lower than the estimated projected income amount. Mortgage applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s and, if applicable, mortgage insurer’s standard credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and maximum permitted loan amounts. Scotiabank can amend, modify or change any of the features, options, benefits or requirements contained in this Factsheet at any time without notice. entries.text: SMA Product Factsheet Purchase Plus Improvements ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. All offers and programs referenced in this Factsheet can be changed or withdrawn at any time without notice and may not be combined with other offers, programs or promotions. All applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and, if applicable, mortgage insurers’ lending criteria. Scotiabank can amend any of the features, options, benefits or requirements contained in this Factsheet without notice. The information in this Factsheet is accurate as of the date of printing and is subject to change. (02/25) Page 1 of 2 KEY FEATURES • This program allows for qualified homebuyers who want to make cosmetic improvements to their newly purchased property within 120 days of taking possession (basement finishing, bathroom fixtures, kitchen cabinets/counters, flooring, new windows, detached garage). • The ‘as-improved’ value is used to determine the maximum loan amount, subject to loan-to-value (LTV) limitations. FAST FACTS Eligible Properties • Uninsured: Owner-occupied properties: maximum of 2 units • Uninsured: Rental Properties • Uninsured: Scotia Secondary Home Financing Program  Type A & B properties: maximum 1 unit • Insured: Owner-occupied properties: maximum 4 units • Insured: Rental properties with maximum 80% LTV (one unit must be owner-occupied) • Insured: Scotia Secondary Home® Financing Program  Type A properties: maximum 4 units  Type B properties: maximum 1 unit Eligible Loan Purposes • Purchase only Maximum Loan to Value (LTV) Ratio (Mortgage default insurance fees and premiums may apply) • Standalone Mortgage • Uninsured 1-2 Units: 80% LTV • Insured 1-2 Units: 95% LTV • Insured 3-4 Units: 90% LTV Scotia Total Equity® Plan (Scotia STEP® Program) • Maximum Scotia STEP® Global Limit is 80% of lending value • One mortgage component only until mortgage is 100% complete and advanced Payment Frequencies • Any day monthly (except the 29th, 30th, and 31st), bi-weekly, and weekly Scotia Mortgage Protection • Life • Critical Illness • Disability • Job loss Amortization • Up to 30 years for eligible applicants Maximum Number of Advances • Maximum 2 advances, including property purchase, to be completed within 120 days of the first advance SMA Product Factsheet Purchase Plus Improvements ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. All offers and programs referenced in this Factsheet can be changed or withdrawn at any time without notice and may not be combined with other offers, programs or promotions. All applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and, if applicable, mortgage insurers’ lending criteria. Scotiabank can amend any of the features, options, benefits or requirements contained in this Factsheet without notice. The information in this Factsheet is accurate as of the date of printing and is subject to change. (02/25) Page 2 of 2 Down Payment • Down payment requirements are based on the ‘as-improved’ value of the property • Standard requirements apply Appraisal Requirements for Uninsured Deals • Full appraisal report, confirming both “as-is” and “as-complete” property values • All estimates and other supporting docs must be provided to appraiser to ensure the as-improved value is confirmed • An inspection must be ordered to ensure improvements are being completed prior to releasing the holdback Maximum Value of Improvements • Uninsured: Improvements cannot exceed 20% of the purchase price (‘as-is’ property value). • Sagen or CG Insured: Improvements cannot exceed 20% of the as-is’ property value • CMHC Insured: Improvements cannot exceed 10% of the ‘as-improved’ property lending value • Notes:  ‘As-is’ property value is the property lending value at time of purchase  ‘As-improved’ property value is equal to the lesser of the improved property value (as determined by the insurer), or the ‘as-is’ property value plus the value of the improvements. (Value of improvements is based on the direct costs associated with the improvements). Documentation Requirements • Official cost estimates for the improvements, supported by suitable plans or diagrams • Copies of the building plans and sketches • A complete breakdown of construction costs with copies of the building contract or subcontractor estimates Exclusions • Improvements to correct deficiencies, make the home livable, complete an addition, or repair structural concerns • When the value of improvements exceeds established program limits or falls under the threshold with two mortgage components • Scotiabank StartRight® for Temporary and Permanent Residents • The Long and Short® Mortgage • Refinances Note to Brokers: Finder fee will be issued once improvements are complete (or no longer required).

entries.text: SMA Product Factsheet Scotia® Rental Property Financing Program ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. All offers and programs referenced in this Factsheet can be changed or withdrawn at any time without notice and may not be combined with other offers, programs or promotions. All applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and, if applicable, mortgage insurers’ lending criteria. Scotiabank can amend any of the features, options, benefits or requirements contained in this Factsheet without notice. The information in this Factsheet is accurate as of the date of printing and is subject to change. (03/25) Page 1 of 2 KEY FEATURES This program allows financing on rental properties up to 4 units. FAST FACTS Borrower Qualifications • Minimum Beacon Score of 621 Eligible Properties • Existing or new (completion only) properties with a maximum of 4 units. Standard property criteria apply. • Properties in the following categories are NOT eligible:  Properties with a commercial component  Time-share ownership interests  Secondary/Vacation homes  Rooming houses  Quarter Interest or Shared Interest Ownership  Properties that are part of rental pool arrangements  Properties located in Fort McMurray, Alberta or within a 100km radius Eligible Loan Purposes • Purchase • Switch • Refinance Maximum Loan to Value Ratio • Up to 80% LTV • Up to 65% if ScotiaLine® with access card (1st mortgages only) Scotia Total Equity® Plan (Scotia STEP® Program) • Maximum Scotia STEP® Global Limit is 80% of lending value Amortization • Maximum 30 years Payment Frequencies • Any day monthly (except the 29th, 30th, or 31st), bi-weekly and weekly Net Worth Requirements • Overall personal net worth must be positive ($1 or higher). • Asset verification is not required. Scotia Mortgage Protection • Life • Critical Illness • Disability • Job Loss SMA Product Factsheet Scotia® Rental Property Financing Program ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. All offers and programs referenced in this Factsheet can be changed or withdrawn at any time without notice and may not be combined with other offers, programs or promotions. All applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and, if applicable, mortgage insurers’ lending criteria. Scotiabank can amend any of the features, options, benefits or requirements contained in this Factsheet without notice. The information in this Factsheet is accurate as of the date of printing and is subject to change. (03/25) Page 2 of 2 Rental Income Verification Rental income can be verified by collecting one of the following: • Notice of Assessment/CRA My Account Assessment (Proof of Income Statement) and corresponding T1 General, including Statement of Real Estate Rentals for the most recent taxation year, or • A full appraisal report with Market Rent Estimate or Schedule A from a Scotiabank-approved appraiser, or • Copies of current leases supported by three recent and consecutive months of bank account statements  Copies of leases without supporting bank account statements are acceptable only for purchase transactions where the existing tenant is being assumed. Note: For existing rental properties owned by the borrower, income verification must be completed by obtaining copies of the lease or CRA documentation; appraiser’s Schedule A or Market Rents are not acceptable as standard verification documents. Rental Income Requirements • For applications with rental income from the subject property: o 100% of verified subject property rental income must be included in the borrower’s gross income, and 85% of this subject property rental income will be used in capacity calculations. o 100% of the subject property expenses must be included in the application, and these subject property expenses will be used in capacity calculations. • Net rental income from a non-subject non-owner-occupied property is calculated as follows: (Gross Rental Income x 50%) – Mortgage PI. The resulting net rental income surplus and/or net rental income shortfall for the property will be included in the TDSR calculation. • Net rental income from a non-subject primary residence property with 2-4 units will be calculated as 50% of the gross rental income from the subject property and will be included in the borrower’s gross annual income. • Rental income for the rental properties that are not the subject property should be calculated using the Broker Form: Rental Analysis Worksheet under the ‘Forms’ tab on www.scotiamortgageauthority.com • Down Payment • Minimum 20% from borrower’s own resources  Gifted down payments and secondary financing are not acceptable unless provided in excess of the minimum 20% down payment Exclusions • Default insured mortgages • Scotia Secondary Home® Financing Program • Scotiabank StartRight® Mortgage Program • Mobile Homes • Pre-approvals • On Reserve Lending Program

entries.text: SMA Product Factsheet Scotia Secondary Home® Financing Program ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. Mortgage applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s and, if applicable, mortgage insurer’s standard credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and maximum permitted loan amounts. Scotiabank can amend, modify or change any of the features, options, benefits, features or requirements contained in this Factsheet at any time without notice. (12/24) Page 1 of 2 KEY FEATURES This program offers uninsured or default insured financing for secondary properties purchased for the purpose of meeting special family circumstances or work demands or acquiring vacation/leisure properties. FAST FACTS Property Classifications • Type A: Zoned and used as residential, rural, agricultural, or seasonal; has central heating, electrical supply, drinkable water supply, and a sanitary sewer/septic system; year-round road access • Type B: Central heating not required; seasonal road access or water only access is acceptable; non-winterized 4 Note: Please refer to the “Basic Borrower Guidelines” fact sheet for a comprehensive list of Scotiabank®’s minimum requirements for all properties. Borrower Qualifications • Standard borrower qualification and adjudication policies apply • The owner or relative must plan to occupy the property on a rent-free basis at some time during the year Eligible Properties • New or existing single-family dwellings, including condominium units • Single units only Eligible Loan Purposes • Purchase • Purchase Plus Improvements • Switch • Refinance Appraisal Requirements • Uninsured: Full appraisal required • Insured Type A: Proof of Type A status and year-round access (i.e., MLS, etc.) Maximum Loan to Value Ratio • Uninsured Type A & B: Up to 80% LTV • Insured Type A Purchase: Up to 95% LTV • Insured Type B: Up to 90% LTV Maximum Loan Amount • Uninsured: Maximum $1.5 million • Default Insured Type A:  Sagen: $700,000 for Toronto, Calgary, and Vancouver areas; $600,000 for the rest of Canada  CG and CMHC: standard limits apply • Insured Type B: Sagen only: $350,000 SMA Product Factsheet Scotia Secondary Home® Financing Program ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. Mortgage applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s and, if applicable, mortgage insurer’s standard credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and maximum permitted loan amounts. Scotiabank can amend, modify or change any of the features, options, benefits, features or requirements contained in this Factsheet at any time without notice. (12/24) Page 2 of 2 Scotia Total Equity® Plan (Scotia STEP® Program) • Maximum Scotia STEP Global Limit is up to 80% of lending value • STEP® is not available on insured Type B properties Amortization • Up to 30 years for eligible applicants Mortgage Default Insurance (Mortgage default insurance fees and premiums may apply) • Type A properties:  For Sagen and Canada Guaranty, maximum of 2 homeowner mortgage loans per borrower  For CMHC, there is only one CMHC default insurance mortgage loan allowed per borrower • Type B properties:  For Sagen only, maximum of 2 homeowner mortgage loans per borrower Payment Frequencies: • Any day monthly (except the 29th, 30th, and 31st), bi-weekly, or weekly Scotia Mortgage Protection • Life • Critical Illness • Disability • Job loss Down Payment • Uninsured Type A & B: Applicant must provide minimum equity of 20%, of which 15% must be provided from the applicant’s own resources; gifted down payment and other borrowing are acceptable for any part over 15% • Insured Type A: Applicant must provide minimum equity of 5% from own resources depending on purchase price gifted down payments are acceptable • Insured Type B: Minimum 10% from own resources; gifted down payments are not acceptable Exclusions • StartRight® for Permanent and Temporary Residents • Scotia Rental Property® Financing Program • Rental properties, investment, rental pool, or timeshare properties • Properties containing 2 or more units • High-ratio financing on Type B properties with 2 or more units • Projected Income: Dentists and Veterinarians program • Projected Income: Physicians program

entries.summary: SMA Product Factsheet Scotiabank StartRight®: Permanent and Temporary Resident Programs ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. Mortgage applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s and, if applicable, mortgage insurer’s standard credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and maximum permitted loan amounts. (02/25) Page 1 of 7 KEY FEATURES • The StartRight for Permanent Residents Program is a residential mortgage financing program for the purchase or switch (no additional funds) of owner-occupied properties for eligible new permanent
entries.text: SMA Product Factsheet Scotiabank StartRight®: Permanent and Temporary Resident Programs ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. Mortgage applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s and, if applicable, mortgage insurer’s standard credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and maximum permitted loan amounts. Scotiabank can amend, modify or change any of the options, benefits, features or requirements in this Factsheet at any time without notice. (02/25) Page 1 of 7 KEY FEATURES • The StartRight for Permanent Residents Program is a residential mortgage financing program for the purchase or switch (no additional funds) of owner-occupied properties for eligible new permanent residents with a bureau risk indicator (BRI) of C or better or no hit/no score. Conditions apply and may be amended from time to time. Qualifying criteria for this program are outlined below. The typical client for the Permanent Resident program is someone who has recently obtained permanent residency in Canada with limited or no Canadian credit history and established income in Canada and who is looking to purchase a principal residence. • The StartRight for Temporary Residents Program is a residential mortgage financing program for purchase or switch (no additional funds) of owner-occupied properties for temporary foreign workers and experience class students working in Canada with a valid Work Permit (IMM 1442) or exemption. Conditions apply and may be amended from time to time. Qualifying criteria for this program are outlined below. The typical client for the Temporary Resident program is someone who has established work history in Canada through contract work, post-graduate studies, or relocation programs and who is looking to purchase a principal residence. PROGRAM CRITERIA Borrower Qualifications: Permanent Resident Temporary Resident • Canadian Resident, filing taxes in Canada. • Have relocated to and established permanent residency in Canada within the last five years. • Have no derogatory credit history. Clients with a BRI of C or better or NS or NH are eligible. BRIs of D or E are not eligible. • Standard requirements for income, net worth, credit history and capacity (GDSR/TDSR) apply. • Maximum of two properties in Canada (including the current application) per StartRight client: o Properties located outside of Canada are not considered in the maximum number of owner- occupied properties. o In this context, "client" means the combined borrowers on the application or the borrower and the spouse (married or common-law) who is not on the application. Permanent Residents who can provide verifiable income and have an existing Canadian credit history may apply for uninsured or insured mortgage financing following standard guidelines. For Sagen and Canada Guaranty default-insured only: • The applicant must have a minimum of three months of full- time employment in Canada. • Foreign workers or experience class students who do not have Permanent Resident status in Canada. • Applicants must have a valid Canadian Employment Authorization Form (Work Permit 1442) or a letter from the current Canadian employer confirming the client is exempt from the requirement to obtain a work permit and the reason for this exemption. • The work permit or exemption letter must have been issued for a minimum period of one year, and the time remaining as of the application date must be six months or longer. • Have no derogatory credit history. Canadian BRIs of C or better or NS or NH are acceptable. BRIs of D or E are not acceptable. • Borrower(s) on the application, or their spouses (if applicable) do not own any other real estate in Canada, whether secondary / vacation homes or rental properties. o The applicant's principal residence in their country of origin does not have to be sold. For Sagen and Canada Guaranty default-insured only: The applicant must have entered Canada within the last five years, must provide a copy of their passport from the country of origin showing the date of entry, and have three months' minimum full- time employment in Canada. Applicants being transferred under a corporate relocation program are exempt from the employment requirement. SMA Product Factsheet Scotiabank StartRight®: Permanent and Temporary Resident Programs ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. Mortgage applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s and, if applicable, mortgage insurer’s standard credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and maximum permitted loan amounts. Scotiabank can amend, modify or change any of the options, benefits, features or requirements in this Factsheet at any time without notice. (02/25) Page 2 of 7 Eligible Loan Purpose: Permanent Resident Temporary Resident For Uninsured: • Purchase, Switch (no additional funds) For Insured (CMHC and CG): • Purchase, Switch (no additional funds) For Insured (Sagen): • Purchase Eligible Properties: Permanent Resident Temporary Resident For uninsured and insured: • Owner-occupied principal residences • Maximum of up to four units, with one unit owner-occupied and individual units must be self-contained. Maximum Mortgage Amount: Permanent Resident Temporary Resident For Uninsured: • Non-apartment style properties (detached, semi-detached, duplex, triplex, fourplex, townhouse and row-style homes): $1.8 million • Apartment style condominium: $1.5 million Requests more than HFS limits are to be referred to ACE. For Uninsured: • $2 million For all default insured: • Appraised value must be less than $1,500,000. Maximum TDSR (Using standard income requirements): Permanent Resident Temporary Resident • Standard credit criteria, including GDSR / TDSR requirements and income and employment verification apply. • All debts held outside the country must be included as liabilities for qualification purposes (for example, TDSR). • Rental income earned outside of Canada is to be excluded for qualification purposes. • Standard credit criteria, including GDSR / TDSR requirements and income and employment verification apply. • All debts held outside Canada must be included in the TDSR. • For uninsured and Sagen-insured mortgages, rental income earned outside of Canada is to be excluded from the debt service ratios. • For CMHC insured mortgages, steady rental income from the principal residence in the country of original may be included, if verified. SMA Product Factsheet Scotiabank StartRight®: Permanent and Temporary Resident Programs ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. Mortgage applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s and, if applicable, mortgage insurer’s standard credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and maximum permitted loan amounts. Scotiabank can amend, modify or change any of the options, benefits, features or requirements in this Factsheet at any time without notice. (02/25) Page 3 of 7 Maximum Loan to Value Ratio: Permanent Resident Temporary Resident For Uninsured: • Maximum LTV is 80% (LTV sliding scales apply). o STEP ScotiaLine with Access Card: 65% LTV For all Default Insured (Sagen, Canada Guaranty, and CMHC): • 1-2 Units: Up to 95% LTV (LTV sliding scales apply) • 3-4 Units: Up to 90% LTV (LTV sliding scales apply) For Uninsured: • Maximum LTV is 65% (LTV sliding scales apply). For all Default Insured (Sagen, Canada Guaranty, and CMHC): • 1-2 Units: Up to 95% LTV (LTV sliding scales apply) • 3-4 Units: Up to 90% LTV (LTV sliding scales apply) Scotia Total Equity® Plan (Scotia STEP® Program): Permanent Resident Temporary Resident • Maximum Scotia STEP® Global Limit is 80% LTV • STEP ScotiaLine with Access Card: 65% LTV • Not eligible for STEP Available Products: Payment Frequencies at Set Up: Permanent Resident Temporary Resident • Any day monthly (except the 29th, 30th, and 31st), bi-weekly, or weekly Amortization: Permanent Resident Temporary Resident For uninsured and insured: Up to 30 years for eligible applicants Scotia Mortgage Protection: Permanent Resident Temporary Resident • Life • Critical Illness • Disability • Job Loss Permanent Resident Temporary Resident • Fixed rate mortgage terms (open and closed) from six months to 10 years • Ultimate Variable Rate Mortgage (UVRM) • Scotia Flex Value Mortgage (Flex VRM) closed or open terms • STEP Scotialine Line of Credit with or without Access Card • Fixed rate mortgage terms (open and closed) from six months to 10 years • Ultimate Variable Rate Mortgage (UVRM) • Scotia Flex Value Mortgage (Flex VRM) closed terms only SMA Product Factsheet Scotiabank StartRight®: Permanent and Temporary Resident Programs ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. Mortgage applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s and, if applicable, mortgage insurer’s standard credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and maximum permitted loan amounts. Scotiabank can amend, modify or change any of the options, benefits, features or requirements in this Factsheet at any time without notice. (02/25) Page 4 of 7 Personal Net Worth Requirements: Permanent Resident Temporary Resident For uninsured: • Personal net worth requirements do not apply. For default insured: • Personal net worth requirements are based on credit score: • A, B, or C+: N/A • C or lower, NH, NS: – $25,000 Documentation Requirements: Permanent Resident Temporary Resident Income: • Standard Income Documentation for verification purposes and to establish TDSR. Income: • Standard Income Documentation for verification purposes and to establish TDSR. • In addition, for full-time and part-time employment, obtain bank statements for a minimum 3 months confirming pay deposit. Permanent Resident Temporary Resident Credit History: The below requirements only apply to default insured deals: Applicants with no Canadian credit history (no hit or no score) must provide any one of the following as a financial reference: For CG-insured purchase applications with LVRs up to and including 90%: • A letter of reference confirming satisfactory banking relationship for a minimum of six months from the country of origin. or • The last six months of bank statements from a Canadian financial institution or a financial institution from the client’s country of origin confirming satisfactory savings and repayment habits. For Sagen -insured purchase applications with LVRs up to and including 90%: ▪ A letter of reference from a recognized financial institution. or ▪ Six months bank statements from a primary bank account. For all Sagen, CG, and CMHC-insured purchase applications with LVRs up to 95.00%: • U.S. or international credit bureau report demonstrating no derogatory credit history. The borrower must provide a complete address from their country of origin for this purpose. Or • Proof of timely payments (no arrears) for the past 12 months from two sources of credit. Acceptable sources include the following: Canadian rental payment history confirmed by letter from the landlord (must indicate name of tenant, monthly rent, length of tenancy, payment history) and bank statements (must support the amount of rent and timeliness of payments outlined in the letter). And • Canadian utility payment history from an acceptable source such as hydro, gas, telephone, or cable. We require a letter from the service provider confirming a satisfactory payment history for the past 12 months or 12 months billing statements. Documentation Requirements Cont’d: SMA Product Factsheet Scotiabank StartRight®: Permanent and Temporary Resident Programs ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. Mortgage applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s and, if applicable, mortgage insurer’s standard credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and maximum permitted loan amounts. Scotiabank can amend, modify or change any of the options, benefits, features or requirements in this Factsheet at any time without notice. (02/25) Page 5 of 7 Permanent Resident Temporary Resident Verification of landed immigrant status: Must provide one of the following documents issued by Citizenship and Immigration Canada that bears the applicant’s name and confirms they entered Canada within the last five years: • Confirmation of Permanent Residence Form (IMM5292/IMM5688), or • Permanent Resident Card (PRC). Verification of temporary resident status: Must provide one of the following documents issued for a minimum period of one year, with the time remaining as of the application date of six months or longer. The document must show the applicant’s name and confirms they entered Canada within the last five years: • Canadian Employment Authorization Form (Work Permit 1442) or • a letter from the current Canadian employer confirming the client is exempt from the requirement to obtain a work permit and the reason for this exemption. Note: For experience class students, the work permit must include “Remarks: Post Graduate Work Program XXX Years Allowable.” Down Payment: Permanent Resident Temporary Resident For Uninsured: • Standard requirements for verifying down payment apply. • Down payment requirement is minimum 20% from own resources. • Gifted down payment and secondary financing are not permitted. For Uninsured: Experience Class Students and Foreign Workers: • A minimum down payment of 35% is required. • No secondary financing is permitted. • Standard requirements for down payment verification apply. Experience Class Students: • Gifted funds are acceptable: for situations where permanent resident status has been obtained, the ACE may consider gifted down payments only if the applicant was on-boarded/became a Bank client while they had student status (i.e., before becoming a permanent resident). Foreign Workers: • No gifted down payments are acceptable. • A relocation bonus or allowance that forms part of a borrower's standard compensation package may form all or part of the down payment. If the relocation allowance is repayable, such as a low- or no-interest loan, then it cannot be applied as part of the minimum down payment. For all Default Insured: CMHC, Sagen, and Canada Guaranty: • A minimum of 5% down payment (10% for 3-4 unit properties). • Standard requirements for down payment verification apply; no secondary financing is permitted. • Gifted Funds may come from a close family member or corporate relocation subsidy, if there are no repayments required and there is no lien or registration on title. Standard verification policies apply. SMA Product Factsheet Scotiabank StartRight®: Permanent and Temporary Resident Programs ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. Mortgage applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s and, if applicable, mortgage insurer’s standard credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and maximum permitted loan amounts. Scotiabank can amend, modify or change any of the options, benefits, features or requirements in this Factsheet at any time without notice. (02/25) Page 6 of 7 Appraisal Requirements: Permanent Resident Temporary Resident For uninsured: • Standard appraisal requirements apply. For Default Insured: • Appraisals will be ordered by the default insurer and must meet the default insurers' requirements. Income Requirements: Permanent Resident Temporary Resident • All income types are eligible. Standard verification requirements apply. • Rental income earned outside of Canada is to be excluded for qualification purposes. For Sagen and Canada Guaranty default insured: • The applicant must have entered Canada within the last five years and have three months' minimum full-time employment in Canada. • The work permit or exemption letter must have been issued for a minimum period of one year, and the time remaining as at the application date must be six months or longer. • All income types are eligible. Standard verification requirements apply. For uninsured and Sagen-insured mortgages: • Rental income earned outside of Canada is to be excluded from the debt service ratios. For Sagen and Canada Guaranty default insured: The applicant must have entered Canada within the last five years and have three months' minimum full-time employment in Canada. Applicants being transferred under a corporate relocation program are exempt from the employment requirement. Exclusions: Permanent Resident Temporary Resident • StartRight Temporary Resident Program • Long and short mortgage • Scotia Flex Value Mortgage (open term) • StartRight Permanent Resident Program • STEP® • Default management purposes • Pre-approvals • Loans with secondary financing • Mobile homes • Non-personal residential mortgages • Progress Advance New Construction Mortgages: Canada Guaranty and Sagen • Progress Advance New Construction Mortgages: uninsured • Cottage/leisure and secondary homes Mortgage applications under these programs: • Builder Cap Program • On Reserve Lending Program • Scotia Rental Property® Financing Program • Purchase Plus Improvements • Total Net Worth Program • Business for Self Plus SMA Product Factsheet Scotiabank StartRight®: Permanent and Temporary Resident Programs ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. Mortgage applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s and, if applicable, mortgage insurer’s standard credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and maximum permitted loan amounts. Scotiabank can amend, modify or change any of the options, benefits, features or requirements in this Factsheet at any time without notice. (02/25) Page 7 of 7

entries.text: SMA Product Factsheet Switch to Scotiabank Program ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. The Switch Program applies to a transfer of an acceptable conventional mortgage from another lender to Scotiabank and does not apply if the other lender will not transfer the mortgage in a form acceptable to us. This Switch Program applies for Scotia Mortgage Authority® broker use only. Scotiabank will cover up to a maximum of $400 towards any discharge or assignment fee (other than prepayment charges) charged by your existing lender in connection with the mortgage transfer (not applicable in Quebec). All offers and programs referenced in this Factsheet can be changed or withdrawn at any time without notice and may not be combined with other offers, programs or promotions. All applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and, if applicable, mortgage insurers’ lending criteria. Scotiabank can amend any of the features, options, benefits or requirements contained in this Factsheet without notice. The information in this Factsheet is accurate as of the date of printing and is subject to change. (04/22) Page 1 of 2 KEY FEATURES • This program allows the transfer of an existing conventional mortgage charge to Scotiabank (with no change to the borrowers on title) • Ability to cap other lender fees charged by the existing lender • Ability to retain existing amortization FAST FACTS Borrower Qualifications • Standard qualifications apply • Standard debt servicing ratios apply • No change to the borrowers on title Eligible Loan Transactions • Switch (no additional funds) Maximum Loan to Value Ratio • Up to 95% LTV Amortization • Insured: up to 25 years • Uninsured: up to 30 years Note: New mortgage will match client’s current remaining amortization period. Scotia Total Equity® Plan (STEP® Program) • Not available Payment Frequencies • Any day Monthly (except the 29th, 30th, and 31st), bi-weekly, and weekly Additional benefits Uninsured • Ability to capitalize up to $8,000 to cover existing lender fees, including prepayment changes if the LTV does not exceed 70%. • For mortgages with a LTV greater than 70%, ability to capitalize up to a maximum of 80% LTV. Insured • Ability to capitalize up to $3,000 to cover existing lender fees, including prepayment charges. SMA Product Factsheet Switch to Scotiabank Program ® Registered trademarks of The Bank of Nova Scotia. The Switch Program applies to a transfer of an acceptable conventional mortgage from another lender to Scotiabank and does not apply if the other lender will not transfer the mortgage in a form acceptable to us. This Switch Program applies for Scotia Mortgage Authority® broker use only. Scotiabank will cover up to a maximum of $400 towards any discharge or assignment fee (other than prepayment charges) charged by your existing lender in connection with the mortgage transfer (not applicable in Quebec). All offers and programs referenced in this Factsheet can be changed or withdrawn at any time without notice and may not be combined with other offers, programs or promotions. All applications are subject to meeting Scotiabank’s credit criteria, residential mortgage standards and, if applicable, mortgage insurers’ lending criteria. Scotiabank can amend any of the features, options, benefits or requirements contained in this Factsheet without notice. The information in this Factsheet is accurate as of the date of printing and is subject to change. (04/22) Page 2 of 2 • Default insurer re-approval is not required. Transfer Fee • Scotiabank will absorb the existing lenders’ discharge/transfer fee to a maximum of $400. Scotia Mortgage Protection • Life • Critical Illness • Disability • Job loss Exclusions • Preapprovals • Collateral mortgages • Lines of credit • Private mortgages • Second mortgages • Purchase Plus Improvements • Leased land properties • Mobile Homes • Scotia STEP® Program • Projected Income: Dentists and Veterinarians For collateral charges, speak to your Broker Onboarding Officer or Broker Relationship Manager about our refinance options.

entries.text: Appraisal Guidelines, Strive (Prime) Underwriting Strive Capital Corporation Appraisal Guidelines, Strive Underwriting, 070924 FSRA License No. 13322 Strive does not maintain a list of ‘approved appraisers’, as such originators may choose a known appraiser in their region or alternatively through an Appraisal Management Company (AMC). At a minimum, the appraiser must meet the criteria listed below. Strive accepts appraisals from the following AMCs; • Value Connect • Solidifi Strive reserves the right to request the selection of another appraiser should the one selected be restricted based on past dealings. Appraisers Appraisers must have a real estate designation of AACI or CRA - no DAR designation is permitted. General • Appraisal reports must be signed by an approved designated appraiser. • Appraisal reports must be addressed and issued directly to Strive. • Appraisers must complete the appraisal on a current full appraisal form approved by the Appraisal Institute of Canada. • Ensure the report is CUSPAP/USPAP compliant and meets any special instructions contained within the order. • Ensure the appraisal report is written and submitted in English. • Produce the appraisal report in a manner consistent with all applicable provincial requirements. • Complete an appraisal on each property. The appraisal report must be signed or co-signed by a designated member in good standing with the AIC. (Maximum lending on 4 units.) • Appraisal reports submitted directly by the Broker will NOT be accepted. Red Flag Consideration • Appraiser to make note in the appraisal if they were denied access to any part of the property. • Appraiser to make note if any bedroom doors had/have dead bolts. (e.g., any signs of property being used as a rooming house) FOLLOW STRIVE Appraisal Guidelines, Strive (Prime) Underwriting Approach To Value The appraisal report must: • Include a current market value. • Confirm access to all interior rooms of the property in the appraisal report. • Confirm that there are no adverse external factors that may affect the marketability of the property. • If an appraiser does not include the cost approach to value, he/she must provide an explanation supporting the facts within the appraisal report. • Rural properties should be accessible from a publicly maintained road and have adequate sewage, water, and utilities. • Working farms are not acceptable. • Derive value based on the house and maximum 15 property acres for insured/insurable, maximum 5 acres for uninsured. • Do not include outbuildings in the value except in the case of a laneway home. • Indicate the values of the outbuildings in the narrative of the appraisal report. • Provide an ‘as completed’ final value for new construction properties, stating the percentage complete in the appropriate field. • New construction must be 98% finished when indicating completed value. Attachments - Location Map • The appraisal report must contain a current street map showing the exact location of the subject property and comparable properties, as well as the location of other properties mentioned within the report as affecting the value of the subject. This includes neighboring properties exhibiting negative characteristics. • State location and proximity of comparable sales in terms of blocks or fractions of a kilometer in urban areas. • Seek comparable sales within the same neighborhood, preferably within 3 or 4 blocks or 1 kilometer for suburban areas and 5 to 10 kilometers for rural areas. • If you include comparable sales from other neighborhoods provide justification of the reasons for obtaining comparable sales outside the subject neighborhood within the narrative comments. • A description of the neighborhood characteristics, negative as well as positive, should include: o The residential setting (e.g., declining, stable or developing) o The conformity of the property to the market o Supply and demand characteristics o Neighborhood age, proximity to services and any adverse factors Photographs The appraisal report must contain current, clear and well-framed color photographs of the following: • Front of subject property • Rear of subject property • Street scene Strive Capital Corporation Appraisal Guidelines, Strive Underwriting, 070924 FSRA License No. 13322 FOLLOW STRIVE Appraisal Guidelines, Strive (Prime) Underwriting The following photographs are considered minimum requirements: • Kitchen • Main living area • Main bedroom • Main bathroom • Basement (where applicable) • Examples of physical deterioration (if present) • Examples of recent updates (e.g., restoration, remodeling and renovations, if present) • Front views of all comparable sales used within the appraisal report Comparables • Use a minimum of 3 comparable sales from the immediate area. • Comparable sales should reflect arms-length transactions and occur within 180 days of the appraisal date. • Comparable sales must have closed and be available from MLS. • Provide MLS photos and listing information in the appraisal report. • Clearly explain subjective adjustment(s) in the report. (e.g., views, appeal, condition, location ratings) • If you use comparable sales greater than 6 months from the effective date of the appraisal report, you must indicate the reasons why you chose that comparable. • Appraiser must choose at least one condo unit comparable sale from an outside but comparable condo building. If there are no comparable properties from outside the building the individual reviewing the appraisal must provide mitigation. • Provide the listing price and the length of time to sell for each of the comparable sales. The final adjusted sale price of the comparable sales, after dollar adjustments, should not exceed 20% of the original sale price of the comparable. Include supporting details if the adjustments exceed 20%. If they do, the individual reviewing the appraisal must provide mitigation. Appraisal Result Requirements Item Net Adjustments for Comparables Price Marketability Economic Life Condos Requirement <20% Typical for the area as supported by 3 or more comparables. Sales dates need to be within last 6 months. Property demonstrates strong marketability. Prime marketable residential properties where economic life exceeds amortization by 5 years. The property must conform and the appraisal must indicate market acceptance of the subject condo complex or building. Strive Capital Corporation Appraisal Guidelines, Strive Underwriting, 070924 FSRA License No. 13322 FOLLOW STRIVE Appraisal Guidelines, Strive (Prime) Underwriting Condos cont. Acreage Electrical/Heating Requirements Water Source and Sewage Disposal Business Use in Subject Property New Home Warranty New Construction Appraisals should include at least one comparable within and one comparable outside the subject development. The value will be based on the home and a maximum of 15 acres of land only for insured/ insurable and 5 acres for uninsured, UNLESS otherwise directed by Strive; other buildings may only be included if there is not attached garage/structure. The outbuilding will not be included in the valuation if it is income generating. Properties should have a permanent conventional heating system. Properties should have a minimum electrical amperage of 100, exceptions required if below. All properties must have a permanent year around potable water supply and sewage disposal system (municipal services or private well and septic systems are acceptable). Shared wells, cisterns and holding tanks will be reviewed case by case where it is typical for the area, and full disclosure to insurer. Partial business use in the subject property is considered on a case by case basis subject to: • Property serves as residence • At least 80% of total floor space is used for personal residency. • Property must be appraised as residence only. • The business must not require structural changes that would affect the value or marketability of the real estate. For new home purchases, a Certificate of Completion (where applicable) and/or possession letter and New Home Warranty (NHW) are required. The final appraisal (or inspection) must indicate the property is at least 97% complete (minor items such as landscaping, trim, driveway may not be complete). In the case of self-build homes or other homes which may not be eligible for NHW, an occupancy permit (to confirm compliance with building code/zoning) together with a Strive Capital Corporation Appraisal Guidelines, Strive Underwriting, 070924 FSRA License No. 13322 FOLLOW STRIVE Appraisal Guidelines, Strive (Prime) Underwriting New Home Warranty New Construction cont. with building code/zoning) together with a satisfactory home inspection are required. Appraisals on newly constructed homes should contain a minimum of one comparable within and one outside the development. Strive Capital Corporation Appraisal Guidelines, Strive Underwriting, 070924 FSRA License No. 13322 FOLLOW STRIVE
